title: Lesson 1 — Greetings
topic: Greetings and polite first moves

phrases.en: Hello.
phrases.pt: Olá.
phrases.note: Use this at any time of day.

phrases.en: Good morning.
phrases.pt: Bom dia.
phrases.note: Use it before noon.

phrases.en: Good afternoon.
phrases.pt: Boa tarde.
phrases.note: Use it after noon.

phrases.en: Good evening.
phrases.pt: Boa noite.
phrases.note: Use it when you meet someone at night.

phrases.en: How are you?
phrases.pt: Como você está?
phrases.note: A common friendly question.

phrases.en: I'm fine, thanks.
phrases.pt: Estou bem, obrigado.
phrases.note: A short answer to How are you?

phrases.en: Nice to meet you.
phrases.pt: Prazer em conhecer você.
phrases.note: Say this the first time you meet a person.

phrases.en: See you soon.
phrases.pt: Até logo.
phrases.note: Use it when you expect to meet again.

title: Lesson 2 — Names
topic: Names and simple introductions

phrases.en: My name is Ana.
phrases.pt: Meu nome é Ana.
phrases.note: Replace Ana with your name.

phrases.en: I'm Pedro.
phrases.pt: Eu sou Pedro.
phrases.note: A natural short introduction.

phrases.en: What's your name?
phrases.pt: Qual é o seu nome?
phrases.note: Use this to ask someone their name.

phrases.en: Where are you from?
phrases.pt: De onde você é?
phrases.note: A basic question about origin.

phrases.en: I'm from Brazil.
phrases.pt: Eu sou do Brasil.
phrases.note: Use from for country or city.

phrases.en: I live in Cuiaba.
phrases.pt: Eu moro em Cuiabá.
phrases.note: Use live in with a city.

phrases.en: I'm a student.
phrases.pt: Eu sou estudante.
phrases.note: Use a before many jobs.

phrases.en: This is my friend.
phrases.pt: Este é meu amigo.
phrases.note: Use this is to introduce a person.

title: Lesson 3 — Countries and Cities
topic: Places, origin, and location

phrases.en: I'm from a small city.
phrases.pt: Eu sou de uma cidade pequena.
phrases.note: Use from to talk about origin.

phrases.en: Brazil is a big country.
phrases.pt: O Brasil é um país grande.
phrases.note: Big describes country.

phrases.en: My city is hot.
phrases.pt: Minha cidade é quente.
phrases.note: A simple description with is.

phrases.en: I live near the center.
phrases.pt: Eu moro perto do centro.
phrases.note: Near means close to.

phrases.en: The school is here.
phrases.pt: A escola fica aqui.
phrases.note: Here points to your current place.

phrases.en: The hotel is there.
phrases.pt: O hotel fica ali.
phrases.note: There points away from you.

phrases.en: Is it far?
phrases.pt: É longe?
phrases.note: Put is before it to ask.

phrases.en: It is not far.
phrases.pt: Não é longe.
phrases.note: Use not after is.

title: Lesson 4 — Be
topic: I am, you are, it is

phrases.en: I am ready.
phrases.pt: Estou pronto.
phrases.note: Use am with I.

phrases.en: You are right.
phrases.pt: Você está certo.
phrases.note: Use are with you.

phrases.en: He is my brother.
phrases.pt: Ele é meu irmão.
phrases.note: Use is with he.

phrases.en: She is my teacher.
phrases.pt: Ela é minha professora.
phrases.note: Use is with she.

phrases.en: It is easy.
phrases.pt: É fácil.
phrases.note: Use it is for things and situations.

phrases.en: We are here.
phrases.pt: Estamos aqui.
phrases.note: Use are with we.

phrases.en: They are busy.
phrases.pt: Eles estão ocupados.
phrases.note: Use are with they.

phrases.en: Are you okay?
phrases.pt: Você está bem?
phrases.note: Put are before you to ask.

title: Lesson 5 — Jobs
topic: Work, study, and simple roles

phrases.en: I work in a store.
phrases.pt: Eu trabalho em uma loja.
phrases.note: Use work in for a place or field.

phrases.en: I study English.
phrases.pt: Eu estudo inglês.
phrases.note: Use study before the subject.

phrases.en: She is a doctor.
phrases.pt: Ela é médica.
phrases.note: Use a before singular jobs.

phrases.en: He is a driver.
phrases.pt: Ele é motorista.
phrases.note: Use a before singular jobs.

phrases.en: My job is simple.
phrases.pt: Meu trabalho é simples.
phrases.note: A simple job description.

phrases.en: I need help.
phrases.pt: Eu preciso de ajuda.
phrases.note: A useful phrase at work or school.

phrases.en: Can you help me?
phrases.pt: Você pode me ajudar?
phrases.note: A polite request.

phrases.en: Thank you for your help.
phrases.pt: Obrigado pela sua ajuda.
phrases.note: Use for before the reason.

title: Lesson 6 — Family
topic: Family and people close to you

phrases.en: This is my mother.
phrases.pt: Esta é minha mãe.
phrases.note: Use this is my to introduce family.

phrases.en: This is my father.
phrases.pt: Este é meu pai.
phrases.note: Use the same pattern with family words.

phrases.en: I have one sister.
phrases.pt: Eu tenho uma irmã.
phrases.note: Use have for people in your family.

phrases.en: I have two brothers.
phrases.pt: Eu tenho dois irmãos.
phrases.note: Add s for brothers.

phrases.en: My family is small.
phrases.pt: Minha família é pequena.
phrases.note: In English, family can be singular.

phrases.en: My parents live here.
phrases.pt: Meus pais moram aqui.
phrases.note: Parents means mother and father.

phrases.en: Do you have children?
phrases.pt: Você tem filhos?
phrases.note: Use do you have to ask about possession.

phrases.en: I don't have children.
phrases.pt: Eu não tenho filhos.
phrases.note: Use don't have for the negative.

title: Lesson 7 — Numbers and Age
topic: Age, phone numbers, and simple quantities

phrases.en: I am twenty years old.
phrases.pt: Eu tenho vinte anos.
phrases.note: English says I am, not I have, for age.

phrases.en: How old are you?
phrases.pt: Quantos anos você tem?
phrases.note: The words are how old.

phrases.en: My phone number is 555-0120.
phrases.pt: Meu telefone é 555-0120.
phrases.note: Say each digit clearly.

phrases.en: I need two tickets.
phrases.pt: Eu preciso de dois ingressos.
phrases.note: Plural nouns usually add s.

phrases.en: It costs ten dollars.
phrases.pt: Custa dez dólares.
phrases.note: Use it costs for price.

phrases.en: I have three questions.
phrases.pt: Eu tenho três perguntas.
phrases.note: Questions is plural.

phrases.en: The class starts at nine.
phrases.pt: A aula começa às nove.
phrases.note: Use at for clock time.

phrases.en: I finish at five.
phrases.pt: Eu termino às cinco.
phrases.note: A simple time sentence.

title: Lesson 8 — Daily Routine
topic: Simple present for everyday actions

phrases.en: I wake up early.
phrases.pt: Eu acordo cedo.
phrases.note: A common daily routine verb.

phrases.en: I drink coffee.
phrases.pt: Eu tomo café.
phrases.note: Use the base verb with I.

phrases.en: I go to work.
phrases.pt: Eu vou para o trabalho.
phrases.note: Use to before a destination.

phrases.en: I eat lunch at noon.
phrases.pt: Eu almoço ao meio-dia.
phrases.note: At noon means at 12:00.

phrases.en: I study at night.
phrases.pt: Eu estudo à noite.
phrases.note: Use at night for the evening period.

phrases.en: I like music.
phrases.pt: Eu gosto de música.
phrases.note: Use like before things you enjoy.

phrases.en: I have dinner with my family.
phrases.pt: Eu janto com minha família.
phrases.note: With shows who is together with you.

phrases.en: I sleep at eleven.
phrases.pt: Eu durmo às onze.
phrases.note: Use at for bedtime.

title: Lesson 9 — Food
topic: Ordering food and saying what you want

phrases.en: I'd like a coffee, please.
phrases.pt: Eu gostaria de um café, por favor.
phrases.note: A polite way to order.

phrases.en: Can I have the menu?
phrases.pt: Posso ver o cardápio?
phrases.note: Use it to ask for something.

phrases.en: I don't eat meat.
phrases.pt: Eu não como carne.
phrases.note: Use don't before the verb.

phrases.en: Is this spicy?
phrases.pt: Isso é apimentado?
phrases.note: Use is this to ask about one item.

phrases.en: The food is very good.
phrases.pt: A comida está muito boa.
phrases.note: Very makes the adjective stronger.

phrases.en: Could we pay, please?
phrases.pt: Podemos pagar, por favor?
phrases.note: Could is polite in requests.

phrases.en: The bill is wrong.
phrases.pt: A conta está errada.
phrases.note: A useful sentence when something is incorrect.

phrases.en: Everything was delicious.
phrases.pt: Tudo estava delicioso.
phrases.note: Was is the past of is.

title: Lesson 10 — Directions
topic: Finding places in town

phrases.en: Excuse me, where is the bank?
phrases.pt: Com licença, onde fica o banco?
phrases.note: Start with excuse me to be polite.

phrases.en: Go straight ahead.
phrases.pt: Siga em frente.
phrases.note: A common instruction in the street.

phrases.en: Turn left at the corner.
phrases.pt: Vire à esquerda na esquina.
phrases.note: At the corner marks the place.

phrases.en: Turn right after the supermarket.
phrases.pt: Vire à direita depois do supermercado.
phrases.note: After means later in the route.

phrases.en: It's next to the pharmacy.
phrases.pt: Fica ao lado da farmácia.
phrases.note: Next to means beside.

phrases.en: It's across from the park.
phrases.pt: Fica em frente ao parque.
phrases.note: Across from means on the opposite side.

phrases.en: How long does it take?
phrases.pt: Quanto tempo leva?
phrases.note: Use this to ask about travel time.

phrases.en: It takes about ten minutes.
phrases.pt: Leva cerca de dez minutos.
phrases.note: Use about for an approximate time.

title: Lesson 11 — Shopping
topic: Shopping and prices

phrases.en: I'm just looking, thanks.
phrases.pt: Só estou olhando, obrigado.
phrases.note: A polite reply to a salesperson.

phrases.en: How much is this?
phrases.pt: Quanto custa isto?
phrases.note: Use how much to ask a price.

phrases.en: Do you have this in blue?
phrases.pt: Você tem isto em azul?
phrases.note: Ask about another color or option.

phrases.en: Can I try it on?
phrases.pt: Posso experimentar?
phrases.note: Try on is for clothes.

phrases.en: It's a bit too expensive.
phrases.pt: Está um pouco caro demais.
phrases.note: Too means more than you want.

phrases.en: Do you take cards?
phrases.pt: Vocês aceitam cartão?
phrases.note: Ask about the payment method.

phrases.en: Can I get a receipt?
phrases.pt: Posso pegar um recibo?
phrases.note: Ask for proof of purchase.

phrases.en: I'll take it.
phrases.pt: Vou levar.
phrases.note: Say yes, you will buy it.

title: Lesson 12 — Last Weekend
topic: Simple past for everyday stories

phrases.en: I went to the beach last weekend.
phrases.pt: Eu fui à praia no fim de semana passado.
phrases.note: Went is the past of go.

phrases.en: We had a great time.
phrases.pt: Nós nos divertimos muito.
phrases.note: Had is the past of have.

phrases.en: I saw an old friend.
phrases.pt: Eu vi um amigo antigo.
phrases.note: Saw is the past of see.

phrases.en: We ate at a nice restaurant.
phrases.pt: Nós comemos em um restaurante legal.
phrases.note: Ate is the past of eat.

phrases.en: It rained on Sunday.
phrases.pt: Choveu no domingo.
phrases.note: Regular past adds -ed.

phrases.en: I didn't do much on Saturday.
phrases.pt: Eu não fiz muita coisa no sábado.
phrases.note: Use didn't + the base verb.

phrases.en: Did you go out last night?
phrases.pt: Você saiu ontem à noite?
phrases.note: Make past questions with did.

phrases.en: I stayed home and relaxed.
phrases.pt: Eu fiquei em casa e relaxei.
phrases.note: Regular past for everyday actions.

title: Lesson 13 — Making Plans
topic: Inviting, suggesting, and confirming

phrases.en: Would you like to grab a coffee?
phrases.pt: Você gostaria de tomar um café?
phrases.note: A polite invitation.

phrases.en: How about Saturday afternoon?
phrases.pt: Que tal sábado à tarde?
phrases.note: Suggest a time or idea.

phrases.en: Let's meet at seven.
phrases.pt: Vamos nos encontrar às sete.
phrases.note: Let's makes a suggestion for us.

phrases.en: I'd love to, but I'm busy.
phrases.pt: Eu adoraria, mas estou ocupado.
phrases.note: Decline politely with a reason.

phrases.en: Sounds good to me.
phrases.pt: Por mim, parece ótimo.
phrases.note: A casual yes.

phrases.en: Can we make it a bit later?
phrases.pt: Podemos deixar um pouco mais tarde?
phrases.note: Ask to change the time.

phrases.en: Do you want to join us?
phrases.pt: Você quer vir com a gente?
phrases.note: Invite someone to come along.

phrases.en: See you on Friday, then.
phrases.pt: Então, até sexta.
phrases.note: Close the plan with a day.

title: Lesson 14 — Getting around
topic: Travel and public transport

phrases.en: Which platform does the train leave from?
phrases.pt: De qual plataforma o trem sai?
phrases.note: Ask where to wait for a train.

phrases.en: How do I get to the airport?
phrases.pt: Como eu chego ao aeroporto?
phrases.note: Ask for directions to a place.

phrases.en: Is this the right bus for downtown?
phrases.pt: Este é o ônibus certo para o centro?
phrases.note: Check before you get on.

phrases.en: A one-way ticket, please.
phrases.pt: Uma passagem só de ida, por favor.
phrases.note: One-way means no return.

phrases.en: What time does the next train leave?
phrases.pt: Que horas sai o próximo trem?
phrases.note: Ask about a schedule.

phrases.en: Could you call me a taxi?
phrases.pt: Você poderia chamar um táxi para mim?
phrases.note: A polite request for help.

phrases.en: I think I missed my stop.
phrases.pt: Acho que perdi a minha parada.
phrases.note: Use it when you went too far.

phrases.en: How long does the trip take?
phrases.pt: Quanto tempo dura a viagem?
phrases.note: Ask about duration.

title: Lesson 15 — At the doctor
topic: Health and feeling unwell

phrases.en: I don't feel well.
phrases.pt: Não estou me sentindo bem.
phrases.note: Say that you are sick.

phrases.en: I have a headache.
phrases.pt: Estou com dor de cabeça.
phrases.note: Use have + ache for pain.

phrases.en: It hurts here.
phrases.pt: Dói aqui.
phrases.note: Point and say where.

phrases.en: I'd like to see a doctor.
phrases.pt: Eu gostaria de ver um médico.
phrases.note: Ask for an appointment.

phrases.en: How often should I take this?
phrases.pt: Com que frequência devo tomar isto?
phrases.note: Ask about medicine timing.

phrases.en: I'm allergic to penicillin.
phrases.pt: Sou alérgico a penicilina.
phrases.note: An important safety phrase.

phrases.en: I've had a fever since yesterday.
phrases.pt: Estou com febre desde ontem.
phrases.note: Since marks when it started.

phrases.en: Do I need a prescription?
phrases.pt: Preciso de uma receita?
phrases.note: Ask if you need a note for medicine.

title: Lesson 16 — Opinions and Agreement
topic: Giving opinions and reacting to them

phrases.en: In my opinion, remote work is more productive.
phrases.pt: Na minha opinião, o trabalho remoto é mais produtivo.
phrases.note: A clear way to introduce your view.

phrases.en: I see your point, but I disagree.
phrases.pt: Entendo seu ponto, mas discordo.
phrases.note: Acknowledge first, then disagree.

phrases.en: That's a really good point.
phrases.pt: Esse é um ótimo argumento.
phrases.note: Use it to support what someone said.

phrases.en: I'm not sure I agree with that.
phrases.pt: Não tenho certeza se concordo com isso.
phrases.note: A gentle way to push back.

phrases.en: It depends on the situation.
phrases.pt: Depende da situação.
phrases.note: Useful when there is no single answer.

phrases.en: As far as I'm concerned, it's a waste of time.
phrases.pt: No que me diz respeito, é perda de tempo.
phrases.note: Marks a strong personal opinion.

phrases.en: You have a point there.
phrases.pt: Você tem razão nisso.
phrases.note: Admit the other person is partly right.

phrases.en: Let's agree to disagree.
phrases.pt: Vamos concordar em discordar.
phrases.note: End a debate without a winner.

title: Lesson 17 — Past Experiences
topic: Present perfect for life experiences

phrases.en: I've never been abroad.
phrases.pt: Eu nunca fui para o exterior.
phrases.note: Use have/has + past participle for experience.

phrases.en: Have you ever tried sushi?
phrases.pt: Você já provou sushi?
phrases.note: Ever asks about any time in your life.

phrases.en: I've already finished the report.
phrases.pt: Eu já terminei o relatório.
phrases.note: Already means sooner than expected.

phrases.en: She hasn't called me back yet.
phrases.pt: Ela ainda não me retornou.
phrases.note: Yet goes at the end in negatives.

phrases.en: We've known each other for years.
phrases.pt: Nós nos conhecemos há anos.
phrases.note: For marks a length of time.

phrases.en: I've lived here since 2019.
phrases.pt: Eu moro aqui desde 2019.
phrases.note: Since marks when something began.

phrases.en: He's just left the office.
phrases.pt: Ele acabou de sair do escritório.
phrases.note: Just means a very short time ago.

phrases.en: I went to Rio last summer.
phrases.pt: Eu fui ao Rio no verão passado.
phrases.note: Use past simple for a finished time.

title: Lesson 18 — Future Plans
topic: Talking about plans and predictions

phrases.en: I'm going to look for a new job.
phrases.pt: Eu vou procurar um novo emprego.
phrases.note: Use going to for plans you decided before.

phrases.en: I'll probably stay home tonight.
phrases.pt: Eu provavelmente vou ficar em casa hoje à noite.
phrases.note: Will fits decisions and predictions.

phrases.en: I'm meeting a client tomorrow.
phrases.pt: Vou me encontrar com um cliente amanhã.
phrases.note: Use it for fixed plans with a time.

phrases.en: We might travel in December.
phrases.pt: Talvez a gente viaje em dezembro.
phrases.note: Might shows it is not certain.

phrases.en: As soon as I finish, I'll call you.
phrases.pt: Assim que eu terminar, eu te ligo.
phrases.note: After as soon as, use the present tense.

phrases.en: I'm planning to study abroad.
phrases.pt: Estou planejando estudar fora.
phrases.note: Use to + verb after planning.

phrases.en: What are you doing this weekend?
phrases.pt: O que você vai fazer neste fim de semana?
phrases.note: A natural way to ask about plans.

phrases.en: I doubt it will make a difference.
phrases.pt: Duvido que vá fazer diferença.
phrases.note: Doubt softens a negative prediction.

title: Lesson 19 — At Work
topic: Everyday phrases for the workplace

phrases.en: Could you give me a hand with this?
phrases.pt: Você poderia me dar uma ajuda com isso?
phrases.note: A friendly way to ask for help.

phrases.en: I'm running a bit behind schedule.
phrases.pt: Estou um pouco atrasado com o cronograma.
phrases.note: Means later than planned.

phrases.en: Let's set up a meeting for Friday.
phrases.pt: Vamos marcar uma reunião para sexta.
phrases.note: Set up means to arrange or organize.

phrases.en: Can we go over the details?
phrases.pt: Podemos revisar os detalhes?
phrases.note: Go over means to review carefully.

phrases.en: I'll get back to you by the end of the day.
phrases.pt: Eu te retorno até o fim do dia.
phrases.note: Means to reply later.

phrases.en: We need to deal with this issue.
phrases.pt: Precisamos resolver esse problema.
phrases.note: Deal with means to handle a problem.

phrases.en: That's not really my responsibility.
phrases.pt: Isso não é bem minha responsabilidade.
phrases.note: A polite way to set a limit.

phrases.en: Let's keep in touch.
phrases.pt: Vamos manter contato.
phrases.note: Use it to stay connected with someone.

title: Lesson 20 — Sorting out problems
topic: Complaints and finding a solution

phrases.en: There seems to be a problem with my order.
phrases.pt: Parece haver um problema com o meu pedido.
phrases.note: A soft way to raise an issue.

phrases.en: I'd like to make a complaint.
phrases.pt: Eu gostaria de fazer uma reclamação.
phrases.note: A formal way to complain.

phrases.en: This isn't what I ordered.
phrases.pt: Isto não é o que eu pedi.
phrases.note: Point out the mistake clearly.

phrases.en: Could you sort this out for me?
phrases.pt: Você poderia resolver isto para mim?
phrases.note: Ask someone to fix a problem.

phrases.en: Is there anything you can do?
phrases.pt: Há algo que você possa fazer?
phrases.note: Ask for a solution politely.

phrases.en: I'd appreciate a refund.
phrases.pt: Eu agradeceria um reembolso.
phrases.note: A polite way to ask for money back.

phrases.en: Let me check what went wrong.
phrases.pt: Deixe-me verificar o que deu errado.
phrases.note: Offer to look into a problem.

phrases.en: Sorry for the inconvenience.
phrases.pt: Desculpe pelo transtorno.
phrases.note: Apologize for trouble caused.

title: Lesson 21 — On the phone
topic: Handling everyday phone calls

phrases.en: Could I speak to the manager, please?
phrases.pt: Eu poderia falar com o gerente, por favor?
phrases.note: Ask for a specific person.

phrases.en: Who's calling, please?
phrases.pt: Quem está falando, por favor?
phrases.note: Ask who is on the line.

phrases.en: Could you hold on a moment?
phrases.pt: Você poderia aguardar um momento?
phrases.note: Ask someone to wait.

phrases.en: I'm calling about my appointment.
phrases.pt: Estou ligando sobre a minha consulta.
phrases.note: State the reason for your call.

phrases.en: Could you put me through to sales?
phrases.pt: Você poderia me transferir para vendas?
phrases.note: Ask to be connected to a department.

phrases.en: Can I leave a message?
phrases.pt: Posso deixar um recado?
phrases.note: Use it when the person is away.

phrases.en: Sorry, you've got the wrong number.
phrases.pt: Desculpe, você ligou para o número errado.
phrases.note: A polite correction.

phrases.en: Could you call me back later?
phrases.pt: Você poderia me ligar de volta mais tarde?
phrases.note: Ask for a return call.

title: Example — Everyday Phrases
topic: Bundled B1 sample from the demo

phrases.en: She called off the wedding.
phrases.pt: Ela cancelou o casamento.
phrases.note: Phrasal verb: "call off" significa cancelar algo planejado.

phrases.en: Sales have picked up this year.
phrases.pt: As vendas melhoraram este ano.
phrases.note: Phrasal verb: "pick up" aqui é melhorar, aumentar.

phrases.en: His career has really taken off.
phrases.pt: A carreira dele realmente decolou.
phrases.note: Phrasal verb: "take off" = deslanchar, crescer rápido.

phrases.en: I give in; it's too difficult.
phrases.pt: Eu desisto; é difícil demais.
phrases.note: Phrasal verb: "give in" = desistir, ceder.

phrases.en: If I can't read it, I'll look up the words.
phrases.pt: Se eu não conseguir ler, vou procurar as palavras.
phrases.note: Phrasal verb: "look up" = procurar (num dicionário, etc.).

phrases.en: They didn't bring the subject up.
phrases.pt: Eles não mencionaram o assunto.
phrases.note: Phrasal verb: "bring up" = trazer à tona, mencionar.

phrases.en: Our supplies finally ran out.
phrases.pt: Nossos suprimentos finalmente acabaram.
phrases.note: Phrasal verb: "run out" = esgotar, acabar.

phrases.en: She knew the poem by heart.
phrases.pt: Ela sabia o poema de cor.
phrases.note: Expressão: "by heart" = de cor, de memória.

phrases.en: I know the city like the back of my hand.
phrases.pt: Eu conheço a cidade como a palma da minha mão.
phrases.concept: like the back of my hand — como a palma da mão
phrases.note: Expressão: conhecer muito bem.

phrases.en: So far, so good.
phrases.pt: Até agora, tudo bem.
phrases.concept: so far, so good — até agora, tudo bem
phrases.note: Expressão usada para dizer que, até o momento, está tudo certo.

phrases.en: He was hitting on me.
phrases.pt: Ele estava dando em cima de mim.
phrases.note: Phrasal verb informal: "hit on" = dar em cima de alguém.

phrases.en: I'll get in touch with you tomorrow.
phrases.pt: Eu entro em contato com você amanhã.
phrases.concept: get in touch — entrar em contato
phrases.note: Expressão: "get in touch" = entrar em contato.

title: Lesson 22 — Making Arguments
topic: Debating and defending a position

phrases.en: While I understand your concern, the data suggests otherwise.
phrases.pt: Embora eu entenda sua preocupação, os dados sugerem o contrário.
phrases.note: While here means although.

phrases.en: There's no denying that costs have risen.
phrases.pt: Não dá para negar que os custos aumentaram.
phrases.note: Admits an obvious fact before arguing.

phrases.en: To be fair, both sides have a point.
phrases.pt: Para ser justo, os dois lados têm razão.
phrases.note: Softens a judgment to sound balanced.

phrases.en: That argument doesn't really hold up.
phrases.pt: Esse argumento não se sustenta de verdade.
phrases.note: Hold up means to stay valid under scrutiny.

phrases.en: Let me play devil's advocate for a moment.
phrases.pt: Deixa eu fazer o advogado do diabo por um momento.
phrases.note: Argue the opposite side on purpose.

phrases.en: I'd argue that the opposite is true.
phrases.pt: Eu diria que o oposto é verdade.
phrases.note: A confident way to state a claim.

phrases.en: That's beside the point.
phrases.pt: Isso foge do assunto.
phrases.note: Means not relevant to the issue.

phrases.en: Ultimately, it comes down to priorities.
phrases.pt: No fim das contas, tudo se resume a prioridades.
phrases.note: Come down to means the key factor is.

title: Lesson 23 — Hypotheticals
topic: Conditionals and unreal situations

phrases.en: If I were you, I'd take the offer.
phrases.pt: Se eu fosse você, aceitaria a oferta.
phrases.note: Use were for all subjects in this pattern.

phrases.en: Had I known, I would have helped.
phrases.pt: Se eu soubesse, teria ajudado.
phrases.note: Had + subject replaces if in formal speech.

phrases.en: Unless we act now, we'll lose the deal.
phrases.pt: A não ser que a gente aja agora, vamos perder o negócio.
phrases.note: Unless means if not.

phrases.en: Suppose the budget gets cut.
phrases.pt: Suponha que o orçamento seja cortado.
phrases.note: Suppose introduces a hypothetical case.

phrases.en: I wish I had more time.
phrases.pt: Eu queria ter mais tempo.
phrases.note: Wish + past tense shows present regret.

phrases.en: If only I had saved more money.
phrases.pt: Se eu ao menos tivesse economizado mais dinheiro.
phrases.note: If only adds emotion to a regret.

phrases.en: Even if it rains, we'll still go.
phrases.pt: Mesmo que chova, a gente vai assim mesmo.
phrases.note: Even if stresses that the result won't change.

phrases.en: Otherwise, we would have missed the flight.
phrases.pt: Caso contrário, teríamos perdido o voo.
phrases.note: Otherwise means if that had not happened.

title: Lesson 24 — Negotiating
topic: Reaching an agreement

phrases.en: Let's try to find some middle ground.
phrases.pt: Vamos tentar encontrar um meio-termo.
phrases.note: A compromise both sides accept.

phrases.en: Would you be willing to compromise?
phrases.pt: Você estaria disposto a ceder um pouco?
phrases.note: Asks if someone is open to giving in.

phrases.en: That works for me.
phrases.pt: Por mim, tudo bem.
phrases.note: A casual way to accept a proposal.

phrases.en: I'm afraid that's out of the question.
phrases.pt: Receio que isso esteja fora de cogitação.
phrases.note: A firm but polite refusal.

phrases.en: Can we meet halfway on the price?
phrases.pt: Podemos chegar a um meio-termo no preço?
phrases.note: Both sides give up a little.

phrases.en: Let's not get ahead of ourselves.
phrases.pt: Não vamos nos adiantar.
phrases.note: Means don't assume too much too soon.

phrases.en: I'll see what I can do.
phrases.pt: Vou ver o que posso fazer.
phrases.note: A soft promise without committing.

phrases.en: So, do we have a deal?
phrases.pt: Então, temos um acordo?
phrases.note: Used to close a negotiation.

title: Lesson 25 — Trends and Data
topic: Describing changes and numbers

phrases.en: Sales have soared over the past quarter.
phrases.pt: As vendas dispararam no último trimestre.
phrases.note: Soar means to rise sharply.

phrases.en: There's been a sharp decline in demand.
phrases.pt: Houve uma queda acentuada na demanda.
phrases.note: A strong, sudden drop.

phrases.en: Profits remained steady throughout the year.
phrases.pt: Os lucros se mantiveram estáveis ao longo do ano.
phrases.note: Steady means stable, with little change.

phrases.en: The numbers have plateaued recently.
phrases.pt: Os números estagnaram recentemente.
phrases.note: Plateau means to stop rising and stay flat.

phrases.en: Costs are expected to rise gradually.
phrases.pt: Espera-se que os custos subam gradualmente.
phrases.note: States a forecast in a neutral tone.

phrases.en: This accounts for roughly half of revenue.
phrases.pt: Isso representa cerca de metade da receita.
phrases.note: Account for means to make up a share.

phrases.en: The trend is likely to continue.
phrases.pt: É provável que a tendência continue.
phrases.note: Likely to shows strong probability.

phrases.en: Overall, the outlook is promising.
phrases.pt: No geral, a perspectiva é promissora.
phrases.note: Outlook means the expected future.

title: Lesson 26 — Nuance and Stance
topic: Expressing precise attitudes and reservations

phrases.en: I would hesitate to call it a failure.
phrases.pt: Eu hesitaria em chamar isso de fracasso.
phrases.note: Use hesitate to soften a strong label.

phrases.en: That said, the proposal still has considerable merit.
phrases.pt: Dito isso, a proposta ainda tem mérito considerável.
phrases.note: Signals a contrast while keeping balance.

phrases.en: There is a subtle but important distinction here.
phrases.pt: Há uma distinção sutil, mas importante aqui.
phrases.note: Useful for drawing fine conceptual lines.

phrases.en: I am broadly sympathetic to that view.
phrases.pt: Eu tendo a simpatizar com essa visão em linhas gerais.
phrases.note: Shows partial agreement without full commitment.

phrases.en: The evidence points in a different direction.
phrases.pt: As evidências apontam em outra direção.
phrases.note: A measured way to challenge a claim.

phrases.en: It is not merely a matter of preference.
phrases.pt: Não é meramente uma questão de preferência.
phrases.note: Use merely to narrow or dismiss a simple explanation.

phrases.en: I take your point, but I remain unconvinced.
phrases.pt: Entendo seu ponto, mas continuo não convencido.
phrases.note: A polished way to disagree after acknowledging.

phrases.en: The issue is more complex than it first appears.
phrases.pt: A questão é mais complexa do que parece à primeira vista.
phrases.note: Introduces nuance before deeper explanation.

title: Lesson 27 — Register and Professional Tone
topic: Adjusting tone in professional contexts

phrases.en: I wanted to follow up on our previous conversation.
phrases.pt: Eu queria dar continuidade à nossa conversa anterior.
phrases.note: A professional way to reopen a topic.

phrases.en: Could you clarify what you mean by that?
phrases.pt: Você poderia esclarecer o que quer dizer com isso?
phrases.note: Direct but polite request for precision.

phrases.en: I appreciate the constraints you are working under.
phrases.pt: Eu entendo as restrições sob as quais você está trabalhando.
phrases.note: Shows empathy while discussing limits.

phrases.en: Let me rephrase that more carefully.
phrases.pt: Deixe-me reformular isso com mais cuidado.
phrases.note: Useful when correcting your own wording.

phrases.en: From a practical standpoint, the timeline is tight.
phrases.pt: Do ponto de vista prático, o prazo está apertado.
phrases.note: Frames an opinion from a specific angle.

phrases.en: I would not want to overstate the impact.
phrases.pt: Eu não gostaria de exagerar o impacto.
phrases.note: Prevents a claim from sounding too strong.

phrases.en: This may require a more coordinated approach.
phrases.pt: Isso talvez exija uma abordagem mais coordenada.
phrases.note: Sounds more strategic than simply saying plan.

phrases.en: Please let me know if I have misunderstood anything.
phrases.pt: Por favor, me avise se eu tiver entendido algo errado.
phrases.note: Keeps accountability while inviting correction.

title: Lesson 28 — Precision and Emphasis
topic: Making exact claims with controlled emphasis

phrases.en: The distinction is not incidental; it is central.
phrases.pt: A distinção não é incidental; ela é central.
phrases.note: Uses contrast to emphasize importance.

phrases.en: That interpretation strikes me as overly reductive.
phrases.pt: Essa interpretação me parece excessivamente reducionista.
phrases.note: A sophisticated way to present a judgment.

phrases.en: We should avoid conflating correlation with causation.
phrases.pt: Devemos evitar confundir correlação com causalidade.
phrases.note: Conflate means to wrongly treat two things as one.

phrases.en: The claim holds only under very specific conditions.
phrases.pt: A afirmação se sustenta apenas sob condições muito específicas.
phrases.note: Restricts a claim with precision.

phrases.en: This is less a contradiction than a shift in emphasis.
phrases.pt: Isso é menos uma contradição do que uma mudança de ênfase.
phrases.note: Reframes how the listener should interpret something.

phrases.en: The wording leaves room for considerable ambiguity.
phrases.pt: A formulação deixa espaço para uma ambiguidade considerável.
phrases.note: Useful for critiquing imprecise language.

phrases.en: I would frame the problem somewhat differently.
phrases.pt: Eu enquadraria o problema de uma forma um pouco diferente.
phrases.note: Frame means define how an issue is understood.

phrases.en: The conclusion follows, but only with several caveats.
phrases.pt: A conclusão procede, mas apenas com várias ressalvas.
phrases.note: Adds limitations to a conclusion.

title: Lesson 29 — Rhetorical Control
topic: Controlling argument flow and emphasis

phrases.en: Let us not lose sight of the broader question.
phrases.pt: Não percamos de vista a questão mais ampla.
phrases.note: Redirects attention to the main issue.

phrases.en: The real issue lies elsewhere.
phrases.pt: A verdadeira questão está em outro lugar.
phrases.note: Moves the discussion away from a false focus.

phrases.en: It is worth pausing over that assumption.
phrases.pt: Vale a pena nos determos nessa suposição.
phrases.note: Slows the argument to inspect a premise.

phrases.en: That may be true, but it does not settle the matter.
phrases.pt: Isso pode ser verdade, mas não resolve a questão.
phrases.note: Concedes a point without accepting the conclusion.

phrases.en: The argument gains force when viewed in context.
phrases.pt: O argumento ganha força quando visto em contexto.
phrases.note: Explains why context strengthens a claim.

phrases.en: I do not think that objection is decisive.
phrases.pt: Não acho que essa objeção seja decisiva.
phrases.note: Acknowledges an objection but limits its impact.

phrases.en: The broader implication is hard to ignore.
phrases.pt: A implicação mais ampla é difícil de ignorar.
phrases.note: Draws attention to consequences beyond the details.

phrases.en: This brings us back to the central tension.
phrases.pt: Isso nos traz de volta à tensão central.
phrases.note: Returns the discussion to its core conflict.

title: Extra A1 — Weather and Clothes
topic: Weather, clothes, and simple daily choices

phrases.en: It's sunny today.
phrases.pt: Hoje está ensolarado.
phrases.note: Use it is to describe the weather.

phrases.en: It's raining now.
phrases.pt: Está chovendo agora.
phrases.note: Use is plus raining for weather happening now.

phrases.en: It's cold outside.
phrases.pt: Está frio lá fora.
phrases.note: Outside means not inside a building.

phrases.en: I need a jacket.
phrases.pt: Eu preciso de uma jaqueta.
phrases.note: Use I need for something necessary.

phrases.en: I'm wearing a blue shirt.
phrases.pt: Estou usando uma camisa azul.
phrases.note: Use wearing for clothes on your body now.

phrases.en: Do I need an umbrella?
phrases.pt: Eu preciso de um guarda-chuva?
phrases.note: Put do before I to ask about a need.

phrases.en: The weather is changing.
phrases.pt: O tempo está mudando.
phrases.note: Changing means becoming different.

phrases.en: Let's stay inside.
phrases.pt: Vamos ficar dentro de casa.
phrases.note: Let's makes a suggestion for you and another person.

title: Extra A2 — Home and Neighborhood
topic: Describing a home and the area around it

phrases.en: I live in a quiet neighborhood.
phrases.pt: Eu moro em um bairro tranquilo.
phrases.note: Quiet describes a place with little noise.

phrases.en: There is a market near my house.
phrases.pt: Há um mercado perto da minha casa.
phrases.note: Use there is for one place or thing.

phrases.en: The bus stop is across the street.
phrases.pt: O ponto de ônibus fica do outro lado da rua.
phrases.note: Across the street means on the opposite side.

phrases.en: My apartment has two bedrooms.
phrases.pt: Meu apartamento tem dois quartos.
phrases.note: Use has to describe parts of a home.

phrases.en: It takes ten minutes to walk downtown.
phrases.pt: Leva dez minutos para caminhar até o centro.
phrases.note: Use it takes to say how long a journey lasts.

phrases.en: The park gets busy on weekends.
phrases.pt: O parque fica movimentado nos fins de semana.
phrases.note: Gets busy means becomes full of people.

phrases.en: I moved here last year.
phrases.pt: Eu me mudei para cá no ano passado.
phrases.note: Moved is the past form of move.

phrases.en: Would you like to visit my place?
phrases.pt: Você gostaria de visitar minha casa?
phrases.note: Would you like is a polite way to invite someone.

title: Extra B1 — Learning Habits
topic: Explaining study routines and learning strategies

phrases.en: I learn faster when I use examples.
phrases.pt: Eu aprendo mais rápido quando uso exemplos.
phrases.note: Use when to connect a result with a regular condition.

phrases.en: I try to review a little every day.
phrases.pt: Eu tento revisar um pouco todos os dias.
phrases.note: Try to introduces an effort or habit.

phrases.en: Writing things down helps me remember them.
phrases.pt: Anotar as coisas me ajuda a lembrá-las.
phrases.note: An activity ending in -ing can be the subject of a sentence.

phrases.en: I lose focus if the session is too long.
phrases.pt: Eu perco o foco se a sessão for longa demais.
phrases.note: Use if for a condition that changes the result.

phrases.en: The hardest part is using new words naturally.
phrases.pt: A parte mais difícil é usar palavras novas naturalmente.
phrases.note: Use this frame to identify your main difficulty.

phrases.en: I've started listening without subtitles.
phrases.pt: Comecei a ouvir sem legendas.
phrases.note: I've started describes a recent change that matters now.

phrases.en: Making mistakes is part of the process.
phrases.pt: Cometer erros faz parte do processo.
phrases.note: Use this phrase to normalize a necessary step.

phrases.en: My goal is to speak with less hesitation.
phrases.pt: Meu objetivo é falar com menos hesitação.
phrases.note: Use my goal is to for a concrete objective.

title: Extra B2 — Decisions and Trade-offs
topic: Comparing options and explaining difficult decisions

phrases.en: Both options have clear advantages.
phrases.pt: As duas opções têm vantagens claras.
phrases.note: Use both to discuss two alternatives together.

phrases.en: The main trade-off is time versus quality.
phrases.pt: A principal compensação é tempo versus qualidade.
phrases.note: A trade-off is a balance where gaining one thing costs another.

phrases.en: I'm leaning toward the more flexible approach.
phrases.pt: Estou inclinado à abordagem mais flexível.
phrases.note: Use leaning toward when your preference is not final.

phrases.en: We need to weigh the risks against the benefits.
phrases.pt: Precisamos comparar os riscos com os benefícios.
phrases.note: Weigh something against something means compare their importance.

phrases.en: In the short term, the cheaper option makes sense.
phrases.pt: No curto prazo, a opção mais barata faz sentido.
phrases.note: Use this to limit a judgment to the near future.

phrases.en: That choice could create problems later on.
phrases.pt: Essa escolha pode criar problemas mais adiante.
phrases.note: Could presents a possible consequence.

phrases.en: I'd rather delay the decision than rush it.
phrases.pt: Eu preferiria adiar a decisão a tomá-la às pressas.
phrases.note: Would rather X than Y compares two actions.

phrases.en: On balance, the long-term option is stronger.
phrases.pt: Considerando tudo, a opção de longo prazo é melhor.
phrases.note: On balance introduces a conclusion after comparing factors.

title: Extra C1 — Leading Meetings
topic: Guiding discussion, disagreement, and decisions at work

phrases.en: Let's clarify what we need to decide today.
phrases.pt: Vamos esclarecer o que precisamos decidir hoje.
phrases.note: Frames the meeting around a concrete outcome.

phrases.en: Before we move on, I'd like to hear the opposing view.
phrases.pt: Antes de avançarmos, eu gostaria de ouvir a visão contrária.
phrases.note: Invites disagreement before closing a topic.

phrases.en: There seems to be broad agreement on the principle.
phrases.pt: Parece haver amplo acordo sobre o princípio.
phrases.note: Summarizes consensus without claiming unanimity.

phrases.en: The point of disagreement is how quickly we should act.
phrases.pt: O ponto de discordância é a rapidez com que devemos agir.
phrases.note: Isolates the exact issue still under debate.

phrases.en: Could you walk us through the assumptions behind that estimate?
phrases.pt: Você poderia nos explicar as premissas por trás dessa estimativa?
phrases.note: Asks for a structured explanation of reasoning.

phrases.en: We may be solving the wrong problem.
phrases.pt: Talvez estejamos resolvendo o problema errado.
phrases.note: Challenges the framing rather than a proposed solution.

phrases.en: I'll summarize the decision and the remaining actions.
phrases.pt: Vou resumir a decisão e as ações restantes.
phrases.note: Signals a concise close with ownership and next steps.

phrases.en: Unless there are objections, we'll proceed on that basis.
phrases.pt: A menos que haja objeções, prosseguiremos com base nisso.
phrases.note: Checks for final objections before confirming a decision.

title: Extra C2 — Critical Synthesis
topic: Combining competing evidence into a precise conclusion

phrases.en: Taken together, the evidence points to a more qualified conclusion.
phrases.pt: Consideradas em conjunto, as evidências apontam para uma conclusão mais ponderada.
phrases.note: Synthesizes several sources before stating a conclusion.

phrases.en: The two accounts are not mutually exclusive.
phrases.pt: As duas explicações não são mutuamente excludentes.
phrases.note: Shows that two explanations can both be true.

phrases.en: One explanation operates at the individual level, the other at the institutional level.
phrases.pt: Uma explicação opera no nível individual, a outra no nível institucional.
phrases.note: Reconciles arguments by separating their level of analysis.

phrases.en: The apparent contradiction dissolves once that distinction is made.
phrases.pt: A aparente contradição desaparece quando essa distinção é feita.
phrases.note: Explains why a conflict was only superficial.

phrases.en: The strongest claim supported by the data is considerably narrower.
phrases.pt: A afirmação mais forte sustentada pelos dados é consideravelmente mais restrita.
phrases.note: Limits a conclusion to what the evidence actually establishes.

phrases.en: That limitation does not invalidate the broader insight.
phrases.pt: Essa limitação não invalida a percepção mais ampla.
phrases.note: Acknowledges a limit while preserving a useful conclusion.

phrases.en: A plausible synthesis must account for both patterns.
phrases.pt: Uma síntese plausível precisa explicar os dois padrões.
phrases.note: Sets a requirement for an adequate combined explanation.

phrases.en: I would therefore resist reducing the issue to a single cause.
phrases.pt: Portanto, eu evitaria reduzir a questão a uma única causa.
phrases.note: Rejects an oversimplified conclusion after synthesis.

title: Lesson 30 — In the Kitchen
topic: Ingredients, quantities, and following a recipe
objective: Follow and give simple cooking instructions using quantities.
pronunciationFocus: Linking a final consonant to 'it': cut it, put it, turn it.

phrases.en: Can you pass me the salt?
phrases.pt: Você pode me passar o sal?
phrases.note: Can you...? is a friendly way to ask for something at the table.

phrases.en: Add a little oil to the pan.
phrases.pt: Adicione um pouco de óleo na panela.
phrases.note: Use a little with things you cannot count, like oil, salt, or water.

phrases.en: Cut the onions into small pieces.
phrases.pt: Corte as cebolas em pedaços pequenos.
phrases.note: Recipes start with the verb: cut, add, mix.

phrases.en: How many eggs do we need?
phrases.pt: De quantos ovos a gente precisa?
phrases.note: Use how many for things you can count, and how much for things you cannot.

phrases.en: Let it cook for ten minutes.
phrases.pt: Deixe cozinhar por dez minutos.
phrases.note: Let it cook tells someone to leave it cooking without touching it.

phrases.en: It tastes a bit salty.
phrases.pt: Está um pouco salgado.
phrases.note: After taste, use an adjective: it tastes salty, not saltily.

phrases.en: We don't have enough flour.
phrases.pt: A gente não tem farinha suficiente.
phrases.note: Enough comes before the noun: enough flour, enough time.

phrases.en: Turn down the heat, please.
phrases.pt: Abaixe o fogo, por favor.
phrases.note: Turn down means reduce; turn up means increase.

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.en: What are we cooking tonight?
dialogue.pt: O que a gente vai cozinhar hoje à noite?

dialogue.speaker: Bruno
dialogue.en: Chicken with rice. Cut the onions into small pieces, please.
dialogue.pt: Frango com arroz. Corte as cebolas em pedaços pequenos, por favor.

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.en: Sure. How many eggs do we need for the sauce?
dialogue.pt: Claro. De quantos ovos a gente precisa para o molho?

dialogue.speaker: Bruno
dialogue.en: Just two. And add a little oil to the pan first.
dialogue.pt: Só dois. E adicione um pouco de óleo na panela primeiro.

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.en: Okay. Wait — we don't have enough flour.
dialogue.pt: Certo. Espera — a gente não tem farinha suficiente.

dialogue.speaker: Bruno
dialogue.en: Then let's use less. Turn down the heat while I check the fridge.
dialogue.pt: Então vamos usar menos. Abaixe o fogo enquanto eu olho a geladeira.

comprehension.prompt: What are the two speakers doing?
comprehension.answer: Cooking a meal together

comprehension.prompt: What does Bruno ask Ana to do first?
comprehension.answer: Cut the onions

comprehension.prompt: Which ingredient is missing?
comprehension.answer: Flour

productionPrompt: Describe a simple dish you cook at home. Say what you need and give two instructions.
retryHint: Use one quantity word (a little, enough, how many) and start one instruction with a verb.

title: Lesson 31 — Free Time
topic: Hobbies and how often you do them
objective: Say what you do in your free time and how often you do it.
pronunciationFocus: Stress on the first syllable: USUally, NORmally, HARDly.

phrases.en: I usually play football on Sundays.
phrases.pt: Eu geralmente jogo futebol aos domingos.
phrases.note: Usually goes before the main verb: I usually play.

phrases.en: I go to the gym twice a week.
phrases.pt: Eu vou à academia duas vezes por semana.
phrases.note: Use once/twice/three times + a week to give exact frequency.

phrases.en: I'm really into photography.
phrases.pt: Eu curto muito fotografia.
phrases.note: Be into something is an informal way to say you like it a lot.

phrases.en: I hardly ever watch TV.
phrases.pt: Eu quase nunca vejo TV.
phrases.note: Hardly ever means almost never. Do not add another negative.

phrases.en: I started learning guitar last year.
phrases.pt: Eu comecei a aprender violão no ano passado.
phrases.note: After start you can use -ing: started learning, started running.

phrases.en: It helps me relax after work.
phrases.pt: Isso me ajuda a relaxar depois do trabalho.
phrases.note: After help, use the base verb: helps me relax.

phrases.en: I'm not very good at drawing.
phrases.pt: Eu não sou muito bom em desenhar.
phrases.note: Use be good at + -ing to talk about ability.

phrases.en: Do you want to join me next time?
phrases.pt: Você quer ir comigo da próxima vez?
phrases.note: Join me is a simple, friendly invitation.

dialogue.speaker: Carla
dialogue.en: What do you do on weekends?
dialogue.pt: O que você faz nos fins de semana?

dialogue.speaker: Diego
dialogue.en: I usually play football on Sundays. And I go to the gym twice a week.
dialogue.pt: Eu geralmente jogo futebol aos domingos. E vou à academia duas vezes por semana.

dialogue.speaker: Carla
dialogue.en: Nice. I hardly ever do sport. I'm really into photography.
dialogue.pt: Legal. Eu quase nunca faço esporte. Eu curto muito fotografia.

dialogue.speaker: Diego
dialogue.en: How long have you done that?
dialogue.pt: Faz quanto tempo que você faz isso?

dialogue.speaker: Carla
dialogue.en: About two years. It helps me relax after work.
dialogue.pt: Uns dois anos. Isso me ajuda a relaxar depois do trabalho.

dialogue.speaker: Diego
dialogue.en: I'd like to try it. Do you want to join me next time I go running?
dialogue.pt: Eu gostaria de experimentar. Você quer ir comigo da próxima vez que eu for correr?

comprehension.prompt: What are Carla and Diego talking about?
comprehension.answer: Their free-time activities

comprehension.prompt: How often does Diego go to the gym?
comprehension.answer: Twice a week

comprehension.prompt: Why does Carla like her hobby?
comprehension.answer: It helps her relax

productionPrompt: Talk about one thing you do in your free time. Say how often you do it and why you like it.
retryHint: Add one frequency expression (usually, twice a week, hardly ever) and one reason.

title: Lesson 32 — At the Hotel
topic: Checking in, room needs, and small complaints
objective: Check into a hotel, ask for what your room needs, and report a small problem.
pronunciationFocus: Polite rising intonation on requests: Could I have a towel?

phrases.en: I have a reservation under Silva.
phrases.pt: Eu tenho uma reserva no nome de Silva.
phrases.note: Under + name is how you give the name on the booking.

phrases.en: What time is check-out?
phrases.pt: Que horas é o check-out?
phrases.note: Use what time to ask about a fixed hour.

phrases.en: Could I have an extra towel, please?
phrases.pt: Eu poderia ter uma toalha extra, por favor?
phrases.note: Could I have...? is more polite than can I have...?

phrases.en: The air conditioning isn't working.
phrases.pt: O ar-condicionado não está funcionando.
phrases.note: Use the present continuous for a problem happening now.

phrases.en: Is breakfast included?
phrases.pt: O café da manhã está incluso?
phrases.note: Start with is to ask a simple yes/no question.

phrases.en: Could you move me to a quieter room?
phrases.pt: Você poderia me mudar para um quarto mais silencioso?
phrases.note: Quieter is the comparative of quiet: add -er to short adjectives.

phrases.en: There's no hot water in the bathroom.
phrases.pt: Não tem água quente no banheiro.
phrases.note: There's no + noun reports something missing.

phrases.en: Can you send someone to fix it?
phrases.pt: Você pode mandar alguém para consertar?
phrases.note: Use this to ask for help without blaming anyone.

dialogue.speaker: Receptionist
dialogue.en: Good evening. Do you have a booking with us?
dialogue.pt: Boa noite. O senhor tem uma reserva conosco?

dialogue.speaker: Guest
dialogue.en: Yes, I have a reservation under Silva. Is breakfast included?
dialogue.pt: Sim, eu tenho uma reserva no nome de Silva. O café da manhã está incluso?

dialogue.speaker: Receptionist
dialogue.en: It is, from seven to ten. You're in room 402.
dialogue.pt: Está sim, das sete às dez. O senhor está no quarto 402.

dialogue.speaker: Guest
dialogue.en: Thank you. What time is check-out?
dialogue.pt: Obrigado. Que horas é o check-out?

dialogue.speaker: Receptionist
dialogue.en: Midday. Is everything else all right?
dialogue.pt: Meio-dia. Está tudo bem no resto?

dialogue.speaker: Guest
dialogue.en: Actually, the air conditioning isn't working. Can you send someone to fix it?
dialogue.pt: Na verdade, o ar-condicionado não está funcionando. Você pode mandar alguém para consertar?

comprehension.prompt: Where is this conversation happening?
comprehension.answer: At a hotel reception desk

comprehension.prompt: What does the guest ask about first?
comprehension.answer: Whether breakfast is included

comprehension.prompt: What problem does the guest report?
comprehension.answer: The air conditioning is broken

productionPrompt: You have just arrived at a hotel. Check in, ask one question about the room, and report one problem.
retryHint: Use one polite request (Could I..., Could you...) and describe the problem with isn't working or there's no.

title: Lesson 33 — At the Airport
topic: Check-in, security, gates, and delays
objective: Get through check-in and security, and understand gate and delay announcements.
pronunciationFocus: Numbers and letters said clearly: gate B12, flight LA8067.

phrases.en: I'd like a window seat, please.
phrases.pt: Eu gostaria de um assento na janela, por favor.
phrases.note: I'd like is a polite way to say I want.

phrases.en: I only have carry-on luggage.
phrases.pt: Eu só tenho bagagem de mão.
phrases.note: Carry-on luggage is the bag you take into the plane.

phrases.en: Which gate does it leave from?
phrases.pt: De qual portão ele sai?
phrases.note: In questions, from often goes at the end: leave from.

phrases.en: The flight has been delayed.
phrases.pt: O voo foi adiado.
phrases.note: Has been delayed reports a change that affects you now.

phrases.en: Do I need to take out my laptop?
phrases.pt: Eu preciso tirar o meu notebook da mochila?
phrases.note: Use need to for something that is necessary.

phrases.en: How long is the layover?
phrases.pt: Quanto tempo é a conexão?
phrases.note: A layover is the waiting time between two flights.

phrases.en: Boarding starts in twenty minutes.
phrases.pt: O embarque começa em vinte minutos.
phrases.note: In twenty minutes means after twenty minutes from now.

phrases.en: Where can I pick up my bags?
phrases.pt: Onde eu pego minhas malas?
phrases.note: Pick up means collect. The bags arrive at baggage claim.

dialogue.speaker: Agent
dialogue.en: Good morning. May I see your passport?
dialogue.pt: Bom dia. Posso ver seu passaporte?

dialogue.speaker: Traveller
dialogue.en: Here you are. I'd like a window seat, please.
dialogue.pt: Aqui está. Eu gostaria de um assento na janela, por favor.

dialogue.speaker: Agent
dialogue.en: Of course. Are you checking any bags?
dialogue.pt: Claro. O senhor vai despachar alguma mala?

dialogue.speaker: Traveller
dialogue.en: No, I only have carry-on luggage. Which gate does it leave from?
dialogue.pt: Não, eu só tenho bagagem de mão. De qual portão ele sai?

dialogue.speaker: Agent
dialogue.en: Gate B12. But I'm afraid the flight has been delayed by an hour.
dialogue.pt: Portão B12. Mas infelizmente o voo foi adiado em uma hora.

dialogue.speaker: Traveller
dialogue.en: That's all right. Boarding starts at eleven, then?
dialogue.pt: Tudo bem. Então o embarque começa às onze?

comprehension.prompt: What is the traveller doing?
comprehension.answer: Checking in for a flight

comprehension.prompt: What does the traveller ask for?
comprehension.answer: A window seat

comprehension.prompt: What does the agent say at the end?
options: The flight is delayed by an hour
comprehension.answer: The flight is delayed by an hour

productionPrompt: You are checking in for a flight. Ask two questions and say what luggage you have.
retryHint: Use I'd like for your request and one question word (which, how long, where).

title: Lesson 34 — Appointments
topic: Booking, changing, and cancelling an appointment
objective: Book an appointment, then move or cancel it politely.
pronunciationFocus: Weak 'to' in phrases like need to and want to.

phrases.en: I'd like to book an appointment.
phrases.pt: Eu gostaria de marcar um horário.
phrases.note: Book is the normal verb for reserving a time.

phrases.en: Are you free on Thursday morning?
phrases.pt: Você está livre na quinta de manhã?
phrases.note: Be free means be available.

phrases.en: Does two o'clock work for you?
phrases.pt: Duas horas funciona para você?
phrases.note: Work for you asks if a time is convenient.

phrases.en: I'm afraid I can't make it.
phrases.pt: Infelizmente eu não vou conseguir ir.
phrases.note: Make it means manage to be there. I'm afraid softens bad news.

phrases.en: Could we move it to next week?
phrases.pt: A gente poderia mudar para a semana que vem?
phrases.note: Move it to + time asks to change the date.

phrases.en: Something has come up.
phrases.pt: Surgiu um imprevisto.
phrases.note: A polite way to say an unexpected thing happened, without details.

phrases.en: I need to cancel my appointment.
phrases.pt: Eu preciso cancelar meu horário.
phrases.note: Say this clearly and early so the other person can plan.

phrases.en: Sorry for the short notice.
phrases.pt: Desculpe pelo aviso em cima da hora.
phrases.note: Short notice means you are telling them very late.

dialogue.speaker: Clinic
dialogue.en: Good afternoon, Northside Clinic.
dialogue.pt: Boa tarde, Clínica Northside.

dialogue.speaker: Patient
dialogue.en: Hello. I'd like to book an appointment. Are you free on Thursday morning?
dialogue.pt: Olá. Eu gostaria de marcar um horário. Vocês têm vaga na quinta de manhã?

dialogue.speaker: Clinic
dialogue.en: Thursday is full, but we have Friday. Does two o'clock work for you?
dialogue.pt: Quinta está cheia, mas temos sexta. Duas horas funciona para você?

dialogue.speaker: Patient
dialogue.en: Yes, perfect. Thank you.
dialogue.pt: Sim, perfeito. Obrigada.

dialogue.speaker: Patient
dialogue.en: Hello, it's Marta. I'm afraid I can't make it on Friday. Something has come up.
dialogue.pt: Olá, aqui é a Marta. Infelizmente eu não vou conseguir ir na sexta. Surgiu um imprevisto.

dialogue.speaker: Clinic
dialogue.en: No problem. Could we move it to next Tuesday at the same time?
dialogue.pt: Sem problema. Podemos mudar para a próxima terça no mesmo horário?

comprehension.prompt: What happens across this conversation?
options: An appointment is booked and then changed
A patient complains about a doctor
A clinic cancels all its appointments
comprehension.answer: An appointment is booked and then changed

comprehension.prompt: Which day was not available?
comprehension.answer: Thursday

comprehension.prompt: Why does Marta call the second time?
comprehension.answer: She cannot come on Friday

productionPrompt: Call to book an appointment, then explain that you have to change it. Suggest a new time.
retryHint: Use one booking phrase and one polite excuse (I'm afraid..., Something has come up).

title: Lesson 35 — When You Don't Understand
topic: Asking someone to repeat, slow down, or explain
objective: Keep a conversation going when you miss a word or lose the thread.
pronunciationFocus: Sorry? and Again? rise at the end; a flat tone sounds rude.

phrases.en: Sorry, could you say that again?
phrases.pt: Desculpe, você poderia repetir?
phrases.note: The most useful repair phrase in English. Always polite.

phrases.en: Could you speak a bit more slowly?
phrases.pt: Você poderia falar um pouco mais devagar?
phrases.note: A bit more softens the request so it never sounds like a complaint.

phrases.en: What does that word mean?
phrases.pt: O que essa palavra significa?
phrases.note: Ask about one word, not the whole sentence.

phrases.en: How do you spell that?
phrases.pt: Como se escreve isso?
phrases.note: Useful for names, streets, and email addresses.

phrases.en: So you mean we start on Monday?
phrases.pt: Então você quer dizer que a gente começa na segunda?
phrases.note: Repeat the idea in your own words to check you understood.

phrases.en: I didn't catch the last part.
phrases.pt: Eu não peguei a última parte.
phrases.note: Naming the part you missed helps the other person help you.

phrases.en: Sorry, I'm not following you.
phrases.pt: Desculpe, não estou acompanhando.
phrases.note: Use this when the whole idea is unclear, not just one word.

phrases.en: Could you give me an example?
phrases.pt: Você poderia me dar um exemplo?
phrases.note: An example is often clearer than a second explanation.

dialogue.speaker: Manager
dialogue.en: We're moving the deadline forward, so the draft is due on Monday.
dialogue.pt: Vamos antecipar o prazo, então o rascunho vence na segunda.

dialogue.speaker: Paulo
dialogue.en: Sorry, could you say that again? I didn't catch the last part.
dialogue.pt: Desculpe, você poderia repetir? Eu não peguei a última parte.

dialogue.speaker: Manager
dialogue.en: Of course. The draft is due on Monday instead of Friday.
dialogue.pt: Claro. O rascunho vence na segunda, e não na sexta.

dialogue.speaker: Paulo
dialogue.en: So you mean we have less time than before?
dialogue.pt: Então você quer dizer que temos menos tempo do que antes?

dialogue.speaker: Manager
dialogue.en: Exactly. It's tight, but the scope is smaller.
dialogue.pt: Exatamente. É apertado, mas o escopo é menor.

dialogue.speaker: Paulo
dialogue.en: What does 'scope' mean here? Could you give me an example?
dialogue.pt: O que 'scope' significa aqui? Você poderia me dar um exemplo?

comprehension.prompt: What is Paulo doing in this conversation?
comprehension.answer: Checking that he understood correctly

comprehension.prompt: When is the draft due now?
comprehension.answer: Monday

comprehension.prompt: What does Paulo ask for at the end?
comprehension.answer: An example

productionPrompt: Someone gives you an instruction you only half understand. Ask two different questions to make it clear.
retryHint: Ask about one specific word or part, and check your understanding with So you mean...?

title: Lesson 36 — Job Interviews
topic: Experience, strengths, and interview follow-ups
objective: Present your experience and strengths in an interview with evidence, not adjectives.
pronunciationFocus: Contracted present perfect: I've worked, I've been, I've led.

phrases.en: I've been working in logistics for six years.
phrases.pt: Eu trabalho com logística há seis anos.
phrases.note: Use it for something that started in the past and is still true.

phrases.en: My background is mainly in customer support.
phrases.pt: Minha experiência é principalmente em atendimento ao cliente.
phrases.note: Background is the professional history you bring to the role.

phrases.en: One thing I'm good at is spotting problems early.
phrases.pt: Uma coisa em que sou bom é perceber problemas cedo.
phrases.note: One thing I'm good at is... puts the strength in focus.

phrases.en: In my last role, I was responsible for the schedule.
phrases.pt: No meu último cargo, eu era responsável pelo cronograma.
phrases.note: Name the concrete responsibility, not a vague quality.

phrases.en: That's something I'd like to develop further.
phrases.pt: Isso é algo que eu gostaria de desenvolver mais.
phrases.note: A safe way to answer a question about a weakness.

phrases.en: I'm looking for a role with more responsibility.
phrases.pt: Estou buscando um cargo com mais responsabilidade.
phrases.note: Say what you want from the job, not what you dislike about the old one.

phrases.en: Could you tell me more about the team?
phrases.pt: Você poderia me falar mais sobre a equipe?
phrases.note: Asking a question at the end shows genuine interest.

phrases.en: When can I expect to hear back from you?
phrases.pt: Quando eu posso esperar um retorno de vocês?
phrases.note: Hear back means receive an answer. Ask this before you leave.

dialogue.speaker: Interviewer
dialogue.en: Tell me a bit about your experience.
dialogue.pt: Fale um pouco sobre sua experiência.

dialogue.speaker: Renata
dialogue.en: I've been working in logistics for six years. In my last role, I was responsible for the schedule.
dialogue.pt: Eu trabalho com logística há seis anos. No meu último cargo, eu era responsável pelo cronograma.

dialogue.speaker: Interviewer
dialogue.en: And what would you say is your main strength?
dialogue.pt: E o que você diria que é seu principal ponto forte?

dialogue.speaker: Renata
dialogue.en: One thing I'm good at is spotting problems early. We cut late deliveries by a third.
dialogue.pt: Uma coisa em que sou boa é perceber problemas cedo. A gente reduziu as entregas atrasadas em um terço.

dialogue.speaker: Interviewer
dialogue.en: Why are you leaving your current company?
dialogue.pt: Por que você está saindo da empresa atual?

dialogue.speaker: Renata
dialogue.en: I'm looking for a role with more responsibility. Could you tell me more about the team?
dialogue.pt: Estou buscando um cargo com mais responsabilidade. Você poderia me falar mais sobre a equipe?

comprehension.prompt: How does Renata support her claim about her strength?
options: With a concrete result from her last job
By comparing herself to her colleagues
comprehension.answer: With a concrete result from her last job

comprehension.prompt: How long has Renata worked in logistics?
comprehension.answer: Six years

comprehension.prompt: How does she explain leaving her current company?
comprehension.answer: She wants more responsibility

productionPrompt: Answer the question 'What is your main strength?' with one strength and one concrete result that proves it.
retryHint: Replace any adjective about yourself with something you actually did and its outcome.

title: Lesson 37 — Meetings
topic: Giving updates, asking questions, and agreeing action items
objective: Give a status update in a meeting and leave with clear action items.
pronunciationFocus: Falling tone on decisions, rising tone on checks: We'll ship Friday. Agreed?

phrases.en: Quick update from my side.
phrases.pt: Uma atualização rápida da minha parte.
phrases.note: Signal what you are about to do so people can follow you.

phrases.en: We're on track to finish by Friday.
phrases.pt: Estamos no caminho certo para terminar até sexta.
phrases.note: On track means progress matches the plan.

phrases.en: We're a bit behind, but it's under control.
phrases.pt: Estamos um pouco atrasados, mas está sob controle.
phrases.note: Report bad news plainly, then say what you are doing about it.

phrases.en: Sorry, can I just come back to that point?
phrases.pt: Desculpe, posso voltar a esse ponto?
phrases.note: A polite way to interrupt and return to something unresolved.

phrases.en: I'm not sure I follow. Could you clarify?
phrases.pt: Não tenho certeza se entendi. Você poderia esclarecer?
phrases.note: Blame your own understanding, not their explanation.

phrases.en: So who's taking that one?
phrases.pt: Então quem vai ficar com isso?
phrases.note: Every action needs a name attached, or it will not happen.

phrases.en: Let's park that and move on.
phrases.pt: Vamos deixar isso de lado e seguir em frente.
phrases.note: Park it means postpone it without dropping it.

phrases.en: Just to confirm, I'll send the report by Wednesday.
phrases.pt: Só confirmando, eu vou enviar o relatório até quarta.
phrases.note: Repeat your own action item so everyone hears the commitment.

dialogue.speaker: Lead
dialogue.en: Let's go round the table. Tomás?
dialogue.pt: Vamos passar pela mesa. Tomás?

dialogue.speaker: Tomás
dialogue.en: Quick update from my side. We're a bit behind, but it's under control.
dialogue.pt: Uma atualização rápida da minha parte. Estamos um pouco atrasados, mas está sob controle.

dialogue.speaker: Lead
dialogue.en: What's blocking you?
dialogue.pt: O que está te travando?

dialogue.speaker: Tomás
dialogue.en: We're waiting on the supplier. I'm not sure I follow the new process, though. Could you clarify?
dialogue.pt: Estamos esperando o fornecedor. Mas não tenho certeza se entendi o novo processo. Você poderia esclarecer?

dialogue.speaker: Lead
dialogue.en: Fair point. Let's park that and cover it after. So who's taking the supplier call?
dialogue.pt: Boa observação. Vamos deixar isso de lado e ver depois. Então quem vai ficar com a ligação para o fornecedor?

dialogue.speaker: Tomás
dialogue.en: I will. Just to confirm, I'll send the report by Wednesday.
dialogue.pt: Eu fico. Só confirmando, eu vou enviar o relatório até quarta.

comprehension.prompt: What is the purpose of this meeting?
options: Sharing status and assigning next steps
Training the team on a tool
comprehension.answer: Sharing status and assigning next steps

comprehension.prompt: What is holding Tomás up?
options: He is waiting on the supplier
He is on holiday next week
comprehension.answer: He is waiting on the supplier

comprehension.prompt: What does Tomás commit to at the end?
comprehension.answer: Sending the report by Wednesday

productionPrompt: Give a one-minute update on something you are working on. Include one problem and one action item with a deadline.
retryHint: Make sure your update says who does what by when, not only how you feel about the work.

title: Lesson 38 — Email and Messages
topic: Professional tone in email and chat
objective: Write a short professional message that is direct without sounding cold.
pronunciationFocus: Read your message aloud: if it sounds abrupt, it reads abrupt.

phrases.en: I'm writing to ask about the invoice.
phrases.pt: Estou escrevendo para perguntar sobre a fatura.
phrases.note: Say why you are writing in the first line.

phrases.en: Just following up on my last message.
phrases.pt: Só retomando minha última mensagem.
phrases.note: A neutral way to chase an answer without sounding annoyed.

phrases.en: Could you send it over when you get a chance?
phrases.pt: Você poderia me enviar quando puder?
phrases.note: This softens a request without making the deadline unclear.

phrases.en: Apologies for the delay in replying.
phrases.pt: Desculpe pela demora em responder.
phrases.note: One short apology is enough. Do not over-explain.

phrases.en: Please let me know if that works for you.
phrases.pt: Por favor, me diga se isso funciona para você.
phrases.note: Invite an answer so the message does not end flatly.

phrases.en: As discussed, I've attached the summary.
phrases.pt: Conforme conversamos, anexei o resumo.
phrases.note: Reminds the reader of the context in three words.

phrases.en: Would it be possible to move the call?
phrases.pt: Seria possível remarcar a ligação?
phrases.note: The most polite way to ask for a change.

phrases.en: Thanks in advance for your help.
phrases.pt: Desde já, obrigado pela ajuda.
phrases.note: A warm closing line before you sign off.

dialogue.speaker: Júlia
dialogue.en: Can you look at this email before I send it? It feels rude.
dialogue.pt: Você pode olhar este e-mail antes de eu enviar? Parece grosseiro.

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.en: Read it to me.
dialogue.pt: Leia para mim.

dialogue.speaker: Júlia
dialogue.en: 'Send me the invoice today.' That's the whole message.
dialogue.pt: 'Send me the invoice today.' É a mensagem inteira.

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.en: The problem isn't the deadline, it's that there's no context. Start with why you're writing.
dialogue.pt: O problema não é o prazo, é a falta de contexto. Comece dizendo por que está escrevendo.

dialogue.speaker: Júlia
dialogue.en: So: 'I'm writing to ask about the invoice. Could you send it over when you get a chance?'
dialogue.pt: Então: 'I'm writing to ask about the invoice. Could you send it over when you get a chance?'

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.en: Much better. Keep the deadline, just add a line and a thank you.
dialogue.pt: Bem melhor. Mantenha o prazo, só acrescente uma linha e um agradecimento.

comprehension.prompt: Why did Júlia's first message sound rude?
options: It gave an order with no context
comprehension.answer: It gave an order with no context

comprehension.prompt: What does Sam tell her to start with?
comprehension.answer: The reason she is writing

comprehension.prompt: What does Sam say about the deadline?
comprehension.answer: She should keep it

productionPrompt: Rewrite this message so it stays direct but sounds polite: 'Send me the report today.'
retryHint: Keep the request and the deadline. Add a reason for writing and one closing line.

title: Lesson 39 — When Travel Goes Wrong
topic: Missed connections, lost items, and finding alternatives
objective: Explain what went wrong on a trip and negotiate a workable alternative.
pronunciationFocus: Stay calm and level: stress the facts, not the frustration.

phrases.en: I've missed my connection to Lisbon.
phrases.pt: Eu perdi minha conexão para Lisboa.
phrases.note: The past action matters because of its effect right now.

phrases.en: My suitcase didn't arrive with the flight.
phrases.pt: Minha mala não chegou com o voo.
phrases.note: State the fact plainly before you ask for anything.

phrases.en: What are my options at this point?
phrases.pt: Quais são minhas opções nesse momento?
phrases.note: This invites help instead of starting an argument.

phrases.en: Is there any way to get there tonight?
phrases.pt: Tem alguma forma de chegar lá hoje à noite?
phrases.note: A softer, more effective request than demanding a solution.

phrases.en: I'd rather wait than pay for a new ticket.
phrases.pt: Eu prefiro esperar a pagar por uma passagem nova.
phrases.note: After would rather, use the base verb: rather wait, not rather to wait.

phrases.en: Who should I speak to about compensation?
phrases.pt: Com quem eu devo falar sobre indenização?
phrases.note: Getting to the right person matters more than repeating the complaint.

phrases.en: Could you put that in writing for me?
phrases.pt: Você poderia me passar isso por escrito?
phrases.note: Ask for written confirmation of anything you were promised.

phrases.en: I understand it's not your fault, but I need a solution.
phrases.pt: Eu entendo que não é culpa sua, mas eu preciso de uma solução.
phrases.note: Separating the person from the problem keeps them helping you.

dialogue.speaker: Agent
dialogue.en: Next, please. How can I help?
dialogue.pt: Próximo, por favor. Como posso ajudar?

dialogue.speaker: Marcos
dialogue.en: I've missed my connection to Lisbon. The first flight landed an hour late.
dialogue.pt: Eu perdi minha conexão para Lisboa. O primeiro voo pousou uma hora atrasado.

dialogue.speaker: Agent
dialogue.en: I'm sorry. The next flight is tomorrow at six.
dialogue.pt: Sinto muito. O próximo voo é amanhã às seis.

dialogue.speaker: Marcos
dialogue.en: Is there any way to get there tonight? What are my options at this point?
dialogue.pt: Tem alguma forma de chegar lá hoje à noite? Quais são minhas opções nesse momento?

dialogue.speaker: Agent
dialogue.en: There's a train, but you would have to buy the ticket yourself.
dialogue.pt: Tem um trem, mas o senhor teria que comprar a passagem por conta própria.

dialogue.speaker: Marcos
dialogue.en: I understand it's not your fault, but I need a solution. Could you put that in writing for me?
dialogue.pt: Eu entendo que não é culpa sua, mas eu preciso de uma solução. Você poderia me passar isso por escrito?

comprehension.prompt: What is Marcos trying to achieve?
options: A way to reach Lisbon tonight
comprehension.answer: A way to reach Lisbon tonight

comprehension.prompt: Why did he miss his connection?
options: He arrived at the airport late
He went to the wrong gate
comprehension.answer: His first flight landed late

comprehension.prompt: What alternative does the agent offer?
options: A train he must pay for
comprehension.answer: A train he must pay for

productionPrompt: Your flight was cancelled and you must be somewhere tomorrow. Explain the problem and ask for two alternatives.
retryHint: Separate the facts from the request: say what happened, then ask what your options are.

title: Lesson 40 — Money Decisions
topic: Budgets, bills, saving, and everyday money choices
objective: Talk about what you spend, what you save, and why a purchase is or is not worth it.
pronunciationFocus: Contracted 'used to' and 'have to': I usta, I hafta.

phrases.en: I'm trying to stick to a budget.
phrases.pt: Estou tentando seguir um orçamento.
phrases.note: Stick to a plan means follow it even when it is hard.

phrases.en: Rent takes up most of my salary.
phrases.pt: O aluguel consome a maior parte do meu salário.
phrases.note: Take up means use a large part of something.

phrases.en: I set aside a little every month.
phrases.pt: Eu guardo um pouco todo mês.
phrases.note: Set aside means save deliberately, not by accident.

phrases.en: It's not worth it for the price.
phrases.pt: Não vale a pena por esse preço.
phrases.note: Worth it judges value, not cost. Something cheap can still not be worth it.

phrases.en: I can't afford that at the moment.
phrases.pt: Eu não posso pagar isso no momento.
phrases.note: Afford means have enough money for. It is not the same as want.

phrases.en: I'd rather save up than borrow.
phrases.pt: Eu prefiro juntar dinheiro a pegar emprestado.
phrases.note: Save up means collect money over time for one thing.

phrases.en: The bill is higher than I expected.
phrases.pt: A conta veio mais alta do que eu esperava.
phrases.note: Use than I expected to compare reality with your prediction.

phrases.en: In the long run, it pays for itself.
phrases.pt: No longo prazo, ele se paga.
phrases.note: Something pays for itself when the savings cover the cost.

dialogue.speaker: Léo
dialogue.en: I'm thinking about buying a car.
dialogue.pt: Estou pensando em comprar um carro.

dialogue.speaker: Bia
dialogue.en: Can you afford the monthly payments?
dialogue.pt: Você consegue pagar as parcelas mensais?

dialogue.speaker: Léo
dialogue.en: Barely. Rent takes up most of my salary already.
dialogue.pt: Por pouco. O aluguel já consome a maior parte do meu salário.

dialogue.speaker: Bia
dialogue.en: Then it's not worth it for the price. What about a used one?
dialogue.pt: Então não vale a pena por esse preço. E um usado?

dialogue.speaker: Léo
dialogue.en: Maybe. I'd rather save up than borrow, honestly.
dialogue.pt: Talvez. Sinceramente, eu prefiro juntar dinheiro a pegar emprestado.

dialogue.speaker: Bia
dialogue.en: That's sensible. If you set aside a little every month, you'll get there.
dialogue.pt: Isso é sensato. Se você guardar um pouco todo mês, você chega lá.

comprehension.prompt: What are Léo and Bia deciding?
options: Whether Léo can afford a car
comprehension.answer: Whether Léo can afford a car

comprehension.prompt: What already takes most of Léo's salary?
comprehension.answer: Rent

comprehension.prompt: What does Léo prefer to do?
comprehension.answer: Save up rather than borrow

productionPrompt: Describe one money decision you are weighing. Say what it costs, what you would give up, and whether it is worth it.
retryHint: Use one value phrase (worth it, afford, pays for itself) and give an actual reason.

title: Lesson 41 — Telling a Story
topic: Sequencing events and adding detail to a personal story
objective: Tell a personal story in order, with background detail and a point at the end.
pronunciationFocus: Slow down before the punchline; pause where the listener should react.

phrases.en: This happened a couple of years ago.
phrases.pt: Isso aconteceu há uns dois anos.
phrases.note: Open a story by placing it in time.

phrases.en: I was waiting for the bus when I saw her.
phrases.pt: Eu estava esperando o ônibus quando a vi.
phrases.note: The long action sets the scene; the short action interrupts it.

phrases.en: At first, I didn't realise what was going on.
phrases.pt: No começo, eu não percebi o que estava acontecendo.
phrases.note: At first prepares the listener for a change later.

phrases.en: Anyway, to cut a long story short...
phrases.pt: Enfim, resumindo a história...
phrases.note: Use this to skip detail and get to the point.

phrases.en: The funny thing is, she said the same to me.
phrases.pt: O engraçado é que ela me disse a mesma coisa.
phrases.note: Signals the surprising part is coming next.

phrases.en: In the end, we both laughed about it.
