phrases.pt: No final, nós dois rimos disso.
phrases.note: In the end gives the outcome. At the end refers to a place or time.

phrases.en: I'd never seen anything like it.
phrases.pt: Eu nunca tinha visto nada parecido.
phrases.note: Use had + participle for something before the main past moment.

phrases.en: Ever since then, I always check twice.
phrases.pt: Desde então, eu sempre confiro duas vezes.
phrases.note: Close the story by linking it to how you behave now.

dialogue.speaker: Nina
dialogue.en: You said you had a strange day. What happened?
dialogue.pt: Você disse que teve um dia estranho. O que aconteceu?

dialogue.speaker: Rafa
dialogue.en: This happened a couple of years ago, actually. I was waiting for the bus when I saw my old teacher.
dialogue.pt: Na verdade, isso aconteceu há uns dois anos. Eu estava esperando o ônibus quando vi minha antiga professora.

dialogue.speaker: Nina
dialogue.en: And did you say hello?
dialogue.pt: E você falou com ela?

dialogue.speaker: Rafa
dialogue.en: At first, I didn't realise it was her. I'd never seen her outside school.
dialogue.pt: No começo, eu não percebi que era ela. Eu nunca a tinha visto fora da escola.

dialogue.speaker: Nina
dialogue.en: So what did you do?
dialogue.pt: E o que você fez?

dialogue.speaker: Rafa
dialogue.en: Anyway, to cut a long story short, we missed the bus talking. In the end, we both laughed about it.
dialogue.pt: Enfim, resumindo a história, perdemos o ônibus de tanto conversar. No final, nós dois rimos disso.

comprehension.prompt: What is Rafa's story about?
options: An unexpected meeting at a bus stop
comprehension.answer: An unexpected meeting at a bus stop

comprehension.prompt: What happened first?
options: He was waiting for the bus
comprehension.answer: He was waiting for the bus

comprehension.prompt: Why didn't he recognise her immediately?
options: He had never seen her outside school
comprehension.answer: He had never seen her outside school

productionPrompt: Tell a short story about something unexpected that happened to you. Set the scene, say what interrupted it, and end with the outcome.
retryHint: Use one past continuous to set the scene and one closing phrase (in the end, ever since then).

title: Lesson 42 — Recommendations
topic: Recommending things and backing the recommendation with reasons
objective: Recommend or warn against something and justify it with a reason and a caveat.
pronunciationFocus: Stress the reason, not the adjective: it's great BECAUSE it's quiet.

phrases.en: You should definitely give it a try.
phrases.pt: Você deveria mesmo experimentar.
phrases.note: Definitely strengthens the advice without making it an order.

phrases.en: It's worth going early to avoid the queue.
phrases.pt: Vale a pena ir cedo para evitar a fila.
phrases.note: After worth, use the -ing form: worth going, worth reading.

phrases.en: What I liked most was the service.
phrases.pt: O que eu mais gostei foi do atendimento.
phrases.note: Starting with What I liked most puts your reason in focus.

phrases.en: The only downside is the price.
phrases.pt: O único ponto negativo é o preço.
phrases.note: Naming one honest downside makes the whole review believable.

phrases.en: It's not as good as everyone says.
phrases.pt: Não é tão bom quanto todo mundo diz.
phrases.note: A measured way to disagree with a popular opinion.

phrases.en: I wouldn't bother, to be honest.
phrases.pt: Eu não perderia meu tempo, para ser sincero.
phrases.note: A soft but clear way to advise against something.

phrases.en: It depends on what you're looking for.
phrases.pt: Depende do que você está procurando.
phrases.note: Use this before giving a conditional recommendation.

phrases.en: If you like quiet places, you'll love it.
phrases.pt: Se você gosta de lugares tranquilos, você vai adorar.
phrases.note: Match the recommendation to the listener, not to yourself.

dialogue.speaker: Tati
dialogue.en: We're looking for somewhere to eat on Saturday. Any ideas?
dialogue.pt: Estamos procurando um lugar para comer no sábado. Alguma ideia?

dialogue.speaker: Gui
dialogue.en: There's a place near the park. You should definitely give it a try.
dialogue.pt: Tem um lugar perto do parque. Você deveria mesmo experimentar.

dialogue.speaker: Tati
dialogue.en: What's it like?
dialogue.pt: Como é lá?

dialogue.speaker: Gui
dialogue.en: What I liked most was the service. The only downside is the price.
dialogue.pt: O que eu mais gostei foi do atendimento. O único ponto negativo é o preço.

dialogue.speaker: Tati
dialogue.en: Hmm. My brother said the food there was overrated.
dialogue.pt: Hmm. Meu irmão disse que a comida de lá é superestimada.

dialogue.speaker: Gui
dialogue.en: It depends on what you're looking for. If you like quiet places, you'll love it.
dialogue.pt: Depende do que você está procurando. Se você gosta de lugares tranquilos, você vai adorar.

comprehension.prompt: What is Gui doing?
options: Recommending a restaurant with an honest caveat
Warning Tati not to go out
comprehension.answer: Recommending a restaurant with an honest caveat

comprehension.prompt: What did Gui like most?
comprehension.answer: The service

comprehension.prompt: What downside does he admit?
comprehension.answer: It is expensive

productionPrompt: Recommend a place, film, or app to someone. Give one reason, one downside, and say who it suits.
retryHint: A recommendation without a reason is just an opinion. Add why, and add one honest downside.

title: Lesson 43 — Apologising and Fixing It
topic: Taking responsibility and repairing a situation
objective: Apologise for a real mistake, take responsibility, and propose the repair.
pronunciationFocus: A sincere apology is slower and lower; speed sounds defensive.

phrases.en: That was my mistake, and I'm sorry.
phrases.pt: Foi erro meu, e eu peço desculpas.
phrases.note: Say what you did before you explain why. No 'but' in this sentence.

phrases.en: I should have told you sooner.
phrases.pt: Eu deveria ter te contado antes.
phrases.note: Use should have for regret about the past.

phrases.en: I take full responsibility for this.
phrases.pt: Eu assumo total responsabilidade por isso.
phrases.note: Stronger and more credible than a general sorry.

phrases.en: There's no excuse for it.
phrases.pt: Não há desculpa para isso.
phrases.note: Say this only when you mean it; then move straight to the fix.

phrases.en: Here's what I'm going to do about it.
phrases.pt: É isso que eu vou fazer a respeito.
phrases.note: An apology without a repair rarely rebuilds trust.

phrases.en: It won't happen again.
phrases.pt: Isso não vai se repetir.
phrases.note: Only promise this if you have changed something concrete.

phrases.en: I understand why you're frustrated.
phrases.pt: Eu entendo por que você está frustrado.
phrases.note: Naming their feeling calms the situation faster than defending yourself.

phrases.en: How can I make it up to you?
phrases.pt: Como eu posso compensar isso?
phrases.note: Let the other person say what a fair repair looks like.

dialogue.speaker: Client
dialogue.en: The report arrived two days late. We had to delay the whole review.
dialogue.pt: O relatório chegou dois dias atrasado. Tivemos que adiar toda a revisão.

dialogue.speaker: Dani
dialogue.en: That was my mistake, and I'm sorry. I should have told you sooner that we were behind.
dialogue.pt: Foi erro meu, e eu peço desculpas. Eu deveria ter te contado antes que estávamos atrasados.

dialogue.speaker: Client
dialogue.en: We could have rescheduled if we'd known.
dialogue.pt: Poderíamos ter remarcado se soubéssemos.

dialogue.speaker: Dani
dialogue.en: I understand why you're frustrated. There's no excuse for it.
dialogue.pt: Eu entendo por que você está frustrado. Não há desculpa para isso.

dialogue.speaker: Client
dialogue.en: So what now?
dialogue.pt: E agora?

dialogue.speaker: Dani
dialogue.en: Here's what I'm going to do about it: you'll get a status note every Friday. How can I make it up to you?
dialogue.pt: É isso que eu vou fazer a respeito: você vai receber um informe toda sexta. Como eu posso compensar isso?

comprehension.prompt: How does Dani handle the complaint?
options: She takes responsibility and offers a fix
She denies the report was late
comprehension.answer: She takes responsibility and offers a fix

comprehension.prompt: What does the client say the delay caused?
options: The review had to be postponed
comprehension.answer: The review had to be postponed

comprehension.prompt: What does Dani promise at the end?
comprehension.answer: A status note every Friday

productionPrompt: You missed a deadline that affected someone else. Apologise, take responsibility, and propose one concrete fix.
retryHint: Check that your apology contains no 'but'. Then add one specific thing you will change.

title: Lesson 44 — In the Classroom
topic: Classroom objects and simple instructions
objective: Follow simple classroom instructions and ask for the things you need.
pronunciationFocus: Stress the important word in a question: Which PAGE? What WORD?

phrases.en: Please open your book.
phrases.pt: Por favor, abra seu livro.
phrases.note: Please makes a direct instruction sound polite.

phrases.en: Which page is it?
phrases.pt: Qual é a página?
phrases.note: Use which when choosing from a known set, such as pages in a book.

phrases.en: I don't have a pen.
phrases.pt: Eu não tenho uma caneta.
phrases.note: Use don't have to say something is missing.

phrases.en: Can I borrow yours?
phrases.pt: Posso pegar a sua emprestada?
phrases.note: Yours replaces your pen, your book, or another object already named.

phrases.en: How do you write that word?
phrases.pt: Como se escreve essa palavra?
phrases.note: Use this when you hear a word but do not know its letters.

phrases.en: Please work with a partner.
phrases.pt: Por favor, trabalhe com uma dupla.
phrases.note: A partner is the one person you do an activity with.

phrases.en: Write your name at the top.
phrases.pt: Escreva seu nome no alto da página.
phrases.note: At the top means in the highest part of the page.

phrases.en: Is this answer right?
phrases.pt: Esta resposta está certa?
phrases.note: Right means correct here, not the direction.

dialogue.speaker: Teacher
dialogue.en: Please open your book and work with a partner.
dialogue.pt: Por favor, abra seu livro e trabalhe com uma dupla.

dialogue.speaker: Lia
dialogue.en: Which page is it?
dialogue.pt: Qual é a página?

dialogue.speaker: Teacher
dialogue.en: Page eighteen. Write your name at the top.
dialogue.pt: Página dezoito. Escreva seu nome no alto da página.

dialogue.speaker: Lia
dialogue.en: I don't have a pen. Can I borrow yours, Pedro?
dialogue.pt: Eu não tenho uma caneta. Posso pegar a sua emprestada, Pedro?

dialogue.speaker: Pedro
dialogue.en: Sure. Here you are.
dialogue.pt: Claro. Aqui está.

dialogue.speaker: Lia
dialogue.en: Thanks. How do you spell the first word?
dialogue.pt: Obrigada. Como se soletra a primeira palavra?

comprehension.prompt: What are the students getting ready to do?
comprehension.answer: An activity in pairs

comprehension.prompt: Which page do they need?
comprehension.answer: Page eighteen

comprehension.prompt: What does Lia borrow?
comprehension.answer: A pen

productionPrompt: You are in class. Ask which page to open, say you do not have one object, and ask to borrow it.
retryHint: Use Which page...? for the instruction and Can I borrow...? for the object.

title: Lesson 45 — Time and Dates
topic: Clock time, days, dates, and schedules
objective: Ask and answer basic questions about times, days, and dates.
pronunciationFocus: Keep thirteen and thirty distinct: thirTEEN, THIRty.

phrases.en: What time does it start?
phrases.pt: A que horas começa?
phrases.note: Use does with it, the class, or the film.

phrases.en: It starts at half past nine.
phrases.pt: Começa às nove e meia.
phrases.note: Use at before a clock time.

phrases.en: What day is it today?
phrases.pt: Que dia da semana é hoje?
phrases.note: What day asks for Monday, Tuesday, and so on.

phrases.en: Today is Monday.
phrases.pt: Hoje é segunda-feira.
phrases.note: Days of the week begin with a capital letter in English.

phrases.en: My birthday is on May twelfth.
phrases.pt: Meu aniversário é em doze de maio.
phrases.note: Use on before a day or a complete date.

phrases.en: Are you free this afternoon?
phrases.pt: Você está livre hoje à tarde?
phrases.note: Free means available in this question.

phrases.en: I'm a little early.
phrases.pt: Cheguei um pouco cedo.
phrases.note: Early means before the planned time.

phrases.en: We're ten minutes late.
phrases.pt: Estamos dez minutos atrasados.
phrases.note: Put the number of minutes before late.

dialogue.speaker: Ivo
dialogue.en: What day is the English class?
dialogue.pt: Que dia é a aula de inglês?

dialogue.speaker: Maya
dialogue.en: It's on Tuesday, May twelfth.
dialogue.pt: É na terça-feira, doze de maio.

dialogue.speaker: Ivo
dialogue.en: What time does it start?
dialogue.pt: A que horas começa?

dialogue.speaker: Maya
dialogue.en: It starts at half past nine.
dialogue.pt: Começa às nove e meia.

dialogue.speaker: Ivo
dialogue.en: It's nine fifteen now. I'm a little early.
dialogue.pt: São nove e quinze agora. Cheguei um pouco cedo.

dialogue.speaker: Maya
dialogue.en: Yes, you are. Let's get some coffee.
dialogue.pt: Sim. Vamos tomar um café.

comprehension.prompt: What are Ivo and Maya checking?
options: The date and time of a class
comprehension.answer: The date and time of a class

comprehension.prompt: When is the class?
comprehension.answer: Tuesday, May twelfth

comprehension.prompt: How early is Ivo?
comprehension.answer: Fifteen minutes

productionPrompt: Tell a classmate the day, date, and time of your next class or appointment, then say if you are early or late.
retryHint: Use on before the day or date and at before the clock time.

title: Lesson 46 — Things You Like
topic: Likes, dislikes, and simple reasons
objective: Talk about things you like and dislike and give a simple reason.
pronunciationFocus: Stress the thing you contrast: I like TEA, but I don't like COFFEE.

phrases.en: I really like this song.
phrases.pt: Eu gosto muito desta música.
phrases.note: Really makes like stronger.

phrases.en: I don't like scary films.
phrases.pt: Eu não gosto de filmes de terror.
phrases.note: Use don't like before a thing or type of thing.

phrases.en: My favourite food is pasta.
phrases.pt: Minha comida preferida é macarrão.
phrases.note: Favourite names the one you like most.

phrases.en: I like it because it's funny.
phrases.pt: Eu gosto porque é engraçado.
phrases.note: Because connects what you like to the reason.

phrases.en: What kind of music do you like?
phrases.pt: De que tipo de música você gosta?
phrases.note: Use what kind of to ask about a type or category.

phrases.en: I prefer tea to coffee.
phrases.pt: Eu prefiro chá a café.
phrases.note: Use to, not than, after prefer.

phrases.en: Me too!
phrases.pt: Eu também!
phrases.note: Use me too to agree with a positive statement.

phrases.en: It's not my favourite.
phrases.pt: Não é o meu preferido.
phrases.note: This is softer than saying you hate something.

dialogue.speaker: Caio
dialogue.en: What kind of music do you like?
dialogue.pt: De que tipo de música você gosta?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.en: I really like pop music because it's fun.
dialogue.pt: Eu gosto muito de música pop porque é divertida.

dialogue.speaker: Caio
dialogue.en: Me too! Do you like this song?
dialogue.pt: Eu também! Você gosta desta música?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.en: It's not my favourite. It's very slow.
dialogue.pt: Não é a minha preferida. É muito lenta.

dialogue.speaker: Caio
dialogue.en: I like it because the singer is great.
dialogue.pt: Eu gosto porque a cantora é ótima.

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.en: The singer is great, but I prefer faster songs.
dialogue.pt: A cantora é ótima, mas eu prefiro músicas mais rápidas.

comprehension.prompt: What are Ana and Caio talking about?
comprehension.answer: Their taste in music

comprehension.prompt: Why does Ana like pop music?
comprehension.answer: Because it is fun

comprehension.prompt: What does Ana prefer?
comprehension.answer: Faster songs

productionPrompt: Talk about one kind of music, food, or film you like and one you do not like. Give a reason for each.
retryHint: Add because after each opinion so the listener knows your reason.

title: Lesson 47 — What You Can Do
topic: Abilities, help, and learning a skill
objective: Say what you can and cannot do and ask someone to show or help you.
pronunciationFocus: Can is usually weak in statements; can't is stressed: I can SWIM. I CAN'T DRIVE.

phrases.en: I can swim quite well.
phrases.pt: Eu sei nadar bastante bem.
phrases.note: After can, use the base verb without to.

phrases.en: I can't drive.
phrases.pt: Eu não sei dirigir.
phrases.note: Can't says that an action is not possible for you.

phrases.en: Could you give me a hand?
phrases.pt: Você poderia me dar uma ajuda?
phrases.note: Use this simple question to ask for help.

phrases.en: I know how to make it.
phrases.pt: Eu sei fazer isso.
phrases.note: Know how to describes a skill you learned.

phrases.en: I'm good at drawing.
phrases.pt: Eu desenho bem.
phrases.note: After good at, use a noun or an -ing verb.

phrases.en: I'm not very good at this.
phrases.pt: Eu não sou muito bom nisso.
phrases.note: This is a gentle way to say a skill is difficult for you.

phrases.en: Could you show me?
phrases.pt: Você poderia me mostrar?
phrases.note: Could you is a polite way to ask for a demonstration.

phrases.en: Let's try it together.
phrases.pt: Vamos tentar juntos.
phrases.note: Let's suggests doing an action together.

dialogue.speaker: Niko
dialogue.en: Can you help me with this drawing?
dialogue.pt: Você pode me ajudar com este desenho?

dialogue.speaker: Bela
dialogue.en: Sure. I'm good at drawing faces.
dialogue.pt: Claro. Eu desenho rostos bem.

dialogue.speaker: Niko
dialogue.en: I'm not very good at this. Could you show me?
dialogue.pt: Eu não sou muito bom nisso. Você poderia me mostrar?

dialogue.speaker: Bela
dialogue.en: Of course. Start with a circle for the head.
dialogue.pt: Claro. Comece com um círculo para a cabeça.

dialogue.speaker: Niko
dialogue.en: Okay. I can draw the eyes now.
dialogue.pt: Certo. Agora eu consigo desenhar os olhos.

dialogue.speaker: Bela
dialogue.en: Great. Let's try the mouth together.
dialogue.pt: Ótimo. Vamos tentar fazer a boca juntos.

comprehension.prompt: What is Bela helping Niko do?
comprehension.answer: Draw a face

comprehension.prompt: What is Bela good at drawing?
comprehension.answer: Faces

comprehension.prompt: What does Niko draw after the circle?
comprehension.answer: The eyes

productionPrompt: Name one thing you can do well and one thing you cannot do yet. Ask someone to show you one step.
retryHint: Use can or can't plus the base verb, then ask Could you show me...?

title: Lesson 48 — Sharing Responsibilities
topic: Chores, responsibilities, and routine obligations
objective: Explain routine responsibilities and agree who will do each task.
pronunciationFocus: Have to often sounds like hafta in natural speech: I hafta clean.

phrases.en: I have to clean the kitchen.
phrases.pt: Eu tenho que limpar a cozinha.
phrases.note: Have to describes something necessary, not a personal preference.

phrases.en: I'm responsible for the shopping.
phrases.pt: Eu sou responsável pelas compras.
phrases.note: Use responsible for to say a task belongs to you.

phrases.en: It's my turn to wash the dishes.
phrases.pt: É a minha vez de lavar a louça.
phrases.note: Use my turn when people take turns doing a task.

phrases.en: Could you take care of the laundry?
phrases.pt: Você poderia cuidar da roupa para lavar?
phrases.note: Take care of means accept responsibility for a task or person.

phrases.en: I need to take the rubbish out.
phrases.pt: Eu preciso levar o lixo para fora.
phrases.note: Take the rubbish out means move it from the home to the collection place.

phrases.en: We share the housework.
phrases.pt: Nós dividimos as tarefas de casa.
phrases.note: Share means divide something fairly between people.

phrases.en: I forgot to feed the cat.
phrases.pt: Eu esqueci de alimentar o gato.
phrases.note: Forget to do means the necessary action did not happen.

phrases.en: I'll do it as soon as I get home.
phrases.pt: Eu vou fazer assim que chegar em casa.
phrases.note: After as soon as, use the present even when you mean the future.

dialogue.speaker: Rui
dialogue.en: We have people coming tonight. How should we share the housework?
dialogue.pt: Vamos receber gente hoje à noite. Como vamos dividir as tarefas de casa?

dialogue.speaker: Cris
dialogue.en: I'll clean the bathroom. Could you take care of the kitchen?
dialogue.pt: Eu limpo o banheiro. Você poderia cuidar da cozinha?

dialogue.speaker: Rui
dialogue.en: Sure. It's also my turn to wash the dishes.
dialogue.pt: Claro. Também é a minha vez de lavar a louça.

dialogue.speaker: Cris
dialogue.en: Thanks. I'm responsible for the shopping, so I'll get some food.
dialogue.pt: Obrigada. Eu sou responsável pelas compras, então vou buscar comida.

dialogue.speaker: Rui
dialogue.en: What about the rubbish?
dialogue.pt: E o lixo?

dialogue.speaker: Cris
dialogue.en: I forgot to take it out. I'll do it as soon as I get home.
dialogue.pt: Eu esqueci de levar para fora. Vou fazer isso assim que chegar em casa.

comprehension.prompt: What are Rui and Cris doing?
options: Dividing the housework before guests arrive
Planning a shopping trip for next week
Looking for someone to clean their home
comprehension.answer: Dividing the housework before guests arrive

comprehension.prompt: Which room will Cris clean?
comprehension.answer: The bathroom

comprehension.prompt: What did Cris forget to do?
comprehension.answer: Take the rubbish out

productionPrompt: You share a home with someone. Divide three chores between you and say when you will do your tasks.
retryHint: Use responsible for or my turn for ownership, then give a clear time with I'll...

title: Lesson 49 — Everyday Technology
topic: Devices, messages, passwords, and basic problems
objective: Describe a common technology problem, follow a simple suggestion, and confirm whether it worked.
pronunciationFocus: Stress the problem word: My phone won't turn ON. The battery is almost DEAD.

phrases.en: My phone won't turn on.
phrases.pt: Meu celular não liga.
phrases.note: Use won't when a device refuses or fails to do something.

phrases.en: The battery is almost dead.
phrases.pt: A bateria está quase descarregada.
phrases.note: A dead battery has no power; almost dead means very little power remains.

phrases.en: I can't connect to the Wi-Fi.
phrases.pt: Não consigo me conectar ao Wi-Fi.
phrases.note: Use connect to for a network, device, or online service.

phrases.en: I forgot my password.
phrases.pt: Esqueci minha senha.
phrases.note: Password is the secret word or set of characters used to enter an account.

phrases.en: Could you send me the link again?
phrases.pt: Você poderia me mandar o link de novo?
phrases.note: Again politely signals that the person already sent it once.

phrases.en: Try restarting the device.
phrases.pt: Tente reiniciar o aparelho.
phrases.note: Try doing suggests a possible solution to a problem.

phrases.en: You need to update the app.
phrases.pt: Você precisa atualizar o aplicativo.
phrases.note: Update means install a newer version of software.

phrases.en: It works now, thank you.
phrases.pt: Agora está funcionando, obrigado.
phrases.note: Works means a device, app, or solution functions correctly.

dialogue.speaker: Leo
dialogue.en: I can't connect to the Wi-Fi, and I need to join a call.
dialogue.pt: Não consigo me conectar ao Wi-Fi e preciso entrar em uma chamada.

dialogue.speaker: Sara
dialogue.en: Is the password correct?
dialogue.pt: A senha está correta?

dialogue.speaker: Leo
dialogue.en: I forgot my password, so I used the one on the wall.
dialogue.pt: Esqueci minha senha, então usei a que está na parede.

dialogue.speaker: Sara
dialogue.en: That's the old one. Try restarting the device, then use this password.
dialogue.pt: Essa é a antiga. Tente reiniciar o aparelho e depois use esta senha.

dialogue.speaker: Leo
dialogue.en: It works now, thank you. Could you send me the call link again?
dialogue.pt: Agora está funcionando, obrigado. Você poderia me mandar o link da chamada de novo?

comprehension.prompt: What problem does Leo solve?
comprehension.answer: He connects to the Wi-Fi

comprehension.prompt: Why did the first password fail?
comprehension.answer: It was the old password

comprehension.prompt: What does Leo ask for after the Wi-Fi works?
comprehension.answer: The call link

productionPrompt: Your phone or computer has a simple problem. Describe it, ask for help, and say whether the suggested solution works.
retryHint: Name the exact problem with won't or can't, then respond to one suggestion with Try... or It works now.

title: Lesson 50 — Comparing Options
topic: Comparing people, places, and products
objective: Compare two products using clear differences and choose the better option for a need.
pronunciationFocus: Stress the comparative word: CHEAPer, MORE comfortable, NOT as heavy.

phrases.en: This one is cheaper than that one.
phrases.pt: Este é mais barato do que aquele.
phrases.note: Use than after a comparative adjective to name the other option.

phrases.en: The blue one is more comfortable.
phrases.pt: O azul é mais confortável.
phrases.note: Use more with many longer adjectives such as comfortable and practical.

phrases.en: Which one is better for travel?
phrases.pt: Qual é melhor para viajar?
phrases.note: Better is the irregular comparative form of good.

phrases.en: They're about the same size.
phrases.pt: Eles têm mais ou menos o mesmo tamanho.
phrases.note: About the same means similar, though perhaps not exactly equal.

phrases.en: This model isn't as heavy as the other one.
phrases.pt: Este modelo não é tão pesado quanto o outro.
phrases.note: Use not as ... as to say the first option has less of a quality.

phrases.en: It has a longer battery life.
phrases.pt: A bateria dura mais.
phrases.note: Battery life is the time a device works before it needs charging.

phrases.en: The cheaper one is good enough for me.
phrases.pt: O mais barato é bom o suficiente para mim.
phrases.note: Good enough means it meets your needs even if it is not the best.

phrases.en: I'd rather pay a little more.
phrases.pt: Eu prefiro pagar um pouco mais.
phrases.note: Use would rather to choose one action over another.

dialogue.speaker: Nina
dialogue.en: Which one is better for travel, the grey bag or the blue one?
dialogue.pt: Qual é melhor para viajar, a mala cinza ou a azul?

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.en: They're about the same size, but the grey one is cheaper.
dialogue.pt: Elas têm mais ou menos o mesmo tamanho, mas a cinza é mais barata.

dialogue.speaker: Nina
dialogue.en: The blue one isn't as heavy as the grey one, and it's more comfortable to carry.
dialogue.pt: A azul não é tão pesada quanto a cinza e é mais confortável de carregar.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.en: That's true, but it costs forty pounds more.
dialogue.pt: É verdade, mas ela custa quarenta libras a mais.

dialogue.speaker: Nina
dialogue.en: I'd rather pay a little more because I travel often.
dialogue.pt: Eu prefiro pagar um pouco mais porque viajo com frequência.

comprehension.prompt: What are Nina and Tom deciding?
comprehension.answer: Which travel bag to buy

comprehension.prompt: How are the two bags similar?
options: They are about the same size
comprehension.answer: They are about the same size

comprehension.prompt: Why does Nina prefer the blue bag?
options: It is lighter and more comfortable
comprehension.answer: It is lighter and more comfortable

productionPrompt: Compare two phones, bags, places, or other familiar options. Give two differences and choose one for a specific need.
retryHint: Use one comparative with than and one not as ... as sentence before you state your choice.

title: Lesson 51 — Childhood Memories
topic: Childhood routines and simple memories
objective: Describe where you grew up and share a few simple childhood habits and memories.
pronunciationFocus: Used to links together in speech: I used_to play; Did_you use_to walk?

phrases.en: I grew up in a small town.
phrases.pt: Eu cresci em uma cidade pequena.
phrases.note: Grow up describes the place or conditions of your childhood.

phrases.en: I used to play outside every day.
phrases.pt: Eu costumava brincar fora todos os dias.
phrases.note: Use used to for a past habit or state that is no longer true.

phrases.en: We lived near my grandparents.
phrases.pt: Nós morávamos perto dos meus avós.
phrases.note: Use the past simple for a completed period in your life.

phrases.en: My favourite game was hide-and-seek.
phrases.pt: Minha brincadeira favorita era esconde-esconde.
phrases.note: Hide-and-seek is a game where one person looks for the others.

phrases.en: I started school when I was six.
phrases.pt: Eu comecei a escola quando tinha seis anos.
phrases.note: English uses was, not had, to state your age in the past.

phrases.en: I didn't have a mobile phone.
phrases.pt: Eu não tinha celular.
phrases.note: After didn't, use the base form have, not had.

phrases.en: Did you use to walk to school?
phrases.pt: Você costumava ir a pé para a escola?
phrases.note: In questions after did, write use to without a final d.

phrases.en: Those are some of my best memories.
phrases.pt: Essas são algumas das minhas melhores lembranças.
phrases.note: Use this to close a story by saying the memories are important to you.

dialogue.speaker: Bia
dialogue.en: Where did you grow up, Sam?
dialogue.pt: Onde você cresceu, Sam?

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.en: I grew up in a small town near my grandparents.
dialogue.pt: Eu cresci em uma cidade pequena perto dos meus avós.

dialogue.speaker: Bia
dialogue.en: Did you use to walk to school?
dialogue.pt: Você costumava ir a pé para a escola?

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.en: Yes. After school, I used to play hide-and-seek outside with my cousins.
dialogue.pt: Sim. Depois da escola, eu costumava brincar de esconde-esconde fora com meus primos.

dialogue.speaker: Bia
dialogue.en: That sounds fun. Did you have a mobile phone?
dialogue.pt: Parece divertido. Você tinha celular?

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.en: No, I didn't. Those are some of my best memories.
dialogue.pt: Não, eu não tinha. Essas são algumas das minhas melhores lembranças.

comprehension.prompt: What is Sam describing?
options: His childhood in a small town
His first job in a city
His plans to visit his cousins
comprehension.answer: His childhood in a small town

comprehension.prompt: Who lived near Sam?
comprehension.answer: His grandparents

comprehension.prompt: What did Sam do after school?
options: He played outside with his cousins
He called friends on his phone
He worked in his grandparents' shop
comprehension.answer: He played outside with his cousins

productionPrompt: Describe where you grew up. Share two things you used to do and one thing you did not have or do.
retryHint: Use used to for repeated habits and the past simple for one fact or period.

title: Lesson 52 — Rules and Permissions
topic: Rules with have to, must, and can
objective: Ask about and explain rules, permission, and what is or is not required in a public place.
pronunciationFocus: Contrast must and mustn't clearly; the final t helps the listener hear the rule.

phrases.en: You must show your ID at the entrance.
phrases.pt: Você deve mostrar seu documento na entrada.
phrases.note: Must states a strong rule or requirement.

phrases.en: You have to wear a helmet.
phrases.pt: Você tem que usar capacete.
phrases.note: Have to describes something required by a rule or situation.

phrases.en: You can't park here.
phrases.pt: Você não pode estacionar aqui.
phrases.note: Can't can mean an action is not permitted, not only that it is impossible.

phrases.en: You don't have to book in advance.
phrases.pt: Você não precisa reservar com antecedência.
phrases.note: Don't have to means something is optional; it does not mean it is forbidden.

phrases.en: Are we allowed to take photos?
phrases.pt: É permitido tirar fotos?
phrases.note: Use allowed to to ask or talk about permission.

phrases.en: You need to keep your ticket.
phrases.pt: Você precisa guardar seu ingresso.
phrases.note: Keep means continue to have something because you will need it later.

phrases.en: Visitors must leave by six.
phrases.pt: Os visitantes devem sair até as seis.
phrases.note: By six means no later than six o'clock.

phrases.en: The rules are different at weekends.
phrases.pt: As regras são diferentes nos fins de semana.
phrases.note: In British English, at weekends means during weekends in general.

dialogue.speaker: Guide
dialogue.en: Before we enter, you must show your ID and keep your ticket.
dialogue.pt: Antes de entrarmos, você deve mostrar seu documento e guardar seu ingresso.

dialogue.speaker: Mara
dialogue.en: Do we have to leave our bags here?
dialogue.pt: Temos que deixar nossas bolsas aqui?

dialogue.speaker: Guide
dialogue.en: No, you don't have to, but you can't take food inside.
dialogue.pt: Não, não é preciso, mas não pode entrar com comida.

dialogue.speaker: Mara
dialogue.en: Are we allowed to take photos?
dialogue.pt: É permitido tirar fotos?

dialogue.speaker: Guide
dialogue.en: Yes, but please don't use a flash. Visitors must leave by six.
dialogue.pt: Sim, mas não use flash, por favor. Os visitantes devem sair até as seis.

comprehension.prompt: What is the guide explaining?
options: The rules for visiting a place
The route to a train station
comprehension.answer: The rules for visiting a place

comprehension.prompt: What must visitors show?
comprehension.answer: Their ID

comprehension.prompt: What are visitors allowed to do?
comprehension.answer: Take photos without a flash

productionPrompt: Explain three rules for a museum, workplace, school, or other familiar place. Include one thing that is optional.
retryHint: Use must or have to for a requirement, can't for a prohibition, and don't have to for something optional.

title: Lesson 53 — Reasons and Examples
topic: Explaining a point with reasons and examples
objective: Support an opinion with an organised reason, a relevant example, and a fair limitation.
pronunciationFocus: Pause after signposts such as The main reason is... and For example... so the structure is easy to follow.

phrases.en: The main reason is that it saves time.
phrases.pt: O principal motivo é que isso economiza tempo.
phrases.note: Use this signpost to make your strongest reason clear.

phrases.en: For example, I can work from home twice a week.
phrases.pt: Por exemplo, eu posso trabalhar de casa duas vezes por semana.
phrases.note: Follow a general reason with one specific, believable example.

phrases.en: Another point is that it costs less.
phrases.pt: Outro ponto é que isso custa menos.
phrases.note: Use this to introduce a second reason without repeating also.

phrases.en: This is especially useful for people with children.
phrases.pt: Isso é especialmente útil para quem tem filhos.
phrases.note: Especially narrows a general point to the group it affects most.

phrases.en: In my experience, shorter meetings are more productive.
phrases.pt: Na minha experiência, reuniões mais curtas são mais produtivas.
phrases.note: This frames a claim as personal evidence rather than a universal fact.

phrases.en: That doesn't mean it works for everyone.
phrases.pt: Isso não significa que funcione para todo mundo.
phrases.note: Use this to acknowledge a limitation without abandoning your point.

phrases.en: A good example of this is our Friday check-in.
phrases.pt: Um bom exemplo disso é nossa reunião rápida de sexta-feira.
phrases.note: Name a concrete case that directly demonstrates the previous claim.

phrases.en: That's why I think we should try it.
phrases.pt: Por isso, acho que deveríamos tentar.
phrases.note: Use that's why to connect your evidence to a conclusion or recommendation.

dialogue.speaker: Jo
dialogue.en: I think our team should have fewer long meetings.
dialogue.pt: Acho que nossa equipe deveria ter menos reuniões longas.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.en: Why would that be better?
dialogue.pt: Por que isso seria melhor?

dialogue.speaker: Jo
dialogue.en: The main reason is that it saves time. In my experience, shorter meetings are more productive.
dialogue.pt: O principal motivo é que isso economiza tempo. Na minha experiência, reuniões mais curtas são mais produtivas.

dialogue.speaker: Jo
dialogue.en: A good example of this is our Friday check-in. We make every decision in twenty minutes.
dialogue.pt: Um bom exemplo disso é nossa reunião rápida de sexta-feira. Tomamos todas as decisões em vinte minutos.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.en: That works for us, but some projects need more discussion.
dialogue.pt: Isso funciona para nós, mas alguns projetos precisam de mais discussão.

dialogue.speaker: Jo
dialogue.en: I agree. That doesn't mean it works for everyone. That's why I think we should try it only for weekly updates.
dialogue.pt: Concordo. Isso não significa que funcione para todo mundo. Por isso, acho que deveríamos tentar apenas nas atualizações semanais.

comprehension.prompt: What change does Jo suggest?
options: Using shorter meetings for weekly updates
comprehension.answer: Using shorter meetings for weekly updates

comprehension.prompt: What example supports Jo's point?
options: Their Friday check-in takes twenty minutes
The team works from home twice a week
A client cancelled a long meeting
comprehension.answer: Their Friday check-in takes twenty minutes

comprehension.prompt: What limitation do Jo and Ben recognise?
options: Weekly updates need every team member
comprehension.answer: Some projects need more discussion

productionPrompt: Give your opinion about a change at work, school, or home. Support it with one main reason, one specific example, and one limitation.
retryHint: Make the logic visible: state The main reason..., add For example..., then acknowledge a limit with That doesn't mean...

title: Lesson 54 — Reading the News Carefully
topic: Summarising news and distinguishing fact from opinion
objective: Summarise a news report, identify what is confirmed, and explain why a claim needs checking.
pronunciationFocus: Stress source and certainty words: ACCORDING to the report; it has NOT been confirmed YET.

phrases.en: According to the report, the council is considering the change.
phrases.pt: Segundo a reportagem, a prefeitura está considerando a mudança.
phrases.note: Use according to to attribute information instead of presenting it as your own claim.

phrases.en: The article says that no decision has been made.
phrases.pt: A matéria diz que nenhuma decisão foi tomada.
phrases.note: Use says that to give the main point of a written source.

phrases.en: It has not been confirmed yet.
phrases.pt: Isso ainda não foi confirmado.
phrases.note: This phrase separates an unverified claim from established information.

phrases.en: The source does not provide any evidence.
phrases.pt: A fonte não apresenta nenhuma evidência.
phrases.note: Evidence is information that supports a claim, not simply another opinion.

phrases.en: This is an opinion rather than a fact.
phrases.pt: Isso é uma opinião, e não um fato.
phrases.note: Rather than contrasts two ways of understanding the same statement.

phrases.en: The headline leaves out an important detail.
phrases.pt: A manchete omite um detalhe importante.
phrases.note: Leave out means omit information that may change how readers understand a story.

phrases.en: Several outlets are reporting the same story.
phrases.pt: Vários veículos estão noticiando a mesma história.
phrases.note: A media outlet is an organisation that publishes news, such as a newspaper or broadcaster.

phrases.en: I would check another source before sharing it.
phrases.pt: Eu verificaria outra fonte antes de compartilhar isso.
phrases.note: Use would here to give cautious advice about information that may be unreliable.

dialogue.speaker: Lia
dialogue.en: Have you seen the headline saying the city will charge people to enter the park?
dialogue.pt: Você viu a manchete dizendo que a cidade vai cobrar entrada no parque?

dialogue.speaker: Owen
dialogue.en: Yes, but the headline leaves out an important detail.
dialogue.pt: Sim, mas a manchete omite um detalhe importante.

dialogue.speaker: Owen
dialogue.en: According to the report, the council is considering the change, but no decision has been made.
dialogue.pt: Segundo a reportagem, a prefeitura está considerando a mudança, mas nenhuma decisão foi tomada.

dialogue.speaker: Lia
dialogue.en: So the entrance fee has not been confirmed yet?
dialogue.pt: Então a cobrança de entrada ainda não foi confirmada?

dialogue.speaker: Owen
dialogue.en: Exactly. The official website says there will be a public meeting first. I would check that source before sharing the story.
dialogue.pt: Exatamente. O site oficial diz que primeiro haverá uma reunião pública. Eu verificaria essa fonte antes de compartilhar a notícia.

comprehension.prompt: Why does Owen question the headline?
options: It presents a possible change as a final decision
It describes a meeting that happened last year
It reports the wrong location for the park
comprehension.answer: It presents a possible change as a final decision

comprehension.prompt: What has the council actually done?
options: It has considered charging an entrance fee
It has closed the park permanently
It has cancelled the public meeting
comprehension.answer: It has considered charging an entrance fee

comprehension.prompt: Which source does Owen suggest checking?
comprehension.answer: The official city website

productionPrompt: Summarise a news story or online claim. Attribute the information, say what is confirmed, and name one detail you would check.
retryHint: Separate the source from the claim with According to..., then use confirmed, evidence, or opinion to show how certain it is.

title: Lesson 55 — A Routine You Can Keep
topic: Exercise, wellbeing, and sustainable routines
objective: Describe an exercise routine, discuss a common obstacle, and suggest a realistic way to make the routine sustainable.
pronunciationFocus: Stress the contrast in sustainable advice: start SLOWLY, then build up GRADUALLY.

phrases.en: I've started exercising three times a week.
phrases.pt: Comecei a me exercitar três vezes por semana.
phrases.note: Use the present perfect to connect a recently started routine to the present.

phrases.en: I'm trying to build up my stamina.
phrases.pt: Estou tentando aumentar minha resistência.
phrases.note: Build up means increase something gradually over time.

phrases.en: I need a routine I can stick to.
phrases.pt: Preciso de uma rotina que eu consiga manter.
phrases.note: Stick to a routine means continue following it consistently.

phrases.en: I tend to skip workouts when I'm busy.
phrases.pt: Tenho a tendência de pular os treinos quando estou ocupado.
phrases.note: Tend to describes a behaviour that happens often but not always.

phrases.en: A short walk still makes a difference.
phrases.pt: Uma caminhada curta ainda faz diferença.
phrases.note: Make a difference means have a useful or noticeable effect.

phrases.en: You should increase the intensity gradually.
phrases.pt: Você deve aumentar a intensidade aos poucos.
phrases.note: Should gives general advice; gradually means in small stages.

phrases.en: Rest is part of the training.
phrases.pt: O descanso faz parte do treino.
phrases.note: Use part of to show that recovery belongs within a routine, not outside it.

phrases.en: I've noticed that I sleep better.
phrases.pt: Percebi que durmo melhor.
phrases.note: Notice that introduces a change or effect you have observed.

dialogue.speaker: Maya
dialogue.en: I've started exercising three times a week, but I tend to skip workouts when I'm busy.
dialogue.pt: Comecei a me exercitar três vezes por semana, mas tenho a tendência de pular os treinos quando estou ocupada.

dialogue.speaker: Dan
dialogue.en: What kind of routine are you doing?
dialogue.pt: Que tipo de rotina você está fazendo?

dialogue.speaker: Maya
dialogue.en: I'm trying to build up my stamina, so I run for thirty minutes each time.
dialogue.pt: Estou tentando aumentar minha resistência, então corro trinta minutos de cada vez.

dialogue.speaker: Dan
dialogue.en: On busy days, a short walk still makes a difference. You need a routine you can stick to.
dialogue.pt: Nos dias corridos, uma caminhada curta ainda faz diferença. Você precisa de uma rotina que consiga manter.

dialogue.speaker: Maya
dialogue.en: That's true. Since I started, I've noticed that I sleep better, so I want to keep going.
dialogue.pt: É verdade. Desde que comecei, percebi que durmo melhor, então quero continuar.

comprehension.prompt: What are Maya and Dan trying to improve?
options: How sustainable Maya's exercise routine is
How quickly Maya can enter a competition
How much sports equipment Maya owns
comprehension.answer: How sustainable Maya's exercise routine is

comprehension.prompt: What makes Maya miss workouts?
comprehension.answer: Being busy

comprehension.prompt: What positive change has Maya noticed?
comprehension.answer: She sleeps better

productionPrompt: Describe a realistic wellbeing or exercise routine. Explain one obstacle, one adjustment that makes it easier to maintain, and one effect you have noticed or expect.
retryHint: Use tend to for the obstacle, stick to for sustainability, and one gradual or short alternative instead of making an extreme plan.

title: Lesson 56 — Changing a Habit
topic: Describing change, setbacks, and progress
objective: Compare a past habit with the present, describe a setback, and explain one strategy that supports progress.
pronunciationFocus: Contrast past and present time markers: I USED to check; NOW I leave it outside.

phrases.en: I used to check my phone as soon as I woke up.
phrases.pt: Eu costumava olhar o celular assim que acordava.
phrases.note: Use used to for a repeated past behaviour that has changed.

phrases.en: I've managed to reduce my screen time.
phrases.pt: Consegui reduzir meu tempo de tela.
phrases.note: Manage to emphasises that achieving something required effort.

phrases.en: I'm still working on my evening routine.
phrases.pt: Ainda estou trabalhando na minha rotina noturna.
phrases.note: Work on something means make an effort to improve it over time.

phrases.en: I keep forgetting to turn off notifications.
phrases.pt: Continuo esquecendo de desativar as notificações.
phrases.note: Keep doing describes a repeated action, often one you want to change.

phrases.en: What helped me most was leaving my phone outside the bedroom.
phrases.pt: O que mais me ajudou foi deixar o celular fora do quarto.
phrases.note: Use this pattern to highlight the strategy with the strongest effect.

phrases.en: I had a setback last weekend.
phrases.pt: Tive uma recaída no fim de semana passado.
phrases.note: A setback is a temporary problem that slows progress without ending it.

phrases.en: I'm getting better at noticing the trigger.
phrases.pt: Estou melhorando em perceber o gatilho.
phrases.note: Use get better at to describe gradual improvement in a skill.

phrases.en: The key is to start with one small change.
phrases.pt: O segredo é começar com uma pequena mudança.
phrases.note: The key is to introduces the action you consider most important.

dialogue.speaker: Priya
dialogue.en: I used to check my phone as soon as I woke up, but I've managed to reduce my screen time.
dialogue.pt: Eu costumava olhar o celular assim que acordava, mas consegui reduzir meu tempo de tela.

dialogue.speaker: Cal
dialogue.en: What helped you change the habit?
dialogue.pt: O que ajudou você a mudar o hábito?

dialogue.speaker: Priya
dialogue.en: What helped me most was leaving my phone outside the bedroom.
dialogue.pt: O que mais me ajudou foi deixar o celular fora do quarto.

dialogue.speaker: Priya
dialogue.en: I had a setback last weekend, though. I spent an hour scrolling in bed.
dialogue.pt: Mas tive uma recaída no fim de semana passado. Passei uma hora rolando a tela na cama.

dialogue.speaker: Cal
dialogue.en: One setback doesn't erase your progress. The key is to start again with one small change.
dialogue.pt: Uma recaída não apaga seu progresso. O segredo é recomeçar com uma pequena mudança.

comprehension.prompt: What habit is Priya changing?
options: How she uses her phone around bedtime
How often she exercises at weekends
How she organises her work meetings
comprehension.answer: How she uses her phone around bedtime

comprehension.prompt: Which strategy helped Priya most?
options: Leaving her phone outside the bedroom
comprehension.answer: Leaving her phone outside the bedroom

comprehension.prompt: How does Cal describe Priya's setback?
options: It does not erase her progress
It proves her strategy cannot work
It means she should make a bigger change
comprehension.answer: It does not erase her progress

productionPrompt: Describe a habit you changed or want to change. Compare the past with now, mention a setback or difficulty, and explain one strategy that helps.
retryHint: Show the timeline with used to and now, then make the setback temporary by adding what helped or what you will try next.

title: Lesson 57 — Explaining a Process
topic: Explaining how a familiar process works
objective: Explain a familiar process in a clear sequence, include a condition, and warn about one common problem.
pronunciationFocus: Pause after sequence markers so each stage is clear: FIRST... ONCE that's done... AFTER that...

phrases.en: Before you start, make sure you have the receipt.
phrases.pt: Antes de começar, confira se está com o recibo.
phrases.note: Make sure introduces a necessary check before another action.

phrases.en: The first step is to open the returns page.
phrases.pt: O primeiro passo é abrir a página de devoluções.
phrases.note: Use this signpost to name the action that begins a process.

phrases.en: Once you've selected the item, choose a reason.
phrases.pt: Depois de selecionar o item, escolha um motivo.
phrases.note: Once shows that the second action follows completion of the first.

phrases.en: While the label is downloading, pack the item securely.
phrases.pt: Enquanto a etiqueta estiver baixando, embale o item com segurança.
phrases.note: While connects two actions that happen during the same period.

phrases.en: If something goes wrong, contact customer support.
phrases.pt: Se algo der errado, entre em contato com o atendimento ao cliente.
phrases.note: Use if to add an alternative step for a possible problem.

phrases.en: After that, attach the label to the package.
phrases.pt: Depois disso, cole a etiqueta no pacote.
phrases.note: After that moves the listener to the next completed stage.

phrases.en: The final step is to take it to a collection point.
phrases.pt: O último passo é levar o pacote a um ponto de coleta.
phrases.note: Use final step to signal clearly that the instructions are ending.

phrases.en: The whole process usually takes about ten minutes.
phrases.pt: O processo inteiro geralmente leva cerca de dez minutos.
phrases.note: Use takes to tell the listener the expected duration.

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.en: I've never returned an online order. How does it work?
dialogue.pt: Nunca devolvi uma compra feita pela internet. Como funciona?

dialogue.speaker: Max
dialogue.en: Before you start, make sure you have the receipt. The first step is to open the returns page.
dialogue.pt: Antes de começar, confira se está com o recibo. O primeiro passo é abrir a página de devoluções.

dialogue.speaker: Max
dialogue.en: Once you've selected the item, choose a reason and download the label.
dialogue.pt: Depois de selecionar o item, escolha um motivo e baixe a etiqueta.

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.en: What should I do if the label doesn't appear?
dialogue.pt: O que devo fazer se a etiqueta não aparecer?

dialogue.speaker: Max
dialogue.en: If something goes wrong, contact customer support. Otherwise, attach the label and take the package to a collection point.
dialogue.pt: Se algo der errado, entre em contato com o atendimento ao cliente. Caso contrário, cole a etiqueta e leve o pacote a um ponto de coleta.

comprehension.prompt: Which process does Max explain?
options: Buying an item in a shop
comprehension.answer: Returning an online order

comprehension.prompt: What should Ana do before choosing a reason?
options: Select the item on the returns page
Take the package to a collection point
comprehension.answer: Select the item on the returns page

comprehension.prompt: When should Ana contact customer support?
options: Before she opens the returns page
comprehension.answer: If something goes wrong

productionPrompt: Explain a familiar process such as returning an item, preparing a document, or using a service. Give the stages in order and include what to do if a problem occurs.
retryHint: Guide the listener with first, once, and after that, then add one if sentence for the problem path.

title: Lesson 58 — Goals and Progress
topic: Setting goals and reflecting on progress
objective: Define a specific goal, assess current progress, and choose a practical next step when the plan needs adjustment.
pronunciationFocus: Stress progress contrasts: I'm ON TRACK with research, but I've FALLEN BEHIND with writing.

phrases.en: I want to achieve this goal by the end of June.
phrases.pt: Quero alcançar esta meta até o fim de junho.
phrases.note: By gives the latest time when you intend to complete the goal.

phrases.en: I've broken the goal down into smaller steps.
phrases.pt: Dividi a meta em etapas menores.
phrases.note: Break a goal down means divide it into more manageable actions.

phrases.en: I'm on track with the research.
phrases.pt: Estou dentro do prazo com a pesquisa.
phrases.note: On track means progressing as planned toward a result.

phrases.en: I've fallen behind with the writing.
phrases.pt: Fiquei atrasado com a parte escrita.
phrases.note: Fall behind means make less progress than the schedule requires.

phrases.en: The original deadline is no longer realistic.
phrases.pt: O prazo original não é mais realista.
phrases.note: No longer shows that something was true before but is not true now.

phrases.en: I measure my progress by completed sections.
phrases.pt: Avalio meu progresso pelas seções concluídas.
phrases.note: Measure by names the evidence you use to judge progress.

phrases.en: I need to adjust my plan.
phrases.pt: Preciso ajustar meu plano.
phrases.note: Adjust means make a limited change in response to new information.

phrases.en: My next step is to finish the introduction.
phrases.pt: Meu próximo passo é terminar a introdução.
phrases.note: Use this phrase to turn a general goal into one immediate action.

dialogue.speaker: Ella
dialogue.en: How is your course project going?
dialogue.pt: Como está indo seu projeto do curso?

dialogue.speaker: Ravi
dialogue.en: I'm on track with the research, but I've fallen behind with the writing.
dialogue.pt: Estou dentro do prazo com a pesquisa, mas fiquei atrasado com a parte escrita.

dialogue.speaker: Ella
dialogue.en: How are you measuring your progress?
dialogue.pt: Como você está avaliando seu progresso?

dialogue.speaker: Ravi
dialogue.en: By completed sections. I've broken the goal down into five sections, and two are ready.
dialogue.pt: Pelas seções concluídas. Dividi a meta em cinco seções, e duas estão prontas.

dialogue.speaker: Ella
dialogue.en: Then the original deadline may no longer be realistic. What's your next step?
dialogue.pt: Então talvez o prazo original não seja mais realista. Qual é seu próximo passo?

dialogue.speaker: Ravi
dialogue.en: I need to adjust my plan. My next step is to finish the introduction tomorrow.
dialogue.pt: Preciso ajustar meu plano. Meu próximo passo é terminar a introdução amanhã.

comprehension.prompt: What does Ravi realise about his project plan?
options: He needs to adjust it because the writing is behind
He has completed every section ahead of schedule
He needs to choose a different course
comprehension.answer: He needs to adjust it because the writing is behind

comprehension.prompt: Which part of the project is on track?
comprehension.answer: The research

comprehension.prompt: What is Ravi's immediate next step?
comprehension.answer: Finish the introduction

productionPrompt: Describe one current goal. Explain how you measure progress, say what is on track or behind, and choose one specific next step.
retryHint: Make the goal measurable with a deadline or result, contrast on track with behind, and finish with My next step is to...

title: Lesson 59 — Causes and Consequences
topic: Explaining causes, consequences, and contributing factors
objective: Explain a complex outcome by separating its immediate trigger, contributing factors, and wider consequences.
pronunciationFocus: Use pauses to mark the causal chain: The supplier was late / which, in turn, delayed testing.

phrases.en: The delay was largely due to a shortage of materials.
phrases.pt: O atraso ocorreu principalmente devido à falta de materiais.
phrases.note: Largely due to identifies the main cause without claiming it was the only one.

phrases.en: Several factors contributed to the problem.
phrases.pt: Vários fatores contribuíram para o problema.
phrases.note: Use contribute to when an outcome has more than one cause.

phrases.en: This, in turn, pushed the launch back by a week.
phrases.pt: Isso, por sua vez, adiou o lançamento em uma semana.
phrases.note: In turn connects one result to the next result in a chain.

phrases.en: The change had the unintended effect of increasing costs.
phrases.pt: A mudança teve o efeito não intencional de aumentar os custos.
phrases.note: This pattern highlights a consequence that was not part of the plan.

phrases.en: Poor planning alone doesn't explain what happened.
phrases.pt: Só o planejamento ruim não explica o que aconteceu.
phrases.note: Use alone doesn't explain to reject an overly simple causal account.

phrases.en: There seems to be a link, but we need more evidence.
phrases.pt: Parece haver uma relação, mas precisamos de mais evidências.
phrases.concept: there seems to be a link
phrases.note: This phrase describes a possible connection while keeping the claim cautious.

phrases.en: Had it not been for the backup supplier, the delay would have been longer.
phrases.pt: Se não fosse pelo fornecedor reserva, o atraso teria sido maior.
phrases.note: This formal conditional shows how one factor prevented a worse result.

phrases.en: The power cut was the immediate trigger, not the underlying cause.
phrases.pt: A queda de energia foi o gatilho imediato, não a causa subjacente.
phrases.note: This contrast separates the event that starts a problem from the conditions behind it.

dialogue.speaker: Mara
dialogue.en: Why did the product launch fall behind schedule?
dialogue.pt: Por que o lançamento do produto ficou atrasado?

dialogue.speaker: Jon
dialogue.en: The delay was largely due to a shortage of materials, but several factors contributed to the problem.
dialogue.pt: O atraso ocorreu principalmente devido à falta de materiais, mas vários fatores contribuíram para o problema.

dialogue.speaker: Mara
dialogue.en: So poor planning alone doesn't explain what happened?
dialogue.pt: Então só o planejamento ruim não explica o que aconteceu?

dialogue.speaker: Jon
dialogue.en: No. A power cut stopped production, and this, in turn, pushed testing back by a week.
dialogue.pt: Não. Uma queda de energia interrompeu a produção e isso, por sua vez, adiou os testes em uma semana.

dialogue.speaker: Mara
dialogue.en: Was the power cut the underlying cause?
dialogue.pt: A queda de energia foi a causa subjacente?

dialogue.speaker: Jon
dialogue.en: It was the immediate trigger. The underlying issue was that we had no recovery plan, although the backup supplier prevented a longer delay.
dialogue.pt: Foi o gatilho imediato. O problema subjacente era não termos um plano de recuperação, embora o fornecedor reserva tenha evitado um atraso maior.

comprehension.prompt: How does Jon explain the launch delay?
options: He describes a material shortage, a power cut, and a missing recovery plan
He blames the entire delay on one employee
He says the launch was deliberately postponed
comprehension.answer: He describes a material shortage, a power cut, and a missing recovery plan

comprehension.prompt: What did the power cut immediately affect?
comprehension.answer: Production

comprehension.prompt: What prevented the delay from becoming longer?
comprehension.answer: The backup supplier

productionPrompt: Explain a problem with more than one cause. Identify the immediate trigger, one underlying factor, and at least one consequence.
retryHint: Avoid a single-cause explanation: use largely due to or contributed to, connect the next result with in turn, and distinguish the trigger from the underlying cause.

title: Lesson 60 — Making a Persuasive Case
topic: Persuading without overstating a claim
objective: Build a persuasive recommendation with relevant benefits, measured claims, and a practical response to concerns.
pronunciationFocus: Stress the contrast between claim and evidence: I wouldn't claim it's PERFECT, but the RESULTS are encouraging.

phrases.en: The strongest case for the change is that it saves time.
phrases.pt: O argumento mais forte a favor da mudança é que ela economiza tempo.
phrases.note: Use this to introduce the most persuasive reason for a proposal.

phrases.en: This would benefit both customers and staff.
phrases.pt: Isso beneficiaria tanto os clientes quanto a equipe.
phrases.note: This pattern shows that a proposal serves more than one group.

phrases.en: I wouldn't claim it's perfect, but the results are encouraging.
phrases.pt: Eu não diria que é perfeito, mas os resultados são animadores.
phrases.note: This concession makes a positive claim sound measured rather than exaggerated.

phrases.en: A practical way to address that concern is to run a trial.
phrases.pt: Uma maneira prática de lidar com essa preocupação é realizar um teste.
phrases.note: Address a concern means respond to it with a relevant action or explanation.

phrases.en: Consider what would happen if demand doubled.
phrases.pt: Considere o que aconteceria se a demanda dobrasse.
phrases.note: This invites the listener to examine a consequence instead of simply accepting a claim.

phrases.en: The proposal is worth trying because the risk is limited.
phrases.pt: Vale a pena testar a proposta porque o risco é limitado.
phrases.note: Worth trying recommends an experiment without promising success.

phrases.en: We could start small and evaluate the outcome.
phrases.pt: Poderíamos começar em pequena escala e avaliar o resultado.
phrases.note: This phrase offers a reversible next step when the listener is cautious.

phrases.en: On that basis, I'd recommend a three-month pilot.
phrases.pt: Com base nisso, eu recomendaria um projeto-piloto de três meses.
phrases.note: On that basis connects a recommendation to the evidence already presented.

dialogue.speaker: Nia
dialogue.en: Why should we let the support team work flexible hours?
dialogue.pt: Por que deveríamos permitir que a equipe de suporte trabalhe em horários flexíveis?

dialogue.speaker: Owen
dialogue.en: The strongest case for the change is that it would extend customer coverage without adding staff.
dialogue.pt: O argumento mais forte a favor da mudança é que ela ampliaria o horário de atendimento sem aumentar a equipe.

dialogue.speaker: Nia
dialogue.en: I'm concerned that communication would become harder.
dialogue.pt: Receio que a comunicação fique mais difícil.

dialogue.speaker: Owen
dialogue.en: That's a fair concern. A practical way to address it is to require a two-hour overlap each day.
dialogue.pt: É uma preocupação válida. Uma maneira prática de lidar com ela é exigir duas horas de sobreposição por dia.

dialogue.speaker: Nia
dialogue.en: Do we know that the change would actually help customers?
dialogue.pt: Sabemos se a mudança realmente ajudaria os clientes?

dialogue.speaker: Owen
dialogue.en: I wouldn't claim it's certain, but the early results are encouraging. On that basis, I'd recommend a three-month pilot and then evaluate the outcome.
dialogue.pt: Eu não diria que é certo, mas os primeiros resultados são animadores. Com base nisso, eu recomendaria um projeto-piloto de três meses e depois avaliaria o resultado.

comprehension.prompt: How does Owen make the proposal less risky?
options: He suggests a limited pilot with overlapping hours and an evaluation
He promises that flexible hours will solve every problem
comprehension.answer: He suggests a limited pilot with overlapping hours and an evaluation

comprehension.prompt: What is Owen's strongest argument for flexible hours?
options: They could extend customer coverage without adding staff
They would eliminate the need for customer support
They would reduce every employee's working hours
comprehension.answer: They could extend customer coverage without adding staff

comprehension.prompt: How does Owen respond to the communication concern?
options: He proposes a daily two-hour overlap
He says communication does not matter
comprehension.answer: He proposes a daily two-hour overlap

productionPrompt: Recommend a change at work, school, or in your community. Present its strongest benefit, acknowledge one concern, and propose a low-risk next step.
retryHint: Keep the claim credible: acknowledge the concern, avoid promising certainty, and connect your final recommendation to the evidence with On that basis...

title: Lesson 61 — Keeping a Project on Track
topic: Scope, deadlines, dependencies, and risks
objective: Give a concise project update that clarifies scope, dependencies, schedule pressure, and the action needed to reduce risk.
pronunciationFocus: Stress the project constraint and the response: The DEADLINE is fixed, so we need to REDUCE the scope.

phrases.en: We need to clarify what is in and out of scope.
phrases.pt: Precisamos esclarecer o que está dentro e fora do escopo.
phrases.note: Scope defines which work the project does and does not include.

phrases.en: The design depends on the research being completed first.
phrases.pt: O design depende de a pesquisa ser concluída primeiro.
phrases.note: Use this to state a dependency that controls when another task can begin.

phrases.en: We're at risk of missing the deadline.
phrases.pt: Corremos o risco de perder o prazo.
phrases.note: At risk of identifies a negative outcome that is becoming more likely.

phrases.en: Adding this feature would push the schedule back.
phrases.pt: Acrescentar este recurso atrasaria o cronograma.
phrases.note: Push back means move planned work to a later time.

phrases.en: The deadline is fixed, so we'll need to reduce the scope.
phrases.pt: O prazo é fixo, então precisaremos reduzir o escopo.
phrases.note: This sentence makes the trade-off between time and scope explicit.

phrases.en: Let's assign an owner to each action item.
phrases.pt: Vamos atribuir um responsável a cada ação.
phrases.note: An owner is the person accountable for completing and updating a task.

phrases.en: We should build some contingency into the plan.
phrases.pt: Deveríamos incluir uma margem para imprevistos no plano.
phrases.note: Contingency is extra time or capacity reserved for unexpected problems.

phrases.en: I'll flag the issue before it becomes a blocker.
phrases.pt: Vou sinalizar o problema antes que ele se torne um impedimento.
phrases.note: Flag means draw attention to a concern early enough for others to act.

dialogue.speaker: Leah
dialogue.en: Can we add the reporting dashboard to this release?
dialogue.pt: Podemos acrescentar o painel de relatórios a esta versão?

dialogue.speaker: Davi
dialogue.en: We need to clarify what is in and out of scope. The deadline is fixed, and the dashboard would push the schedule back.
dialogue.pt: Precisamos esclarecer o que está dentro e fora do escopo. O prazo é fixo, e o painel atrasaria o cronograma.

dialogue.speaker: Leah
dialogue.en: Could we finish the dashboard if the research team delivers its findings on Monday?
dialogue.pt: Conseguiríamos terminar o painel se a equipe de pesquisa entregasse os resultados na segunda-feira?

dialogue.speaker: Davi
dialogue.en: The design depends on that research being completed first. Even on Monday, we'd be at risk of missing the deadline.
dialogue.pt: O design depende de essa pesquisa ser concluída primeiro. Mesmo na segunda-feira, correríamos o risco de perder o prazo.

dialogue.speaker: Leah
dialogue.en: Then let's move the dashboard to the next release. How do we protect the current schedule?
dialogue.pt: Então vamos passar o painel para a próxima versão. Como protegemos o cronograma atual?

dialogue.speaker: Davi
dialogue.en: We'll assign an owner to each action item and build two days of contingency into the testing plan.
dialogue.pt: Vamos atribuir um responsável a cada ação e incluir dois dias de margem para imprevistos no plano de testes.

comprehension.prompt: What do Leah and Davi decide about the dashboard?
options: They move it to a later release to protect the deadline
They add it immediately and cancel testing
They replace it with a different research project
comprehension.answer: They move it to a later release to protect the deadline

comprehension.prompt: What must happen before the dashboard can be designed?
options: The release date must be announced
The testing team must hire a manager
comprehension.answer: The research must be completed

comprehension.prompt: What contingency does Davi add?
comprehension.answer: Two extra days for testing

productionPrompt: Give an update on a real or imagined project. Explain one dependency, identify a schedule risk, and recommend how to adjust the scope or plan.
retryHint: Name the constraint clearly, connect the dependency to its impact, and finish with an owner, scope decision, or contingency action.

title: Lesson 62 — Feedback and Expectations
topic: Giving balanced feedback and setting expectations
objective: Give specific, balanced feedback, explain its impact, and agree on a clear standard and follow-up action.
pronunciationFocus: Keep the praise warm and the expectation firm: Your analysis was THOROUGH. Next time, send it by THURSDAY.

phrases.en: One thing you handled particularly well was the analysis.
phrases.pt: Uma coisa que você fez especialmente bem foi a análise.
phrases.note: This opening makes positive feedback specific rather than general.

phrases.en: I noticed that the final update arrived later than agreed.
phrases.pt: Percebi que a atualização final chegou mais tarde do que o combinado.
phrases.note: I noticed that introduces an observable fact without guessing at motives.

phrases.en: That made it difficult for the team to respond in time.
phrases.pt: Isso dificultou que a equipe respondesse a tempo.
phrases.note: This pattern explains the impact of a behaviour on other people or work.

phrases.en: I'd like you to raise the issue sooner next time.
phrases.pt: Gostaria que você sinalizasse o problema mais cedo da próxima vez.
phrases.note: Use this to state a direct but professional expectation for future action.

phrases.en: What support would help you meet that expectation?
phrases.pt: Que apoio ajudaria você a atender a essa expectativa?
phrases.note: This question makes accountability collaborative without removing it.

phrases.en: To be clear, the draft needs to be ready by Thursday.
phrases.pt: Para deixar claro, o rascunho precisa estar pronto até quinta-feira.
phrases.note: To be clear signals that the following standard or deadline is important.

phrases.en: Let's check in on Wednesday before the deadline.
phrases.pt: Vamos conversar na quarta-feira antes do prazo.
phrases.note: A check-in is a short follow-up used to review progress or surface problems.

phrases.en: I'm confident you can make that adjustment.
phrases.pt: Confio que você consegue fazer esse ajuste.
phrases.note: This ending expresses confidence while keeping the agreed expectation in place.

dialogue.speaker: Sofia
dialogue.en: Thanks for preparing the client report, Malik. One thing you handled particularly well was the analysis.
dialogue.pt: Obrigada por preparar o relatório do cliente, Malik. Uma coisa que você fez especialmente bem foi a análise.

dialogue.speaker: Malik
dialogue.en: Thanks. Was there anything I should do differently?
dialogue.pt: Obrigado. Há algo que eu deveria fazer de forma diferente?

dialogue.speaker: Sofia
dialogue.en: I noticed that the final update arrived later than agreed. That made it difficult for the team to respond in time.
dialogue.pt: Percebi que a atualização final chegou mais tarde do que o combinado. Isso dificultou que a equipe respondesse a tempo.

dialogue.speaker: Malik
dialogue.en: I was waiting for figures from finance and didn't flag the delay.
dialogue.pt: Eu estava esperando os números do financeiro e não sinalizei o atraso.

dialogue.speaker: Sofia
dialogue.en: I'd like you to raise the issue sooner next time. What support would help you meet that expectation?
dialogue.pt: Gostaria que você sinalizasse o problema mais cedo da próxima vez. Que apoio ajudaria você a atender a essa expectativa?

dialogue.speaker: Malik
dialogue.en: A Wednesday check-in would help. Then I can warn the team before the Thursday deadline if any figures are missing.
dialogue.pt: Uma conversa na quarta-feira ajudaria. Assim, posso avisar a equipe antes do prazo de quinta-feira se algum número estiver faltando.

comprehension.prompt: What feedback does Sofia give Malik?
options: His analysis was strong, but he needs to communicate delays earlier
His report contained no useful analysis
He should stop working with the finance team
comprehension.answer: His analysis was strong, but he needs to communicate delays earlier

comprehension.prompt: Why did Malik's update arrive late?
options: He was waiting for figures from finance
He sent the report to the wrong team
comprehension.answer: He was waiting for figures from finance

comprehension.prompt: What support do Sofia and Malik agree on?
options: A second person to write the analysis
comprehension.answer: A check-in on Wednesday

productionPrompt: Give feedback on a piece of work. Name one specific strength, describe one behaviour and its impact, then set an expectation and offer useful support.
retryHint: Base the feedback on something observable, connect it to its impact, and make the next expectation concrete with a deadline or follow-up.

title: Lesson 63 — Interpreting Data Carefully
topic: Interpreting charts, changes, and uncertainty
objective: Interpret a chart by describing its main pattern, making a relevant comparison, and qualifying what the data can support.
pronunciationFocus: Use contrastive stress for careful comparisons: Sales ROSE overall, but the final MONTH was flat.

phrases.en: At first glance, the figures show steady growth.
phrases.pt: À primeira vista, os números mostram um crescimento constante.
phrases.note: At first glance introduces an initial reading that may need closer examination.

phrases.en: Compared with the baseline, participation increased by twelve percent.
phrases.pt: Em comparação com o valor inicial, a participação aumentou doze por cento.
phrases.note: A baseline is the starting value used to judge later change.

phrases.en: The overall pattern is positive despite some fluctuation.
phrases.pt: O padrão geral é positivo apesar de alguma oscilação.
phrases.note: This phrase separates the broad direction from smaller rises and falls.

phrases.en: The gap is most noticeable among younger users.
phrases.pt: A diferença é mais perceptível entre os usuários mais jovens.
phrases.concept: the gap is most noticeable among
phrases.note: Use this to identify the group where a difference is clearest.

phrases.en: The final month is an exception to the broader trend.
phrases.pt: O último mês é uma exceção à tendência mais ampla.
phrases.concept: an exception to the broader trend
phrases.note: This highlights a data point that does not follow the main pattern.

phrases.en: These figures suggest a connection, not necessarily a cause.
phrases.pt: Esses números sugerem uma relação, não necessariamente uma causa.
phrases.concept: suggest a connection, not a cause
phrases.note: Use this to avoid treating correlation as proof that one thing caused another.

phrases.en: The sample is too small to support a firm conclusion.
phrases.pt: A amostra é pequena demais para sustentar uma conclusão firme.
phrases.note: This identifies a limitation that reduces confidence in an interpretation.

phrases.en: We would need data from a longer period to confirm the pattern.
phrases.pt: Precisaríamos de dados de um período mais longo para confirmar o padrão.
phrases.note: This states what additional evidence would make the conclusion stronger.

dialogue.speaker: Iris
dialogue.en: What does the chart tell us about the new exercise programme?
dialogue.pt: O que o gráfico nos mostra sobre o novo programa de exercícios?

dialogue.speaker: Theo
dialogue.en: At first glance, the figures show steady growth. Participation increased by twelve percent compared with the baseline.
dialogue.pt: À primeira vista, os números mostram um crescimento constante. A participação aumentou doze por cento em comparação com o valor inicial.

dialogue.speaker: Iris
dialogue.en: Is the increase similar across all age groups?
dialogue.pt: O aumento é semelhante em todas as faixas etárias?

dialogue.speaker: Theo
dialogue.en: No. The gap is most noticeable among younger users, and the final month is an exception to the broader trend.
dialogue.pt: Não. A diferença é mais perceptível entre os usuários mais jovens, e o último mês é uma exceção à tendência mais ampla.

dialogue.speaker: Iris
dialogue.en: Can we conclude that the programme caused the increase?
dialogue.pt: Podemos concluir que o programa causou o aumento?

dialogue.speaker: Theo
dialogue.en: Not yet. The figures suggest a connection, not necessarily a cause, and the sample is too small to support a firm conclusion.
dialogue.pt: Ainda não. Os números sugerem uma relação, não necessariamente uma causa, e a amostra é pequena demais para sustentar uma conclusão firme.

comprehension.prompt: What is Theo's interpretation of the chart?
options: Participation rose, but the data does not prove the programme caused the increase
Participation fell equally in every age group
The programme certainly caused steady growth
comprehension.answer: Participation rose, but the data does not prove the programme caused the increase

comprehension.prompt: How much did participation increase from the baseline?
comprehension.answer: Twelve percent

comprehension.prompt: Which limitation does Theo identify?
options: The age groups are not labelled
comprehension.answer: The sample is too small

productionPrompt: Interpret a chart or imagined dataset. Describe the main pattern, compare it with a baseline or group, identify an exception, and state one limitation.
retryHint: Separate description from conclusion: report the pattern first, add a precise comparison, then qualify the claim with suggest, exception, or a data limitation.

title: Lesson 64 — Running Errands
topic: Simple errands and everyday requests
