objective: Make simple requests while buying, finding, paying for, or collecting everyday items.
pronunciationFocus: Keep polite requests smooth: I'd LIKE this one, PLEASE; Can I PAY by CARD?

phrases.en: I need to go to the pharmacy.
phrases.pt: Preciso ir à farmácia.
phrases.note: Use need to go to when an errand is necessary.

phrases.en: Where can I find batteries?
phrases.pt: Onde posso encontrar pilhas?
phrases.note: Use this question when you cannot see an item in a shop.

phrases.en: I'd like this one, please.
phrases.pt: Eu gostaria deste, por favor.
phrases.note: Point to the item as you say this one.

phrases.en: Can I pay by card?
phrases.pt: Posso pagar com cartão?
phrases.note: Use by card to ask about the payment method.

phrases.en: Do you have a smaller bag?
phrases.pt: Você tem uma sacola menor?
phrases.note: Use Do you have...? to ask whether something is available.

phrases.en: I'm here to collect a package.
phrases.pt: Estou aqui para retirar um pacote.
phrases.note: Use I'm here to followed by the purpose of your visit.

phrases.en: That's everything, thank you.
phrases.pt: É só isso, obrigado.
phrases.note: Use this to show that your order or purchase is complete.

phrases.en: I have one more stop.
phrases.pt: Tenho mais uma parada para fazer.
phrases.note: One more means one additional errand or place.

dialogue.speaker: Clara
dialogue.en: Excuse me. Where can I find batteries?
dialogue.pt: Com licença. Onde posso encontrar pilhas?

dialogue.speaker: Noah
dialogue.en: They're beside the front counter.
dialogue.pt: Elas ficam ao lado do caixa da frente.

dialogue.speaker: Clara
dialogue.en: Great. I'd like this one, please. Can I pay by card?
dialogue.pt: Ótimo. Eu gostaria deste, por favor. Posso pagar com cartão?

dialogue.speaker: Noah
dialogue.en: Yes, you can. Do you need a bag?
dialogue.pt: Sim. Você precisa de uma sacola?

dialogue.speaker: Clara
dialogue.en: No, thanks. That's everything.
dialogue.pt: Não, obrigada. É só isso.

dialogue.speaker: Noah
dialogue.en: All right. The total is six pounds.
dialogue.pt: Certo. O total é seis libras.

comprehension.prompt: What is Clara doing?
options: Returning a bag to a friend
comprehension.answer: Buying batteries in a shop

comprehension.prompt: Where are the batteries?
comprehension.answer: Beside the front counter

comprehension.prompt: How does Clara want to pay?
comprehension.answer: By card

productionPrompt: Imagine two errands you need to do. Say where you need to go, ask for one item, and ask how you can pay.
retryHint: Use I need to go to... for the errand, Where can I find...? for the item, and Can I pay...? at the counter.

title: Lesson 65 — Feelings and Needs
topic: Feelings, preferences, and immediate needs
objective: Name a basic feeling, ask how someone feels, and say what would help right now.
pronunciationFocus: Stress the feeling word: I'm a little TIRED; I'm EXCITED about the trip.

phrases.en: I feel a little tired.
phrases.pt: Estou um pouco cansado.
phrases.note: Use feel plus an adjective to describe your condition.

phrases.en: I'm worried about tomorrow.
phrases.pt: Estou preocupado com amanhã.
phrases.note: Use worried about before the thing causing concern.

phrases.en: I'm excited about the trip.
phrases.pt: Estou animado com a viagem.
phrases.note: Excited describes a strong positive feeling about something coming.

phrases.en: I'm not feeling well.
phrases.pt: Não estou me sentindo bem.
phrases.note: Use this general phrase when you feel ill or unwell.

phrases.en: I need a short break.
phrases.pt: Preciso de uma pausa rápida.
phrases.note: Say this when you need to stop an activity briefly.

phrases.en: I'd rather stay home tonight.
phrases.pt: Prefiro ficar em casa hoje à noite.
phrases.note: Use I'd rather to state your preference between possible plans.

phrases.en: What's wrong?
phrases.pt: O que foi?
phrases.note: Use this when someone looks upset or unwell.

phrases.en: I feel better now.
phrases.pt: Estou me sentindo melhor agora.
phrases.note: Better compares how you feel now with how you felt before.

dialogue.speaker: Lia
dialogue.en: You look quiet today. What's wrong?
dialogue.pt: Você está quieto hoje. O que foi?

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.en: I feel a little tired, and I'm worried about tomorrow's test.
dialogue.pt: Estou um pouco cansado e preocupado com a prova de amanhã.

dialogue.speaker: Lia
dialogue.en: Do you need a short break?
dialogue.pt: Você precisa de uma pausa rápida?

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.en: Yes, please. I'd rather sit outside for a few minutes.
dialogue.pt: Sim, por favor. Prefiro sentar lá fora por alguns minutos.

dialogue.speaker: Lia
dialogue.en: Good idea. We can study again after the break.
dialogue.pt: Boa ideia. Podemos estudar de novo depois da pausa.

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.en: Thanks. I feel better now.
dialogue.pt: Obrigado. Estou me sentindo melhor agora.

comprehension.prompt: Why does Lia talk to Sam?
options: He is excited about a trip
comprehension.answer: He seems tired and worried

comprehension.prompt: What is Sam worried about?
comprehension.answer: Tomorrow's test

comprehension.prompt: What does Sam want to do during the break?
options: Sit outside for a few minutes
comprehension.answer: Sit outside for a few minutes

productionPrompt: Say how you feel today, explain one reason, and say what you need or would rather do right now.
retryHint: Name the feeling with I feel... or I'm..., add the reason with about or because, then state one need or preference.

title: Lesson 66 — Problems at Home
topic: Repairs and common problems at home
objective: Report a problem at home, explain how serious it is, and arrange access for a repair.
pronunciationFocus: Stress the broken item and the problem: The TAP is LEAKING; the HEATING isn't WORKING.

phrases.en: The kitchen tap is leaking.
phrases.pt: A torneira da cozinha está vazando.
phrases.note: Use is leaking for water escaping now from a tap or pipe.

phrases.en: The power keeps going off.
phrases.pt: A energia fica caindo.
phrases.note: Keeps going off means the same problem happens repeatedly.

phrases.en: The heating isn't working.
phrases.pt: O aquecimento não está funcionando.
phrases.note: Use isn't working when a device or system does not operate.

phrases.en: There's a strange noise in the wall.
phrases.pt: Há um barulho estranho na parede.
phrases.note: Use There's... to introduce a problem you can hear, see, or smell.

phrases.en: Could you send someone to look at it?
phrases.pt: Você poderia mandar alguém para verificar?
phrases.note: This polite request asks a landlord or service to arrange a repair visit.

phrases.en: How soon can it be fixed?
phrases.pt: Em quanto tempo isso pode ser consertado?
phrases.note: How soon asks about the earliest possible time.

phrases.en: I'll be home after six.
phrases.pt: Estarei em casa depois das seis.
phrases.note: Use I'll be home to tell the repair person when they can enter.

phrases.en: The problem has got worse.
phrases.pt: O problema piorou.
phrases.note: Use this when a problem is more serious than it was before.

dialogue.speaker: Marta
dialogue.en: Hello. I'm calling because the kitchen tap is leaking.
dialogue.pt: Olá. Estou ligando porque a torneira da cozinha está vazando.

dialogue.speaker: Eli
dialogue.en: When did the problem start?
dialogue.pt: Quando o problema começou?

dialogue.speaker: Marta
dialogue.en: Yesterday, but it has got worse. There's water under the sink now.
dialogue.pt: Ontem, mas piorou. Agora há água embaixo da pia.

dialogue.speaker: Eli
dialogue.en: I'll send someone this afternoon. Will anybody be home?
dialogue.pt: Vou mandar alguém hoje à tarde. Haverá alguém em casa?

dialogue.speaker: Marta
dialogue.en: I'll be home after six. Is that too late?
dialogue.pt: Estarei em casa depois das seis. É tarde demais?

dialogue.speaker: Eli
dialogue.en: No. The repair person can come at half past six.
dialogue.pt: Não. O profissional pode ir às seis e meia.

comprehension.prompt: Why does Marta call Eli?
options: To report a leaking kitchen tap
To ask for a new kitchen
comprehension.answer: To report a leaking kitchen tap

comprehension.prompt: How has the problem changed?
options: It has moved to another room
comprehension.answer: It has got worse

comprehension.prompt: When can the repair person come?
comprehension.answer: At half past six

productionPrompt: Call about a problem in your home. Describe what is wrong, say when it started or changed, and arrange a time for someone to visit.
retryHint: Name the item and exact problem, add when it started or how it changed, then give a clear time when you will be home.

title: Lesson 67 — Celebrations
topic: Invitations, birthdays, and celebrations
objective: Invite someone to a celebration, respond politely, and ask or offer practical details.
pronunciationFocus: Let your voice rise for friendly invitation questions: Would you like to COME? Can I bring ANYTHING?

phrases.en: We're having a birthday dinner on Friday.
phrases.pt: Vamos fazer um jantar de aniversário na sexta-feira.
phrases.note: Use the present continuous for a celebration already arranged.

phrases.en: Would you like to come?
phrases.pt: Você gostaria de vir?
phrases.note: This is a warm, polite way to invite someone.

phrases.en: What time should I arrive?
phrases.pt: A que horas devo chegar?
phrases.note: Use should I to ask the host what is expected.

phrases.en: Can I bring anything?
phrases.pt: Posso levar alguma coisa?
phrases.note: Ask this when you want to help the host with food or supplies.

phrases.en: Thanks for inviting me.
phrases.pt: Obrigado por me convidar.
phrases.note: Use thanks for followed by an -ing form to thank someone for an action.

phrases.en: I'm sorry, I can't come.
phrases.pt: Desculpe, não posso ir.
phrases.note: Start with I'm sorry to make a refusal sound polite.

phrases.en: Congratulations on your graduation!
phrases.pt: Parabéns pela sua formatura!
phrases.note: Use congratulations on before the achievement or special event.

phrases.en: We had such a good time.
phrases.pt: Nós nos divertimos muito.
phrases.note: Use this after an enjoyable event or celebration.

dialogue.speaker: Rosa
dialogue.en: We're having a birthday dinner for Mum on Friday. Would you like to come?
dialogue.pt: Vamos fazer um jantar de aniversário para a mamãe na sexta-feira. Você gostaria de vir?

dialogue.speaker: Jack
dialogue.en: I'd love to. What time should I arrive?
dialogue.pt: Eu adoraria. A que horas devo chegar?

dialogue.speaker: Rosa
dialogue.en: Around seven. We're eating at half past seven.
dialogue.pt: Por volta das sete. Vamos jantar às sete e meia.

dialogue.speaker: Jack
dialogue.en: Can I bring anything?
dialogue.pt: Posso levar alguma coisa?

dialogue.speaker: Rosa
dialogue.en: Could you bring a dessert? Mum loves chocolate cake.
dialogue.pt: Você poderia levar uma sobremesa? A mamãe adora bolo de chocolate.

dialogue.speaker: Jack
dialogue.en: Of course. Thanks for inviting me.
dialogue.pt: Claro. Obrigado por me convidar.

comprehension.prompt: What are Rosa and Jack arranging?
options: A birthday dinner for Rosa's mother
comprehension.answer: A birthday dinner for Rosa's mother

comprehension.prompt: What time will they eat?
comprehension.answer: At half past seven

comprehension.prompt: What does Rosa ask Jack to bring?
comprehension.answer: A dessert

productionPrompt: Invite someone to a birthday, graduation, or other celebration. Give the day and time, then answer one question about what the guest can bring.
retryHint: Use Would you like to...? for the invitation, a clear time expression, and Can I bring...? or Could you bring...? for the practical detail.

title: Lesson 68 — Making Social Plans
topic: Hosting, joining, confirming, and declining social events
objective: Suggest and confirm a social plan, negotiate a small change, or postpone it politely.
pronunciationFocus: Use friendly intonation to soften changes: Could we make it a little LATER? Let's do it another DAY.

phrases.en: Are we still on for Saturday?
phrases.pt: Nosso plano de sábado continua de pé?
phrases.note: Use still on to confirm that an existing plan has not changed.

phrases.en: What do you feel like doing?
phrases.pt: O que você está com vontade de fazer?
phrases.note: Use feel like followed by -ing to ask about a preferred activity.

phrases.en: Why don't we meet at seven?
phrases.pt: Por que não nos encontramos às sete?
phrases.note: Why don't we...? is a common informal suggestion.

phrases.en: Eight works for me.
phrases.pt: Oito horas está ótimo para mim.
phrases.note: Use a time plus works for me to accept a suggested arrangement.

phrases.en: Could we make it a little later?
phrases.pt: Podemos deixar um pouco mais tarde?
phrases.note: Use make it later to negotiate a different time.

phrases.en: I'll let you know by lunchtime.
phrases.pt: Aviso você até a hora do almoço.
phrases.note: By gives the latest time when you will confirm.

phrases.en: I need to change our plan.
phrases.pt: Preciso mudar nosso plano.
phrases.note: Use this direct sentence before you suggest a different time or day.

phrases.en: Let's do it another day.
phrases.pt: Vamos fazer isso outro dia.
phrases.note: This keeps the invitation open when today's plan cannot happen.

dialogue.speaker: Nina
dialogue.en: Are we still on for Saturday?
dialogue.pt: Nosso plano de sábado continua de pé?

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.en: Yes. What do you feel like doing?
dialogue.pt: Sim. O que você está com vontade de fazer?

dialogue.speaker: Nina
dialogue.en: Why don't we try the new café near the station?
dialogue.pt: Por que não conhecemos o café novo perto da estação?

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.en: Good idea. Could we make it a little later, perhaps around eight?
dialogue.pt: Boa ideia. Podemos deixar um pouco mais tarde, talvez por volta das oito?

dialogue.speaker: Nina
dialogue.en: Eight works for me. I'll book a table.
dialogue.pt: Oito horas está ótimo para mim. Vou reservar uma mesa.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.en: Perfect. I'll let you know by lunchtime if anything changes.
dialogue.pt: Perfeito. Aviso você até a hora do almoço se alguma coisa mudar.

comprehension.prompt: What plan do Nina and Ben make?
options: They will visit a new café on Saturday
They will cook dinner at the station
They will postpone the plan until next week
comprehension.answer: They will visit a new café on Saturday

comprehension.prompt: What time will Nina book the table for?
comprehension.answer: Eight o'clock

comprehension.prompt: When will Ben report any change?
comprehension.answer: By lunchtime

productionPrompt: Arrange a social plan with a friend. Suggest an activity and time, ask for one small change, and finish by confirming what happens next.
retryHint: Open with Are we still on...? or Why don't we...?, negotiate with Could we...?, then confirm with a time plus works for me.

title: Lesson 69 — Talking About Cultural Differences
topic: Comparing customs without overgeneralizing
objective: Compare familiar customs, describe personal experience, and avoid presenting cultural tendencies as universal facts.
pronunciationFocus: Stress the qualifier that keeps a comparison careful: In MY experience; SOME people tend to...

phrases.en: In my experience, people usually arrive a little later.
phrases.pt: Na minha experiência, as pessoas geralmente chegam um pouco mais tarde.
phrases.note: Use this to frame a comparison as something you have observed, not a fact about everyone.

phrases.en: It depends on the person and the situation.
phrases.pt: Depende da pessoa e da situação.
phrases.note: This phrase limits a generalization by naming factors that can change the answer.

phrases.en: One difference I noticed was how people greet each other.
phrases.pt: Uma diferença que percebi foi a forma como as pessoas se cumprimentam.
phrases.note: Use this to introduce a specific observation before explaining it.

phrases.en: Some people tend to keep work and family life separate.
phrases.pt: Algumas pessoas tendem a manter o trabalho e a vida familiar separados.
phrases.note: Some and tend to describe a tendency without claiming that it applies to everyone.

phrases.en: I wasn't used to eating dinner that early.
phrases.pt: Eu não estava acostumado a jantar tão cedo.
phrases.note: Be used to plus an -ing form describes something familiar or unfamiliar to you.

phrases.en: What is considered polite can vary from place to place.
phrases.pt: O que é considerado educado pode variar de um lugar para outro.
phrases.concept: can vary from place to place
phrases.note: Use this to explain that a social rule is not the same everywhere.

phrases.en: There are more similarities than I expected.
phrases.pt: Há mais semelhanças do que eu esperava.
phrases.note: This comparison is useful when experience changes an earlier assumption.

phrases.en: I try to ask rather than assume.
phrases.pt: Eu procuro perguntar em vez de presumir.
phrases.note: Rather than contrasts the action you choose with the one you avoid.

dialogue.speaker: Maya
dialogue.en: How are you finding life in Helsinki, André?
dialogue.pt: Como está sendo a vida em Helsinque, André?

dialogue.speaker: André
dialogue.en: I'm enjoying it. One difference I noticed was how people greet each other at work.
dialogue.pt: Estou gostando. Uma diferença que percebi foi a forma como as pessoas se cumprimentam no trabalho.

dialogue.speaker: Maya
dialogue.en: What feels different?
dialogue.pt: O que parece diferente?

dialogue.speaker: André
dialogue.en: In my experience, greetings are quieter and some people tend to keep work and family life separate. But it depends on the person and the situation.
dialogue.pt: Na minha experiência, os cumprimentos são mais discretos e algumas pessoas tendem a manter o trabalho e a vida familiar separados. Mas depende da pessoa e da situação.

dialogue.speaker: Maya
dialogue.en: Was anything easier than you expected?
dialogue.pt: Alguma coisa foi mais fácil do que você esperava?

dialogue.speaker: André
dialogue.en: Yes. There are more similarities than I expected. When I'm unsure what is considered polite, I try to ask rather than assume.
dialogue.pt: Sim. Há mais semelhanças do que eu esperava. Quando não tenho certeza do que é considerado educado, procuro perguntar em vez de presumir.

comprehension.prompt: How does André describe his experience in Helsinki?
options: He notices differences but avoids treating them as rules about everyone
He believes people everywhere behave in exactly the same way
He has decided that workplace customs are impossible to understand
comprehension.answer: He notices differences but avoids treating them as rules about everyone

comprehension.prompt: What difference has André noticed at work?
options: Greetings are quieter and some people separate work from family life
Everyone arrives late and talks about family all day
comprehension.answer: Greetings are quieter and some people separate work from family life

comprehension.prompt: What does André do when he is unsure about politeness?
options: He copies the first person he sees
comprehension.answer: He asks instead of assuming

productionPrompt: Compare one custom you have experienced in two families, workplaces, cities, or countries. Describe a difference and a similarity without saying that everyone behaves the same way.
retryHint: Frame the comparison with In my experience or One difference I noticed, soften tendencies with some or tend to, and finish with a similarity or exception.

title: Lesson 70 — Asking for Community Support
topic: Public services, local issues, and asking for support
objective: Report a local problem to the appropriate service, provide useful details, and ask what action will happen next.
pronunciationFocus: Stress the service problem and location: The STREETLIGHT is OUT near the BUS STOP.

phrases.en: I'm calling to report a problem in my neighbourhood.
phrases.pt: Estou ligando para informar um problema no meu bairro.
phrases.note: Use this opening to state the purpose of a call to a public or community service.

phrases.en: The streetlight has been out for three nights.
phrases.pt: O poste de iluminação está apagado há três noites.
phrases.note: The present perfect plus for shows when an ongoing problem began.

phrases.en: It's on Pine Street, opposite the bus stop.
phrases.pt: Fica na Pine Street, em frente ao ponto de ônibus.
phrases.note: A street name and landmark help the service find the problem quickly.

phrases.en: It makes the crossing difficult to see at night.
phrases.pt: Isso dificulta enxergar a faixa de pedestres à noite.
phrases.note: Use this pattern to explain the practical impact of a local problem.

phrases.en: Which department is responsible for this?
phrases.pt: Qual departamento é responsável por isso?
phrases.note: This question helps you find the service that owns the issue.

phrases.en: Could you give me a reference number?
phrases.pt: Você poderia me dar um número de protocolo?
phrases.note: Ask for a reference number so you can identify the report later.

phrases.en: When should I expect an update?
phrases.pt: Quando devo esperar uma atualização?
phrases.note: Use this to ask for a realistic follow-up time.

phrases.en: I'll follow up if the problem continues.
phrases.pt: Vou entrar em contato novamente se o problema continuar.
phrases.note: Follow up means contact the service again to check progress or add information.

dialogue.speaker: Agent
dialogue.en: City services. How can I help?
dialogue.pt: Serviços municipais. Como posso ajudar?

dialogue.speaker: Camila
dialogue.en: I'm calling to report a problem in my neighbourhood. A streetlight has been out for three nights.
dialogue.pt: Estou ligando para informar um problema no meu bairro. Um poste de iluminação está apagado há três noites.

dialogue.speaker: Agent
dialogue.en: Can you give me the exact location?
dialogue.pt: Você pode me dar a localização exata?

dialogue.speaker: Camila
dialogue.en: It's on Pine Street, opposite the bus stop. It makes the crossing difficult to see at night.
dialogue.pt: Fica na Pine Street, em frente ao ponto de ônibus. Isso dificulta enxergar a faixa de pedestres à noite.

dialogue.speaker: Agent
dialogue.en: The lighting department is responsible. I'll send the report today, and they should inspect it within two working days.
dialogue.pt: O departamento de iluminação é responsável. Vou enviar o chamado hoje, e eles devem fazer uma vistoria em até dois dias úteis.

dialogue.speaker: Camila
dialogue.en: Could you give me a reference number, and when should I expect an update?
dialogue.pt: Você poderia me dar um número de protocolo e dizer quando devo esperar uma atualização?

comprehension.prompt: Why does Camila contact city services?
options: To report a streetlight that is creating a safety problem
To apply for a job in the lighting department
To change the location of a bus stop
comprehension.answer: To report a streetlight that is creating a safety problem

comprehension.prompt: Where is the broken streetlight?
options: On Pine Street opposite the bus stop
comprehension.answer: On Pine Street opposite the bus stop

comprehension.prompt: When should the light be inspected?
comprehension.answer: Within two working days

productionPrompt: Report a real or imagined issue to a community service. Give its exact location and duration, explain its impact, and ask for a reference number or update.
retryHint: Start with I'm calling to report..., locate the issue with a street and landmark, explain why it matters, then ask when you should expect an update.

title: Lesson 71 — Expectations and Boundaries
topic: Expectations, boundaries, and respectful disagreement
objective: State a personal boundary, acknowledge another person's needs, and negotiate a practical agreement without escalating conflict.
pronunciationFocus: Keep boundary statements calm and firm: I NEED some QUIET time after WORK.

phrases.en: I'd like to talk about something that's been bothering me.
phrases.pt: Gostaria de conversar sobre algo que vem me incomodando.
phrases.note: This opening signals a serious conversation without beginning with blame.

phrases.en: I understand that you need time to relax.
phrases.pt: Entendo que você precisa de tempo para relaxar.
phrases.note: Acknowledge the other person's need before explaining your own position.

phrases.en: I need some quiet time after work.
phrases.pt: Preciso de um tempo de silêncio depois do trabalho.
phrases.note: An I statement describes your need directly without accusing the listener.

phrases.en: I'm not comfortable sharing that information.
phrases.pt: Não me sinto à vontade para compartilhar essa informação.
phrases.note: Use this polite but clear boundary when you do not want to discuss or do something.

phrases.en: Could we agree to check first?
phrases.pt: Poderíamos combinar de perguntar antes?
phrases.note: This question turns a boundary into a shared agreement for the future.

phrases.en: That would work for me as long as we plan ahead.
phrases.pt: Isso funcionaria para mim, desde que a gente planeje com antecedência.
phrases.note: As long as introduces the condition that makes a compromise acceptable.

phrases.en: I see it differently, but I want to find a solution.
phrases.pt: Vejo isso de outra forma, mas quero encontrar uma solução.
phrases.note: This phrase expresses disagreement while keeping the conversation cooperative.

phrases.en: Let's try that and talk again next week.
phrases.pt: Vamos tentar isso e conversar novamente na semana que vem.
phrases.note: A short trial and a review point make a new agreement easier to test.

dialogue.speaker: Jordan
dialogue.en: I'd like to talk about something that's been bothering me. When friends visit without warning, I find it hard to rest after work.
dialogue.pt: Gostaria de conversar sobre algo que vem me incomodando. Quando amigos aparecem sem avisar, acho difícil descansar depois do trabalho.

dialogue.speaker: Rafa
dialogue.en: I didn't realise it was a problem. I enjoy having people around.
dialogue.pt: Eu não tinha percebido que isso era um problema. Gosto de ter gente por perto.

dialogue.speaker: Jordan
dialogue.en: I understand that you need time with friends. I need some quiet time after work, too.
dialogue.pt: Entendo que você precisa de tempo com os amigos. Eu também preciso de um tempo de silêncio depois do trabalho.

dialogue.speaker: Rafa
dialogue.en: Could we agree to check first before inviting anyone on a weekday?
dialogue.pt: Poderíamos combinar de perguntar antes de convidar alguém durante a semana?

dialogue.speaker: Jordan
dialogue.en: That would work for me as long as we plan ahead. Weekends can be more flexible.
dialogue.pt: Isso funcionaria para mim, desde que a gente planeje com antecedência. Nos fins de semana podemos ser mais flexíveis.

dialogue.speaker: Rafa
dialogue.en: Great. Let's try that and talk again next week.
dialogue.pt: Ótimo. Vamos tentar isso e conversar novamente na semana que vem.

comprehension.prompt: What agreement do Jordan and Rafa make?
options: They will check with each other before weekday visitors and review the plan
They will never invite friends to their home again
They will move to different homes next week
comprehension.answer: They will check with each other before weekday visitors and review the plan

comprehension.prompt: Why do unexpected visits bother Jordan?
options: Jordan needs quiet time after work
Jordan dislikes all of Rafa's friends
comprehension.answer: Jordan needs quiet time after work

comprehension.prompt: When can their arrangement be more flexible?
comprehension.answer: On weekends

productionPrompt: Describe a small conflict between friends, relatives, colleagues, or housemates. State one need, acknowledge the other person's view, and propose an agreement with a condition.
retryHint: Open without blame, use I need or I'm not comfortable for the boundary, acknowledge the other view, and propose Could we agree to...? plus a clear condition.

title: Lesson 72 — Weighing an Ethical Dilemma
topic: Weighing principles and practical consequences
objective: Analyse an ethical dilemma by identifying competing duties, affected groups, likely consequences, and a defensible course of action.
pronunciationFocus: Use contrastive stress for competing duties: We should protect PRIVACY, but we also owe the public TRANSPARENCY.

phrases.en: We have a duty to protect people's privacy.
phrases.pt: Temos o dever de proteger a privacidade das pessoas.
phrases.note: Use duty to name a moral or professional obligation.

phrases.en: At the same time, the public deserves an explanation.
phrases.pt: Ao mesmo tempo, o público merece uma explicação.
phrases.note: This connector introduces a competing principle that must also be considered.

phrases.en: The decision could affect people who had no choice in the matter.
phrases.pt: A decisão pode afetar pessoas que não tiveram escolha nessa questão.
phrases.concept: have no choice in the matter
phrases.note: This phrase draws attention to people who bear consequences without consenting.

phrases.en: The short-term benefit may come at a wider social cost.
phrases.pt: O benefício de curto prazo pode vir acompanhado de um custo social mais amplo.
phrases.note: Come at a cost shows that a benefit creates a significant negative consequence elsewhere.

phrases.en: We should distinguish what is legal from what is fair.
phrases.pt: Devemos distinguir o que é legal do que é justo.
phrases.note: Use this when two standards overlap but are not identical.

phrases.en: A reasonable compromise would limit access to the data.
phrases.pt: Um meio-termo razoável seria limitar o acesso aos dados.
phrases.note: This phrase introduces an option that protects part of each competing value.

phrases.en: That safeguard would reduce the risk of misuse.
phrases.pt: Essa medida de proteção reduziria o risco de uso indevido.
phrases.note: A safeguard is a rule or control designed to prevent harm.

phrases.en: On balance, this option does the least harm.
phrases.pt: Considerando tudo, esta opção causa o menor dano.
phrases.note: Use on balance for a conclusion reached after considering benefits, duties, and risks together.

dialogue.speaker: Imani
dialogue.en: The health department wants to publish detailed location data from the outbreak study.
dialogue.pt: O departamento de saúde quer publicar dados detalhados de localização do estudo sobre o surto.

dialogue.speaker: Noah
dialogue.en: The public deserves an explanation, but we have a duty to protect participants' privacy.
dialogue.pt: O público merece uma explicação, mas temos o dever de proteger a privacidade dos participantes.

dialogue.speaker: Imani
dialogue.en: The data could help neighbourhood clinics prepare. Isn't that a strong public benefit?
dialogue.pt: Os dados podem ajudar as clínicas dos bairros a se preparar. Isso não é um benefício público importante?

dialogue.speaker: Noah
dialogue.en: It is, but participants had no choice in the matter. Detailed addresses could expose individuals, so the benefit may come at a wider social cost.
dialogue.pt: É, mas os participantes não tiveram escolha nessa questão. Endereços detalhados podem expor indivíduos, então o benefício pode vir acompanhado de um custo social mais amplo.

dialogue.speaker: Imani
dialogue.en: What would a reasonable compromise look like?
dialogue.pt: Como seria um meio-termo razoável?

dialogue.speaker: Noah
dialogue.en: We could publish totals by district and let approved researchers access the detailed data. That safeguard would reduce the risk of misuse.
dialogue.pt: Poderíamos publicar totais por distrito e permitir que pesquisadores autorizados acessem os dados detalhados. Essa medida de proteção reduziria o risco de uso indevido.

comprehension.prompt: What compromise do Imani and Noah consider?
options: Publish district totals while restricting access to detailed data
Publish every participant's address without restrictions
Destroy the study and provide no public information
comprehension.answer: Publish district totals while restricting access to detailed data

comprehension.prompt: What public benefit could the data provide?
options: It could help neighbourhood clinics prepare
It could replace all local health services
It could guarantee that no outbreak happens again
comprehension.answer: It could help neighbourhood clinics prepare

comprehension.prompt: What harm does Noah want to prevent?
options: Individuals being exposed through detailed addresses
comprehension.answer: Individuals being exposed through detailed addresses

productionPrompt: Analyse an ethical choice at work, in technology, health, education, or public life. Name two competing duties, identify who could be harmed, and recommend a safeguard or compromise.
retryHint: Set up the tension with We have a duty to... and At the same time..., separate what is legal from what is fair, then justify your choice with a consequence and safeguard.

title: Lesson 73 — Making Remote Work Work
topic: Collaboration, autonomy, and communication trade-offs
objective: Evaluate a remote-work arrangement, balance autonomy with coordination needs, and propose explicit communication practices.
pronunciationFocus: Contrast flexibility with coordination: People need AUTONOMY, but the team still needs OVERLAP.

phrases.en: Remote work gives people more control over their day.
phrases.pt: O trabalho remoto dá às pessoas mais controle sobre o próprio dia.
phrases.note: Use this to describe autonomy over time, place, or working conditions.

phrases.en: That flexibility can make coordination more demanding.
phrases.pt: Essa flexibilidade pode tornar a coordenação mais exigente.
phrases.note: This pattern states a trade-off without rejecting the original benefit.

phrases.en: We need a few shared hours for real-time collaboration.
phrases.pt: Precisamos de algumas horas em comum para a colaboração em tempo real.
phrases.note: Shared hours are periods when distributed colleagues are available at the same time.

phrases.en: Not every update needs to become a meeting.
phrases.pt: Nem toda atualização precisa virar uma reunião.
phrases.note: Use this to challenge an inefficient default while leaving room for exceptions.

phrases.en: Important decisions should be documented in writing.
phrases.pt: Decisões importantes devem ser documentadas por escrito.
phrases.note: Written decisions keep information available across schedules and locations.

phrases.en: People can feel left out when information stays informal.
phrases.pt: As pessoas podem se sentir excluídas quando a informação fica apenas em conversas informais.
phrases.note: Feel left out means not being included in a group, conversation, or decision.

phrases.en: Managers should focus on outcomes rather than online presence.
phrases.pt: Gestores devem se concentrar em resultados, e não na presença on-line.
phrases.concept: focus on A rather than B
phrases.note: This contrast shifts attention from visible activity to completed work.

phrases.en: Let's review the arrangement after a six-week trial.
phrases.pt: Vamos avaliar o acordo depois de um período de teste de seis semanas.
phrases.note: A trial period creates a clear point for evaluating evidence and adjusting the arrangement.

dialogue.speaker: Priya
dialogue.en: The team likes working remotely, but some people say they hear about decisions too late.
dialogue.pt: A equipe gosta de trabalhar remotamente, mas algumas pessoas dizem que ficam sabendo das decisões tarde demais.

dialogue.speaker: Lucas
dialogue.en: Remote work gives people more control over their day, but that flexibility can make coordination more demanding.
dialogue.pt: O trabalho remoto dá às pessoas mais controle sobre o próprio dia, mas essa flexibilidade pode tornar a coordenação mais exigente.

dialogue.speaker: Priya
dialogue.en: Should we add a daily meeting so everyone gets the same information?
dialogue.pt: Deveríamos acrescentar uma reunião diária para que todos recebam a mesma informação?

dialogue.speaker: Lucas
dialogue.en: Not every update needs to become a meeting. We could set three shared hours and document important decisions in writing.
dialogue.pt: Nem toda atualização precisa virar uma reunião. Poderíamos definir três horas em comum e documentar as decisões importantes por escrito.

dialogue.speaker: Priya
dialogue.en: That would help people in other time zones. Managers would also need to focus on outcomes rather than online presence.
dialogue.pt: Isso ajudaria as pessoas em outros fusos horários. Os gestores também precisariam se concentrar em resultados, e não na presença on-line.

dialogue.speaker: Lucas
dialogue.en: Agreed. Let's review the arrangement after a six-week trial and ask whether anyone still feels left out.
dialogue.pt: De acordo. Vamos avaliar o acordo depois de um período de teste de seis semanas e perguntar se alguém ainda se sente excluído.

comprehension.prompt: What remote-work arrangement do Priya and Lucas propose?
options: Shared collaboration hours, written decisions, and a six-week review
A return to the office every day with no written updates
No shared hours and no communication expectations
comprehension.answer: Shared collaboration hours, written decisions, and a six-week review

comprehension.prompt: Why does Lucas reject a daily meeting for every update?
options: Not every update requires real-time discussion
The team is not allowed to hold online meetings
comprehension.answer: Not every update requires real-time discussion

comprehension.prompt: What should managers evaluate instead of online presence?
comprehension.answer: Outcomes

productionPrompt: Evaluate a remote or hybrid arrangement you know or can imagine. Explain one benefit and one coordination risk, then propose communication rules and a review point.
retryHint: State the trade-off with gives people... but can make..., distinguish meetings from written updates, and finish with a measurable trial or review.

title: Lesson 74 — Reading Beyond the Frame
topic: Framing, evidence selection, and source reliability
objective: Compare how two reports frame the same event, test their claims against the evidence, and give a neutral summary.
pronunciationFocus: Use contrastive stress to expose framing choices: The plan was DELAYED, not ABANDONED.

phrases.en: The headline frames the decision as a failure.
phrases.pt: A manchete apresenta a decisão como um fracasso.
phrases.note: Frame as describes the perspective a report encourages readers to adopt.

phrases.en: This report leaves out an important detail.
phrases.pt: Esta reportagem deixa de fora um detalhe importante.
phrases.note: Leave out means omit information, whether deliberately or not.

phrases.en: Both outlets cite the same figures.
phrases.pt: Os dois veículos citam os mesmos números.
phrases.note: An outlet is an organisation that publishes news or commentary.

phrases.en: The evidence supports a narrower claim.
phrases.pt: As evidências sustentam uma afirmação mais limitada.
phrases.note: A narrower claim says less and stays closer to what the evidence can establish.

phrases.en: The source has a clear interest in the outcome.
phrases.pt: A fonte tem um interesse claro no resultado.
phrases.note: A source's interests can affect its perspective without automatically making its information false.

phrases.en: We should distinguish bias from an error.
phrases.pt: Devemos diferenciar viés de erro.
phrases.note: Use distinguish from when two explanations need different evidence or responses.

phrases.en: That wording invites the reader to assume the worst.
phrases.pt: Essa escolha de palavras leva o leitor a presumir o pior.
phrases.note: Invite here means subtly encourage a reaction rather than request it directly.

phrases.en: A more neutral summary would mention both results.
phrases.pt: Um resumo mais neutro mencionaria os dois resultados.
phrases.note: Use would to propose wording that reduces unsupported judgement.

dialogue.speaker: Livia
dialogue.en: One headline says the transport pilot failed, while the other says it produced mixed results.
dialogue.pt: Uma manchete diz que o projeto-piloto de transporte fracassou, enquanto a outra diz que ele teve resultados mistos.

dialogue.speaker: Omar
dialogue.en: Both outlets cite the same figures: journey times improved, but the project exceeded its budget.
dialogue.pt: Os dois veículos citam os mesmos números: o tempo das viagens melhorou, mas o projeto ultrapassou o orçamento.

dialogue.speaker: Livia
dialogue.en: The first report leaves out the shorter journey times and frames the decision as a failure.
dialogue.pt: A primeira reportagem deixa de fora a redução no tempo das viagens e apresenta a decisão como um fracasso.

dialogue.speaker: Omar
dialogue.en: The second report also has a weakness. Its main source helped design the pilot and has a clear interest in the outcome.
dialogue.pt: A segunda reportagem também tem um ponto fraco. Sua principal fonte ajudou a projetar o piloto e tem um interesse claro no resultado.

dialogue.speaker: Livia
dialogue.en: So the evidence supports a narrower claim: the service improved, but at a higher cost than planned.
dialogue.pt: Então, as evidências sustentam uma afirmação mais limitada: o serviço melhorou, mas com um custo maior que o planejado.

dialogue.speaker: Omar
dialogue.en: Exactly. That is more neutral than calling the whole pilot either a success or a failure.
dialogue.pt: Exatamente. Isso é mais neutro do que chamar o projeto-piloto inteiro de sucesso ou fracasso.

comprehension.prompt: What do Livia and Omar conclude about the transport pilot?
options: It improved the service but cost more than planned
It failed in every measured area
It stayed under budget without changing journey times
comprehension.answer: It improved the service but cost more than planned

comprehension.prompt: What does the first report leave out?
options: The name of the transport service
The fact that the pilot had a budget
comprehension.answer: The improvement in journey times

comprehension.prompt: Why do they treat the second report's main source cautiously?
options: The source helped design the pilot
The source refuses to cite any figures
The source works for the first newspaper
comprehension.answer: The source helped design the pilot

productionPrompt: Compare two headlines or accounts of the same event. Identify one framing choice or omitted detail, evaluate the source, and give a more neutral summary.
retryHint: Name the frame with frames... as..., identify what the report leaves out, then limit your conclusion to what the evidence supports.

title: Lesson 75 — Reasoning Under Uncertainty
topic: Speculation, probability, and calibrated confidence
objective: Discuss competing explanations with calibrated confidence, state what could change your view, and avoid presenting speculation as fact.
pronunciationFocus: Stress probability markers to calibrate a claim: it's LIKELY, but not CERTAIN.

phrases.en: Based on the current evidence, a delay is likely.
phrases.pt: Com base nas evidências atuais, é provável que haja um atraso.
phrases.note: This opening limits a prediction to the information available now.

phrases.en: There's a strong chance that demand was underestimated.
phrases.pt: Há uma grande chance de a demanda ter sido subestimada.
phrases.note: A strong chance expresses high probability without claiming certainty.

phrases.en: That remains a plausible explanation, but it is not confirmed.
phrases.pt: Essa continua sendo uma explicação plausível, mas não está confirmada.
phrases.note: Plausible means reasonably possible, not proven.

phrases.en: We can't rule out a problem with the supplier.
phrases.pt: Não podemos descartar um problema com o fornecedor.
phrases.note: Can't rule out keeps an explanation open when there is not enough evidence to reject it.

phrases.en: I'd be cautious about drawing a firm conclusion.
phrases.pt: Eu teria cautela antes de chegar a uma conclusão definitiva.
phrases.note: This phrase recommends restraint without dismissing the available evidence.

phrases.en: If this assumption is wrong, the forecast changes completely.
phrases.pt: Se essa suposição estiver errada, a previsão muda completamente.
phrases.note: State the consequence of a weak assumption to show how robust a conclusion is.

phrases.en: My confidence is moderate rather than high.
phrases.pt: Meu grau de confiança é moderado, e não alto.
phrases.note: Naming a confidence level makes the strength of your judgement explicit.

phrases.en: We should update our view as new data comes in.
phrases.pt: Devemos atualizar nossa avaliação à medida que novos dados chegarem.
phrases.note: A provisional judgement should change when relevant evidence changes.

dialogue.speaker: Mei
dialogue.en: The launch team says the new product may be delayed because the supplier is behind schedule.
dialogue.pt: A equipe de lançamento diz que o novo produto pode atrasar porque o fornecedor está com o cronograma atrasado.

dialogue.speaker: Dan
dialogue.en: That remains a plausible explanation, but it is not confirmed. We can't rule out an internal testing problem.
dialogue.pt: Essa continua sendo uma explicação plausível, mas não está confirmada. Não podemos descartar um problema nos testes internos.

dialogue.speaker: Mei
dialogue.en: Based on the current evidence, how likely is a delay?
dialogue.pt: Com base nas evidências atuais, qual é a probabilidade de atraso?

dialogue.speaker: Dan
dialogue.en: I would say moderately likely. The forecast assumes testing will finish on Friday.
dialogue.pt: Eu diria que é moderadamente provável. A previsão pressupõe que os testes terminarão na sexta-feira.

dialogue.speaker: Mei
dialogue.en: If that assumption is wrong, the forecast changes completely. What would increase your confidence?
dialogue.pt: Se essa suposição estiver errada, a previsão muda completamente. O que aumentaria seu grau de confiança?

dialogue.speaker: Dan
dialogue.en: The final test results and a confirmed delivery date. We should update our view when those come in.
dialogue.pt: Os resultados finais dos testes e uma data de entrega confirmada. Devemos atualizar nossa avaliação quando essas informações chegarem.

comprehension.prompt: How do Mei and Dan assess the possible launch delay?
options: It is moderately likely, but key evidence is still missing
It is certain because the supplier admitted responsibility
It is impossible because internal testing is complete
comprehension.answer: It is moderately likely, but key evidence is still missing

comprehension.prompt: What alternative explanation does Dan keep open?
options: A change in the product's price
A mistake in the marketing campaign
comprehension.answer: A problem with internal testing

comprehension.prompt: What evidence would increase Dan's confidence?
options: Final test results and a confirmed delivery date
An informal promise from the launch team
comprehension.answer: Final test results and a confirmed delivery date

productionPrompt: Assess an uncertain outcome at work, in study, or in daily life. Compare two explanations, state your confidence, and name evidence that would change your view.
retryHint: Mark uncertainty with likely, plausible, or can't rule out; identify one assumption, then say what new evidence would update your view.

title: Lesson 76 — Disagreeing with Precision
topic: Challenging assumptions with diplomatic precision
objective: Challenge an assumption precisely while recognising shared goals and proposing a constructive way to test the disagreement.
pronunciationFocus: Soften the opening, then stress the exact point of disagreement: I see the LOGIC, but not the ASSUMPTION.

phrases.en: I see the logic, but I'm not convinced by the assumption behind it.
phrases.pt: Entendo a lógica, mas a premissa por trás dela não me convence.
phrases.note: Recognising the reasoning before challenging it keeps the disagreement specific.

phrases.en: Could we examine the assumption that demand will remain stable?
phrases.pt: Poderíamos examinar a premissa de que a demanda permanecerá estável?
phrases.note: A question invites joint analysis without hiding the point you are challenging.

phrases.en: That conclusion may be premature.
phrases.pt: Essa conclusão pode ser prematura.
phrases.note: Premature means reached before enough evidence or time is available.

phrases.en: I wonder whether we're giving enough weight to the operational risk.
phrases.pt: Será que estamos dando peso suficiente ao risco operacional?
phrases.note: Give weight to means treat a factor as important in a judgement.

phrases.en: There may be a distinction between speed and readiness.
phrases.pt: Talvez seja preciso distinguir velocidade de prontidão.
phrases.note: Introduce a distinction when two ideas are being treated as equivalent but should not be.

phrases.en: I'd frame the issue slightly differently.
phrases.pt: Eu formularia a questão de uma maneira um pouco diferente.
phrases.note: This signals a change of perspective before you explain it clearly.

phrases.en: Would you be open to testing the proposal on a smaller scale?
phrases.pt: Você consideraria testar a proposta em uma escala menor?
phrases.note: Offer a test when evidence can resolve a disagreement more effectively than further assertion.

phrases.en: We may agree on the goal while differing on the timing.
phrases.pt: Podemos concordar quanto ao objetivo e divergir quanto ao momento.
phrases.note: This separates shared intent from a genuine disagreement about method or timing.

dialogue.speaker: Rina
dialogue.en: If we expand customer support into three markets at once, we can establish ourselves before our competitors do.
dialogue.pt: Se expandirmos o atendimento ao cliente para três mercados ao mesmo tempo, podemos nos estabelecer antes dos concorrentes.

dialogue.speaker: Elias
dialogue.en: I see the logic, but I'm not convinced by the assumption that our current support model will scale smoothly.
dialogue.pt: Entendo a lógica, mas não me convence a premissa de que nosso modelo atual de atendimento ganhará escala sem dificuldades.

dialogue.speaker: Rina
dialogue.en: We handled the last increase in volume without adding staff.
dialogue.pt: Lidamos com o último aumento de volume sem ampliar a equipe.

dialogue.speaker: Elias
dialogue.en: True, although there may be a distinction between higher volume in one language and supporting three new markets.
dialogue.pt: É verdade, embora talvez seja preciso distinguir um volume maior em um idioma do atendimento a três novos mercados.

dialogue.speaker: Rina
dialogue.en: So you agree with the expansion but question the timing?
dialogue.pt: Então você concorda com a expansão, mas questiona o momento?

dialogue.speaker: Elias
dialogue.en: Exactly. Would you be open to testing one market first and defining the support capacity we need before the other two?
dialogue.pt: Exatamente. Você consideraria testar um mercado primeiro e definir a capacidade de atendimento necessária antes dos outros dois?

comprehension.prompt: What does Elias propose instead of entering three markets at once?
options: Testing one market before expanding to the other two
Entering all three markets without customer support
comprehension.answer: Testing one market before expanding to the other two

comprehension.prompt: Which assumption does Elias challenge?
options: The current support model will scale smoothly
Competitors are already in all three markets
The company needs fewer support languages
comprehension.answer: The current support model will scale smoothly

comprehension.prompt: Where do Rina and Elias still agree?
options: No additional support capacity is necessary
All three markets have identical needs
comprehension.answer: The company should expand internationally

productionPrompt: Respond to a proposal you partly disagree with. Recognise its logic, identify the exact assumption or distinction you challenge, and propose a test or alternative.
retryHint: Open with shared ground, challenge one named assumption with but or although, and finish with Would you be open to...?

title: Lesson 77 — Handling Difficult Questions
topic: Handling difficult questions after a presentation
objective: Answer a challenging presentation question directly, clarify the scope of the evidence, acknowledge limits, and commit to an accurate follow-up.
pronunciationFocus: Pause after the direct answer, then signpost the qualification: YES. However, that figure refers SPECIFICALLY to....

phrases.en: That's an important challenge to the proposal.
phrases.pt: Esse é um questionamento importante à proposta.
phrases.note: Acknowledge a difficult question without using the phrase to avoid answering it.

phrases.en: The short answer is yes, with one qualification.
phrases.pt: A resposta curta é sim, com uma ressalva.
phrases.note: Give the answer before adding the condition that makes it accurate.

phrases.en: Let me separate what we know from what we expect.
phrases.pt: Vou separar o que sabemos do que esperamos.
phrases.note: Use this signpost to prevent forecasts from sounding like observed results.

phrases.en: That figure refers specifically to the first-quarter pilot.
phrases.pt: Esse número se refere especificamente ao projeto-piloto do primeiro trimestre.
phrases.note: Clarify the population, period, or measure a figure actually describes.

phrases.en: I don't have that breakdown with me.
phrases.pt: Não estou com esse detalhamento em mãos.
phrases.note: A precise limit is more credible than guessing or claiming not to know anything.

phrases.en: I'd rather verify that than speculate.
phrases.pt: Prefiro verificar isso a especular.
phrases.note: Use this when an accurate follow-up is more valuable than an immediate guess.

phrases.en: The main limitation is the size of the sample.
phrases.pt: A principal limitação é o tamanho da amostra.
phrases.note: Name the limitation and its effect instead of adding a vague disclaimer.

phrases.en: Does that address the concern behind your question?
phrases.pt: Isso responde à preocupação por trás da sua pergunta?
phrases.note: This checks whether your answer resolved the issue, not merely whether you spoke long enough.

dialogue.speaker: Audience
dialogue.en: You predict a fifteen percent saving, but did the pilot include the smaller regional offices?
dialogue.pt: Você prevê uma economia de quinze por cento, mas o projeto-piloto incluiu os escritórios regionais menores?

dialogue.speaker: Presenter
dialogue.en: That's an important challenge to the proposal. The short answer is no.
dialogue.pt: Esse é um questionamento importante à proposta. A resposta curta é não.

dialogue.speaker: Presenter
dialogue.en: Let me separate what we know from what we expect. The fifteen percent figure refers specifically to the first-quarter pilot in two large offices.
dialogue.pt: Vou separar o que sabemos do que esperamos. O número de quinze por cento se refere especificamente ao projeto-piloto do primeiro trimestre em dois escritórios grandes.

dialogue.speaker: Audience
dialogue.en: Then what saving do you expect in the regional offices?
dialogue.pt: Então que economia você espera nos escritórios regionais?

dialogue.speaker: Presenter
dialogue.en: I don't have that breakdown with me, and I'd rather verify it than speculate. The main limitation is the size and location of the sample.
dialogue.pt: Não estou com esse detalhamento em mãos e prefiro verificá-lo a especular. A principal limitação é o tamanho e a localização da amostra.

dialogue.speaker: Presenter
dialogue.en: I'll send the regional estimate and its assumptions tomorrow. Does that address the concern behind your question?
dialogue.pt: Enviarei amanhã a estimativa regional e suas premissas. Isso responde à preocupação por trás da sua pergunta?

comprehension.prompt: How does the presenter handle the question about regional offices?
options: She limits the claim, admits missing detail, and promises a verified follow-up
She applies the fifteen percent figure to every office without qualification
She rejects the question because it was not submitted in advance
comprehension.answer: She limits the claim, admits missing detail, and promises a verified follow-up

comprehension.prompt: What does the fifteen percent figure describe?
options: A first-quarter pilot in two large offices
Every regional office over a full year
A forecast with no observed data
comprehension.answer: A first-quarter pilot in two large offices

comprehension.prompt: What follow-up does the presenter promise?
options: A regional estimate with its assumptions
A new pilot completed by tomorrow
The names of everyone in the sample
comprehension.answer: A regional estimate with its assumptions

productionPrompt: Answer a difficult question about a proposal or result. Give the direct answer first, define the evidence's scope, acknowledge one limitation, and offer a precise follow-up if needed.
retryHint: Lead with The short answer is..., clarify what the figure refers to, and replace any guess with I'd rather verify that than speculate.

title: Lesson 78 — Aligning Competing Priorities
topic: Aligning stakeholders with conflicting priorities
objective: Surface the constraints behind conflicting stakeholder positions, protect shared outcomes, and negotiate a phased decision with explicit unresolved points.
pronunciationFocus: Use parallel stress to make a trade-off audible: protect RELIABILITY without delaying LEARNING.

phrases.en: We're aligned on the outcome, but not yet on the sequence.
phrases.pt: Estamos alinhados quanto ao resultado, mas ainda não quanto à sequência.
phrases.note: Separate agreement on the destination from disagreement about how to reach it.

phrases.en: Each group is optimising for a different constraint.
phrases.pt: Cada grupo está priorizando uma restrição diferente.
phrases.note: This describes the pressure shaping a position without reducing it to personal preference.

phrases.en: Let's make the trade-off explicit.
phrases.pt: Vamos explicitar a escolha envolvida.
phrases.note: An explicit trade-off shows what each option gains and gives up.

phrases.en: What would have to be true for you to support this?
phrases.pt: O que precisaria ser verdade para você apoiar isso?
phrases.note: Ask for conditions to turn a fixed position into testable requirements.

phrases.en: Which part is non-negotiable from your perspective?
phrases.pt: Qual parte é inegociável do seu ponto de vista?
phrases.note: A genuine non-negotiable should be distinguished from a preference or opening position.

phrases.en: We can protect reliability without committing to a full rollout.
phrases.pt: Podemos preservar a confiabilidade sem nos comprometer com uma implementação completa.
phrases.note: This pattern looks for a smaller commitment that preserves an essential concern.

phrases.en: A phased decision gives us room to adjust.
phrases.pt: Uma decisão em etapas nos dá espaço para ajustar o rumo.
phrases.note: A phased decision sequences commitments so later steps can use evidence from earlier ones.

phrases.en: I'll document where we agree and what remains unresolved.
phrases.pt: Vou documentar os pontos de acordo e o que continua sem solução.
phrases.note: Recording unresolved points prevents apparent consensus from becoming a later conflict.

dialogue.speaker: Facilitator
dialogue.en: Product wants to launch next month, while Operations wants another quarter of reliability testing.
dialogue.pt: A equipe de Produto quer lançar no mês que vem, enquanto Operações quer mais um trimestre de testes de confiabilidade.

dialogue.speaker: Product
dialogue.en: If we wait a quarter, we lose the chance to learn from customers before our busiest season.
dialogue.pt: Se esperarmos um trimestre, perderemos a oportunidade de aprender com os clientes antes da nossa época de maior movimento.

dialogue.speaker: Operations
dialogue.en: Our non-negotiable is that a failure must not interrupt the existing service.
dialogue.pt: Para nós, é inegociável que uma falha não interrompa o serviço existente.

dialogue.speaker: Facilitator
dialogue.en: We're aligned on the outcome, but not yet on the sequence. Each group is optimising for a different constraint.
dialogue.pt: Estamos alinhados quanto ao resultado, mas ainda não quanto à sequência. Cada grupo está priorizando uma restrição diferente.

dialogue.speaker: Facilitator
dialogue.en: Could we protect reliability with a limited launch that is isolated from the existing service?
dialogue.pt: Poderíamos preservar a confiabilidade com um lançamento limitado e isolado do serviço existente?

dialogue.speaker: Operations
dialogue.en: Yes, if we set a strict customer limit and an automatic rollback. A phased decision would give us room to adjust.
dialogue.pt: Sim, se definirmos um limite rigoroso de clientes e uma reversão automática. Uma decisão em etapas nos daria espaço para ajustar o rumo.

dialogue.speaker: Product
dialogue.en: That works for us if the first phase starts next month and we review the evidence after two weeks.
dialogue.pt: Isso funciona para nós se a primeira etapa começar no mês que vem e avaliarmos as evidências depois de duas semanas.

comprehension.prompt: What compromise do the stakeholders reach?
options: A limited launch with safeguards and a two-week review
A full launch next month without additional controls
A permanent delay with no customer testing
comprehension.answer: A limited launch with safeguards and a two-week review

comprehension.prompt: What is Operations' non-negotiable condition?
options: The existing service must not be interrupted
The first phase must include every customer
Product must cancel the launch completely
comprehension.answer: The existing service must not be interrupted

comprehension.prompt: Why does Product resist waiting a full quarter?
options: It wants customer learning before the busiest season
It has already promised a full launch to Operations
It believes reliability testing has no value
comprehension.answer: It wants customer learning before the busiest season

productionPrompt: Mediate a disagreement between two groups with different priorities. Name each constraint, identify a non-negotiable, and propose a phased decision that preserves the shared outcome.
retryHint: Separate outcome from sequence, ask what would have to be true for support, and document the safeguard, phase, and review point.

title: Lesson 79 — Making a Strong Proposal
topic: Presenting and defending a structured proposal
objective: Present a structured proposal with a clear rationale, practical safeguards, and a specific decision request.
pronunciationFocus: Use signposting pauses to reveal the structure: the GOAL / the EVIDENCE / and the SAFEGUARD.

phrases.en: The proposal has three parts.
phrases.pt: A proposta tem três partes.
phrases.note: Announce the structure before giving detail so listeners can follow a longer proposal.

phrases.en: The case for this change rests on recent customer data.
phrases.pt: A justificativa para essa mudança se baseia em dados recentes de clientes.
phrases.note: Use rests on to identify the evidence or reasoning that supports a proposal.

phrases.en: The expected benefit is a shorter response time.
phrases.pt: O benefício esperado é um tempo de resposta menor.
phrases.note: Expected keeps a projected benefit distinct from a result that has already been proven.

phrases.en: A practical constraint is the team's current workload.
phrases.pt: Uma restrição prática é a carga de trabalho atual da equipe.
phrases.note: A credible proposal states the limits it must work within rather than hiding them.

phrases.en: To reduce that risk, we could begin with a small trial.
phrases.pt: Para reduzir esse risco, poderíamos começar com um teste pequeno.
phrases.note: Connect a safeguard directly to the risk it is designed to reduce.

phrases.en: The alternative would require more time and deliver less evidence.
phrases.pt: A alternativa exigiria mais tempo e produziria menos evidências.
phrases.note: Compare options using the same criteria instead of dismissing an alternative vaguely.

phrases.en: I'm asking for approval to run the trial for six weeks.
phrases.pt: Estou pedindo aprovação para realizar o teste durante seis semanas.
phrases.note: End with a concrete request so the audience knows what decision is needed.

phrases.en: What concerns would prevent you from supporting this approach?
phrases.pt: Que preocupações impediriam você de apoiar essa abordagem?
phrases.note: This question invites specific objections that can be examined or addressed.

dialogue.speaker: Nora
dialogue.en: The proposal has three parts: a six-week trial, a limited customer group, and a review before any wider launch.
dialogue.pt: A proposta tem três partes: um teste de seis semanas, um grupo limitado de clientes e uma avaliação antes de qualquer lançamento mais amplo.

dialogue.speaker: Victor
dialogue.en: What is the case for changing the support schedule now?
dialogue.pt: Qual é a justificativa para mudar o horário de atendimento agora?

dialogue.speaker: Nora
dialogue.en: It rests on recent customer data. A third of our urgent requests arrive after the team has finished for the day.
dialogue.pt: Ela se baseia em dados recentes de clientes. Um terço das solicitações urgentes chega depois que a equipe encerra o expediente.

dialogue.speaker: Victor
dialogue.en: The team's current workload is a practical constraint. Extending the schedule could increase overtime.
dialogue.pt: A carga de trabalho atual da equipe é uma restrição prática. Ampliar o horário poderia aumentar as horas extras.

dialogue.speaker: Nora
dialogue.en: To reduce that risk, we could begin with volunteers from two teams and cap the trial at fifty customers.
dialogue.pt: Para reduzir esse risco, poderíamos começar com voluntários de duas equipes e limitar o teste a cinquenta clientes.

dialogue.speaker: Victor
dialogue.en: That safeguard helps. What decision do you need today?
dialogue.pt: Essa salvaguarda ajuda. De que decisão você precisa hoje?

dialogue.speaker: Nora
dialogue.en: I'm asking for approval to run the trial for six weeks and report on response time, overtime, and customer satisfaction.
dialogue.pt: Estou pedindo aprovação para realizar o teste durante seis semanas e apresentar os resultados sobre tempo de resposta, horas extras e satisfação dos clientes.

comprehension.prompt: What is Nora proposing?
options: A limited six-week trial of a different support schedule
A permanent schedule change for the entire company
A reduction in support hours for urgent requests
comprehension.answer: A limited six-week trial of a different support schedule

comprehension.prompt: What evidence supports the proposal?
options: Many urgent requests arrive after the team finishes
Every employee has requested additional hours
comprehension.answer: Many urgent requests arrive after the team finishes

comprehension.prompt: How does Nora address the overtime risk?
options: She limits the trial to volunteers and fifty customers
She removes overtime from the review criteria
She delays the trial until workload data is unavailable
comprehension.answer: She limits the trial to volunteers and fifty customers

productionPrompt: Present a proposal for a change at work, in study, or in your community. Structure the idea, support it with evidence, name one constraint and safeguard, and ask for a specific decision.
retryHint: Signpost the parts, connect the expected benefit to evidence, address one risk with To reduce that risk..., and finish with I'm asking for....

title: Lesson 80 — Disagreeing and Moving Forward
topic: Disagreeing clearly while preserving cooperation
objective: Express a professional disagreement clearly, locate the source of the difference, and agree on evidence that can move the discussion forward.
pronunciationFocus: Keep the acknowledgement calm, then stress the contrast: I agree with the GOAL, but not the APPROACH.

phrases.en: I agree with the objective, but not with the proposed approach.
phrases.pt: Concordo com o objetivo, mas não com a abordagem proposta.
phrases.note: This makes the scope of disagreement clear while preserving genuine common ground.

phrases.en: I interpret the evidence differently.
phrases.pt: Eu interpreto as evidências de outra forma.
phrases.note: Use this when the disagreement concerns what shared information means, not whether it exists.

phrases.en: My concern is less about cost than about reliability.
phrases.pt: Minha preocupação é menos com o custo e mais com a confiabilidade.
phrases.note: This contrast corrects a misunderstanding about the main reason for your position.

phrases.en: Could you walk me through how you reached that conclusion?
phrases.pt: Você poderia me explicar como chegou a essa conclusão?
phrases.note: Ask for the reasoning before assuming that the other person's position is careless.

phrases.en: That would hold true if demand stayed constant.
phrases.pt: Isso continuaria válido se a demanda permanecesse constante.
phrases.note: Name the condition that must be true for an argument or prediction to remain valid.

phrases.en: We may be talking past each other.
phrases.pt: Talvez estejamos falando de coisas diferentes sem perceber.
phrases.note: This signals that the speakers may be answering different questions or using terms differently.

phrases.en: Where I think we differ is the acceptable level of risk.
phrases.pt: Acho que nossa divergência está no nível de risco aceitável.
phrases.note: Summarise the disagreement in neutral terms that both sides can confirm or correct.

phrases.en: Can we agree on the criteria before choosing an option?
phrases.pt: Podemos concordar quanto aos critérios antes de escolher uma opção?
phrases.note: Shared criteria can turn competing preferences into a decision that both sides can evaluate.

dialogue.speaker: Amira
dialogue.en: I agree with the objective of reducing delivery time, but not with the proposed approach of changing suppliers immediately.
dialogue.pt: Concordo com o objetivo de reduzir o prazo de entrega, mas não com a abordagem proposta de trocar de fornecedor imediatamente.

dialogue.speaker: Jonas
dialogue.en: Our current supplier was late four times this quarter. Why keep the contract?
dialogue.pt: Nosso fornecedor atual atrasou quatro vezes neste trimestre. Por que manter o contrato?

dialogue.speaker: Amira
dialogue.en: I interpret the evidence differently. Three delays followed late design changes from our side.
dialogue.pt: Eu interpreto as evidências de outra forma. Três atrasos aconteceram depois de mudanças tardias no projeto feitas por nós.

dialogue.speaker: Jonas
dialogue.en: So your concern is that a new supplier would not solve the underlying process problem?
dialogue.pt: Então, sua preocupação é que um novo fornecedor não resolveria o problema subjacente do processo?

dialogue.speaker: Amira
dialogue.en: Exactly. My concern is less about cost than about reliability during the transition.
dialogue.pt: Exatamente. Minha preocupação é menos com o custo e mais com a confiabilidade durante a transição.

dialogue.speaker: Jonas
dialogue.en: We may be talking past each other. I want a reliable service too, but I think the current delays are unacceptable.
dialogue.pt: Talvez estejamos falando de coisas diferentes sem perceber. Eu também quero um serviço confiável, mas acho os atrasos atuais inaceitáveis.

dialogue.speaker: Amira
dialogue.en: Can we agree on reliability, transition risk, and total cost as the criteria, then compare both suppliers next week?
dialogue.pt: Podemos concordar em usar confiabilidade, risco de transição e custo total como critérios e então comparar os dois fornecedores na semana que vem?

comprehension.prompt: How do Amira and Jonas move their disagreement forward?
options: They agree on criteria for comparing the suppliers
They cancel both supplier contracts immediately
They decide that delivery time no longer matters
comprehension.answer: They agree on criteria for comparing the suppliers

comprehension.prompt: Why does Amira interpret the delay evidence differently?
options: Most delays followed late changes from their own team
The supplier delivered every order on time
The quarterly records do not include delivery dates
comprehension.answer: Most delays followed late changes from their own team

comprehension.prompt: What is Amira mainly concerned about?
options: The colour of the supplier's packaging
Jonas presenting the comparison next week
comprehension.answer: Reliability during a supplier transition

productionPrompt: Respond to a professional decision you disagree with. Acknowledge the shared objective, explain exactly where your interpretation differs, and propose common criteria or evidence for the next decision.
retryHint: Separate the objective from the approach, ask how the conclusion was reached, and summarise the exact difference before proposing shared criteria.

title: Lesson 81 — Finding the Root Cause
topic: Diagnosing problems beyond immediate symptoms
objective: Distinguish triggers, contributing factors, and root causes in order to recommend a lasting response to a recurring problem.
pronunciationFocus: Stress the diagnostic contrast: that explains WHEN it failed, not WHY it was possible.

phrases.en: The immediate trigger was a failed software update.
phrases.pt: O gatilho imediato foi uma atualização de software que falhou.
phrases.note: A trigger starts the visible incident but may not explain why the system was vulnerable.

phrases.en: That explains when it failed, not why it was possible.
phrases.pt: Isso explica quando ocorreu a falha, não por que ela foi possível.
phrases.note: Use this contrast when a timeline is being mistaken for a complete diagnosis.

phrases.en: We need to look beyond the most visible symptom.
phrases.pt: Precisamos olhar além do sintoma mais visível.
phrases.note: A symptom is evidence of a problem, but removing it may not prevent recurrence.

phrases.en: A recurring pattern suggests a weakness in the process.
phrases.pt: Um padrão recorrente indica uma fragilidade no processo.
phrases.note: Repeated incidents often reveal a systemic condition rather than isolated bad luck.

phrases.en: What conditions made this failure possible?
phrases.pt: Que condições tornaram essa falha possível?
phrases.note: This question broadens the analysis from a person's final action to the surrounding system.

phrases.en: The underlying issue appears to be unclear ownership.
phrases.pt: O problema subjacente parece ser a falta de clareza sobre as responsabilidades.
phrases.note: Appears to be makes a diagnosis clear while leaving room for further evidence.

phrases.en: Let's separate contributing factors from the root cause.
phrases.pt: Vamos separar os fatores contribuintes da causa-raiz.
phrases.note: Several factors can worsen an incident even when one deeper condition makes recurrence likely.

phrases.en: A lasting fix would address the approval process.
phrases.pt: Uma solução duradoura corrigiria o processo de aprovação.
phrases.note: Connect the proposed response to the diagnosed cause, not only to the latest symptom.

dialogue.speaker: Priya
dialogue.en: The website went offline after a failed software update, so the update was the immediate trigger.
dialogue.pt: O site saiu do ar depois de uma atualização de software que falhou, então a atualização foi o gatilho imediato.

dialogue.speaker: Marco
dialogue.en: We could simply block that version and remind the engineer to be more careful.
dialogue.pt: Poderíamos simplesmente bloquear essa versão e lembrar o engenheiro de ter mais cuidado.

dialogue.speaker: Priya
dialogue.en: That explains when it failed, not why it was possible. This is the third unreviewed update in two months.
dialogue.pt: Isso explica quando ocorreu a falha, não por que ela foi possível. Esta é a terceira atualização sem revisão em dois meses.

dialogue.speaker: Marco
dialogue.en: A recurring pattern suggests a weakness in the process. What conditions made this failure possible?
dialogue.pt: Um padrão recorrente indica uma fragilidade no processo. Que condições tornaram essa falha possível?

dialogue.speaker: Priya
dialogue.en: The release owner was away, and nobody else knew who could approve an urgent update.
dialogue.pt: O responsável pela publicação estava ausente, e ninguém mais sabia quem poderia aprovar uma atualização urgente.

dialogue.speaker: Marco
dialogue.en: Then the underlying issue appears to be unclear ownership, while time pressure was a contributing factor.
dialogue.pt: Então, o problema subjacente parece ser a falta de clareza sobre as responsabilidades, enquanto a pressão de tempo foi um fator contribuinte.

dialogue.speaker: Priya
dialogue.en: Agreed. A lasting fix would name a backup approver and prevent any update without a recorded review.
dialogue.pt: Concordo. Uma solução duradoura nomearia um aprovador substituto e impediria qualquer atualização sem uma revisão registrada.

comprehension.prompt: What do Priya and Marco identify as the underlying issue?
options: Unclear ownership of urgent update approvals
A website that cannot receive software updates
An engineer who refuses to record incidents
comprehension.answer: Unclear ownership of urgent update approvals

comprehension.prompt: What pattern suggests that the problem is not isolated?
options: Three updates went unreviewed in two months
The website has been online for three years
Three engineers approved the same update
comprehension.answer: Three updates went unreviewed in two months

comprehension.prompt: What lasting fix do they recommend?
options: Naming a backup approver and requiring recorded review
Asking one engineer to work without holidays
comprehension.answer: Naming a backup approver and requiring recorded review

productionPrompt: Analyse a recurring problem from work, study, or daily life. Distinguish the visible symptom, immediate trigger, contributing factors, and likely root cause, then recommend a lasting fix.
retryHint: Ask what made the failure possible, use appears to be for the diagnosis, and make sure the lasting fix addresses that cause rather than only the symptom.

title: Lesson 82 — Comparing Competing Views
topic: Summarizing and comparing competing positions
objective: Summarise competing positions fairly, expose their assumptions and shared ground, and reach a qualified evidence-based judgement.
pronunciationFocus: Use balanced stress for fair comparison: supporters point to ACCESS; critics emphasise COST.

phrases.en: One side argues that the change would improve access.
phrases.pt: Um lado argumenta que a mudança melhoraria o acesso.
phrases.note: Report a position without adopting it by naming whose argument it is.

phrases.en: Supporters point to the results of a recent trial.
phrases.pt: Os defensores apontam para os resultados de um teste recente.
phrases.note: Point to introduces the evidence a group uses to support its position.

phrases.en: Critics counter that the trial was too limited.
phrases.pt: Os críticos respondem que o teste foi limitado demais.
phrases.note: Counter introduces a direct response to another argument rather than an unrelated objection.

phrases.en: Both positions assume that demand will continue to grow.
phrases.pt: As duas posições pressupõem que a demanda continuará crescendo.
phrases.note: Competing positions can depend on the same assumption even when their recommendations differ.

phrases.en: The key point of disagreement is how the costs should be shared.
phrases.pt: O principal ponto de divergência é como os custos devem ser divididos.
phrases.note: Reduce a broad debate to the central question the sides answer differently.
