phrases.en: The two views overlap more than they first appear.
phrases.pt: As duas visões têm mais pontos em comum do que parece à primeira vista.
phrases.note: Use overlap to identify shared goals, facts, or concerns without erasing real disagreement.

phrases.en: The evidence is stronger on short-term effects than long-term outcomes.
phrases.pt: As evidências são mais sólidas quanto aos efeitos de curto prazo do que aos resultados de longo prazo.
phrases.note: Specify which part of a claim has stronger support instead of rating all evidence together.

phrases.en: On balance, a revised trial seems more justified than a full rollout.
phrases.pt: Considerando todos os fatores, um teste revisado parece mais justificável do que uma implementação completa.
phrases.note: On balance introduces a judgement made after weighing evidence and competing concerns.

dialogue.speaker: Helen
dialogue.en: Supporters of free weekend buses argue that the change would improve access to the city centre.
dialogue.pt: Os defensores dos ônibus gratuitos nos fins de semana argumentam que a mudança melhoraria o acesso ao centro da cidade.

dialogue.speaker: Rafael
dialogue.en: They point to a recent trial in which passenger numbers rose by eighteen percent.
dialogue.pt: Eles apontam para um teste recente em que o número de passageiros aumentou dezoito por cento.

dialogue.speaker: Helen
dialogue.en: Critics counter that the trial lasted only two weekends and did not measure the cost of extra services.
dialogue.pt: Os críticos respondem que o teste durou apenas dois fins de semana e não mediu o custo dos serviços extras.

dialogue.speaker: Rafael
dialogue.en: The two views overlap more than they first appear. Both sides want better access and a financially sustainable service.
dialogue.pt: As duas visões têm mais pontos em comum do que parece à primeira vista. Os dois lados querem melhor acesso e um serviço financeiramente sustentável.

dialogue.speaker: Helen
dialogue.en: The key point of disagreement is whether increased activity in the city centre would cover the additional cost.
dialogue.pt: O principal ponto de divergência é se o aumento da atividade no centro da cidade cobriria o custo adicional.

dialogue.speaker: Rafael
dialogue.en: The evidence is stronger on short-term passenger numbers than long-term economic effects.
dialogue.pt: As evidências são mais sólidas quanto ao número de passageiros no curto prazo do que aos efeitos econômicos de longo prazo.

dialogue.speaker: Helen
dialogue.en: On balance, a longer trial with full cost data seems more justified than either rejecting the idea or rolling it out now.
dialogue.pt: Considerando todos os fatores, um teste mais longo com dados completos de custo parece mais justificável do que rejeitar a ideia ou implementá-la agora.

comprehension.prompt: What conclusion do Helen and Rafael reach?
options: A longer trial should gather passenger and cost data
Free weekend buses should begin permanently tomorrow
The city should abandon all public transport trials
comprehension.answer: A longer trial should gather passenger and cost data

comprehension.prompt: What evidence do supporters use?
options: Passenger numbers rose during a recent trial
The city centre eliminated all transport costs
The trial measured ten years of economic activity
comprehension.answer: Passenger numbers rose during a recent trial

comprehension.prompt: What is the key unresolved question?
options: Whether increased activity would cover the extra cost
Whether passengers know where the city centre is
Whether both sides want public transport to disappear
comprehension.answer: Whether increased activity would cover the extra cost

productionPrompt: Compare two competing views on a public, professional, or everyday issue. Present each side's evidence fairly, identify shared ground and the key disagreement, and give a qualified judgement.
retryHint: Use Supporters point to... and Critics counter..., name one shared assumption or goal, then qualify your judgement with On balance....

title: Lesson 83 — Communicating Through a Crisis
topic: Communicating uncertainty and action under pressure
objective: Communicate verified facts, uncertainty, immediate action, and the next update under pressure without minimising harm or encouraging speculation.
pronunciationFocus: Use a firm fall for verified facts and a measured rise for uncertainty: we CAN confirm this; we are still ESTABLISHING that.

phrases.en: We can confirm that the service interruption began at nine fifteen.
phrases.pt: Podemos confirmar que a interrupção do serviço começou às nove e quinze.
phrases.note: Reserve can confirm for information that has been checked and can be stated publicly.

phrases.en: What we know at this stage is that one region is affected.
phrases.pt: O que sabemos neste momento é que uma região foi afetada.
phrases.concept: limit a statement to current knowledge
phrases.note: At this stage makes the time boundary of an evolving factual statement explicit.

phrases.en: We are still establishing whether customer data was exposed.
phrases.pt: Ainda estamos verificando se houve exposição de dados de clientes.
phrases.note: Name the important unknown directly and describe the work under way to resolve it.

phrases.en: Our immediate priority is to restore access safely.
phrases.pt: Nossa prioridade imediata é restabelecer o acesso com segurança.
phrases.note: A clear priority helps people understand what the response team is doing first and why.

phrases.en: As a precaution, we have suspended password changes.
phrases.pt: Como medida de precaução, suspendemos as alterações de senha.
phrases.note: Identify precautionary action without implying that the feared outcome has been confirmed.

phrases.en: I don't want to speculate about the cause before the review is complete.
phrases.pt: Não quero especular sobre a causa antes que a análise seja concluída.
phrases.note: Refuse unsupported conclusions while showing when or how better information will become available.

phrases.en: We will provide the next update by eleven, even if the situation is unchanged.
phrases.pt: Forneceremos a próxima atualização até as onze, mesmo que a situação não tenha mudado.
phrases.note: A time-bound update reduces the information vacuum that often fuels rumours.

phrases.en: If the circumstances change, we will contact affected customers directly.
phrases.pt: Se as circunstâncias mudarem, entraremos em contato diretamente com os clientes afetados.
phrases.note: Explain what would trigger further action without promising that the trigger will occur.

dialogue.speaker: Reporter
dialogue.en: Can you confirm when the payment service failed and how many customers are affected?
dialogue.pt: Você pode confirmar quando o serviço de pagamentos falhou e quantos clientes foram afetados?

dialogue.speaker: Spokesperson
dialogue.en: We can confirm that the interruption began at nine fifteen. What we know at this stage is that one region is affected.
dialogue.pt: Podemos confirmar que a interrupção começou às nove e quinze. O que sabemos neste momento é que uma região foi afetada.

dialogue.speaker: Reporter
dialogue.en: Was customer data exposed, and was a cyberattack responsible?
dialogue.pt: Houve exposição de dados de clientes, e um ataque cibernético foi o responsável?

dialogue.speaker: Spokesperson
dialogue.en: We are still establishing whether any data was exposed. I don't want to speculate about the cause before the technical review is complete.
dialogue.pt: Ainda estamos verificando se houve exposição de dados. Não quero especular sobre a causa antes que a análise técnica seja concluída.

dialogue.speaker: Reporter
dialogue.en: What action are you taking now?
dialogue.pt: Que medidas vocês estão tomando agora?

dialogue.speaker: Spokesperson
dialogue.en: Our immediate priority is to restore access safely. As a precaution, we have suspended password changes while the team isolates the fault.
dialogue.pt: Nossa prioridade imediata é restabelecer o acesso com segurança. Como medida de precaução, suspendemos as alterações de senha enquanto a equipe isola a falha.

dialogue.speaker: Spokesperson
dialogue.en: We will provide the next update by eleven, even if the situation is unchanged, and contact affected customers directly if the circumstances change.
dialogue.pt: Forneceremos a próxima atualização até as onze, mesmo que a situação não tenha mudado, e entraremos em contato diretamente com os clientes afetados se as circunstâncias mudarem.

comprehension.prompt: How does the spokesperson communicate during the incident?
options: By separating verified facts, unknowns, actions, and update times
By claiming the incident is resolved before the review begins
By refusing to provide any information until every fact is known
comprehension.answer: By separating verified facts, unknowns, actions, and update times

comprehension.prompt: What remains unknown?
options: Whether data was exposed and what caused the interruption
Which response team is restoring access
comprehension.answer: Whether data was exposed and what caused the interruption

comprehension.prompt: What precaution has the company taken?
options: It has deleted all customer accounts
It has restored service without testing
comprehension.answer: It has suspended password changes

productionPrompt: Give a short crisis update about a service, safety, or operational incident. Separate confirmed facts from unknowns, state the immediate action and precaution, and commit to the next update.
retryHint: Use We can confirm... only for verified facts, name one unknown with still establishing, decline speculation, and give a specific time for the next update.

title: Lesson 84 — Analysing Policy Choices
topic: Evaluating policy aims, mechanisms, and side effects
objective: Evaluate whether a policy's mechanism can achieve its stated aim, identify likely side effects, and recommend proportionate revisions.
pronunciationFocus: Use contrastive stress to separate intention from mechanism: the AIM is reasonable; the INCENTIVE may work against it.

phrases.en: The policy is intended to reduce short car journeys.
phrases.pt: A política pretende reduzir os trajetos curtos de carro.
phrases.note: Use is intended to for the stated purpose without assuming the policy will achieve it.

phrases.en: The proposed mechanism is a charge during peak hours.
phrases.pt: O mecanismo proposto é uma cobrança nos horários de pico.
phrases.note: Distinguish what a policy seeks to change from the instrument used to change it.

phrases.en: That incentive may alter behaviour at the margin.
phrases.pt: Esse incentivo pode alterar o comportamento de parte das pessoas.
phrases.note: At the margin refers to people whose choices are close enough for the incentive to affect them.

phrases.en: The measure could have an unintended distributional effect.
phrases.pt: A medida pode ter um efeito distributivo não intencional.
phrases.note: A distributional effect concerns how costs or benefits fall on different groups.

phrases.en: Its effectiveness depends on viable alternatives being available.
phrases.pt: Sua eficácia depende da disponibilidade de alternativas viáveis.
phrases.note: Name a condition the policy requires rather than treating success as automatic.

phrases.en: We should distinguish implementation failure from design failure.
phrases.pt: Devemos distinguir falha de implementação de falha de concepção.
phrases.note: A sound instrument can be applied badly, while flawless delivery cannot rescue a flawed mechanism.

phrases.en: A targeted exemption would mitigate the burden without removing the incentive.
phrases.pt: Uma isenção direcionada reduziria o ônus sem eliminar o incentivo.
phrases.note: Use this pattern to propose a safeguard that preserves the policy's central mechanism.

phrases.en: The policy should be reviewed against outcomes, not activity alone.
phrases.pt: A política deve ser avaliada pelos resultados, não apenas pelas atividades realizadas.
phrases.note: Separate evidence that a programme operated from evidence that it produced the intended result.

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.en: The city wants to reduce short car journeys by charging drivers who enter the centre during peak hours.
dialogue.pt: A prefeitura quer reduzir os trajetos curtos de carro cobrando dos motoristas que entram no centro nos horários de pico.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.en: The aim is reasonable, but the mechanism depends on viable alternatives being available.
dialogue.pt: O objetivo é razoável, mas o mecanismo depende da disponibilidade de alternativas viáveis.

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.en: Central neighbourhoods have frequent buses. Shift workers in outer districts often have no practical alternative.
dialogue.pt: Os bairros centrais têm ônibus frequentes. Trabalhadores por turnos nos bairros periféricos muitas vezes não têm alternativa prática.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.en: Then the measure could have an unintended distributional effect while changing behaviour only at the margin.
dialogue.pt: Então, a medida pode ter um efeito distributivo não intencional e mudar o comportamento apenas de parte das pessoas.

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.en: A targeted exemption for workers without public transport would mitigate the burden without removing the incentive for most drivers.
dialogue.pt: Uma isenção direcionada a trabalhadores sem transporte público reduziria o ônus sem eliminar o incentivo para a maioria dos motoristas.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.en: Agreed. We should review journey numbers, bus access, and costs by income group after six months.
dialogue.pt: Concordo. Devemos avaliar o número de trajetos, o acesso aos ônibus e os custos por faixa de renda após seis meses.

comprehension.prompt: How do Ana and Ben revise the transport policy?
options: They add a targeted exemption and an outcomes review
They replace the charge with a ban on all city travel
They apply the charge only where buses are most frequent
comprehension.answer: They add a targeted exemption and an outcomes review

comprehension.prompt: What condition may limit the policy's effectiveness?
options: Some workers lack viable transport alternatives
The city has too many empty buses at peak hours
Drivers do not know where the city centre is
comprehension.answer: Some workers lack viable transport alternatives

comprehension.prompt: Which possible side effect concerns them?
options: The burden may fall unfairly across income groups
Every short car journey may become longer
Public transport may become completely free
comprehension.answer: The burden may fall unfairly across income groups

productionPrompt: Analyse a policy from work, education, transport, health, or public life. Separate its aim from its mechanism, identify a condition and unintended effect, and recommend a safeguard and review measure.
retryHint: Use is intended to for the aim, depends on for a necessary condition, and could have for a side effect; finish with an outcome the policy should be reviewed against.

title: Lesson 85 — Discussing Research Responsibly
topic: Discussing evidence, limitations, and implications
objective: Discuss a research finding by separating results from interpretation, evaluating limitations, and drawing implications that remain within the evidence.
pronunciationFocus: Downstep from finding to qualification: the association was CONSISTENT, although the effect was MODEST.

phrases.en: The study found a consistent association between the two factors.
phrases.pt: O estudo encontrou uma associação consistente entre os dois fatores.
phrases.note: An association describes variables moving together and does not by itself establish causation.

phrases.en: That finding should not be interpreted as proof of causation.
phrases.pt: Esse resultado não deve ser interpretado como prova de causalidade.
phrases.note: Use this qualification when a design cannot rule out alternative causes.

phrases.en: The effect was statistically detectable but modest in practical terms.
phrases.pt: O efeito foi detectável estatisticamente, mas modesto em termos práticos.
phrases.note: A detectable effect can still be too small to matter in a real decision.

phrases.en: The sample may not be representative of the wider population.
phrases.pt: A amostra pode não ser representativa da população em geral.
phrases.note: Question whether the participants reflect the group to which a claim is being applied.

phrases.en: A plausible alternative explanation is self-selection.
phrases.pt: Uma explicação alternativa plausível é a autosseleção.
phrases.note: Offer a specific competing mechanism rather than vaguely saying that more research is needed.

phrases.en: The result is robust across several analytical choices.
phrases.pt: O resultado se mantém sólido em várias escolhas analíticas.
phrases.note: Robust means the conclusion does not disappear under reasonable alternative analyses.

phrases.en: The evidence supports a pilot, not a universal recommendation.
phrases.pt: As evidências sustentam um projeto-piloto, não uma recomendação universal.
phrases.note: Match the strength and scope of a recommendation to the evidence available.

phrases.en: Further work should test whether the result generalises to other settings.
phrases.pt: Trabalhos futuros devem testar se o resultado se aplica a outros contextos.
phrases.note: Specify the uncertainty future research should resolve.

dialogue.speaker: Maya
dialogue.en: The study found that employees who chose two remote days reported higher concentration after three months.
dialogue.pt: O estudo constatou que funcionários que escolheram dois dias de trabalho remoto relataram maior concentração após três meses.

dialogue.speaker: Theo
dialogue.en: The association is consistent, but it should not be interpreted as proof that remote work caused the improvement.
dialogue.pt: A associação é consistente, mas não deve ser interpretada como prova de que o trabalho remoto causou a melhora.

dialogue.speaker: Maya
dialogue.en: A plausible alternative explanation is self-selection: people with suitable roles were more likely to volunteer.
dialogue.pt: Uma explicação alternativa plausível é a autosseleção: pessoas com funções adequadas tinham maior probabilidade de se voluntariar.

dialogue.speaker: Theo
dialogue.en: The sample also came from one technology company, so it may not represent the wider workforce.
dialogue.pt: A amostra também veio de uma única empresa de tecnologia, portanto pode não representar a força de trabalho em geral.

dialogue.speaker: Maya
dialogue.en: Still, the result is robust across several analytical choices and absenteeism did not increase.
dialogue.pt: Ainda assim, o resultado se mantém sólido em várias escolhas analíticas, e o absenteísmo não aumentou.

dialogue.speaker: Theo
dialogue.en: That supports a broader pilot with comparison groups, not a universal recommendation.
dialogue.pt: Isso sustenta um projeto-piloto mais amplo com grupos de comparação, não uma recomendação universal.

comprehension.prompt: What conclusion do Maya and Theo draw from the study?
options: It justifies a broader controlled pilot
It proves every employee should work remotely
It shows remote work reduces concentration
comprehension.answer: It justifies a broader controlled pilot

comprehension.prompt: Why can the study not establish causation?
options: Participants selected themselves into the arrangement
The study measured concentration more than once
Absenteeism remained stable during the study
comprehension.answer: Participants selected themselves into the arrangement

comprehension.prompt: What limits how widely the result can be applied?
options: The sample came from one technology company
The analysis used several reasonable methods
The study continued for three months
comprehension.answer: The sample came from one technology company

productionPrompt: Discuss a research result or evidence-based claim. State the finding, distinguish association from causation, identify a limitation or alternative explanation, and propose a proportionate implication.
retryHint: Report what the study found before interpreting it, qualify the scope with may not be representative, and make the recommendation no stronger than the evidence.

title: Lesson 86 — Mentoring Through Questions
topic: Coaching, reframing, and asking productive questions
objective: Guide a mentoring conversation with diagnostic questions, constructive reframing, and a learner-owned next step instead of prescribing a solution.
pronunciationFocus: Use an open rise for exploratory questions and a calm fall for reframing: what have you TRIED?; this is a PRIORITY problem.

phrases.en: What would a useful outcome look like to you?
phrases.pt: Como seria um resultado útil para você?
phrases.note: Let the other person define success before moving into advice or action.

phrases.en: What have you tried, and what happened?
phrases.pt: O que você já tentou, e o que aconteceu?
phrases.note: Ask about evidence from prior action rather than assuming no effort has been made.

phrases.en: It sounds as though the issue is prioritisation rather than capability.
phrases.pt: Parece que a questão é de priorização, não de capacidade.
phrases.note: Offer a tentative reframe that the learner can confirm, refine, or reject.

phrases.en: Which assumption is narrowing your options?
phrases.pt: Que premissa está limitando suas opções?
phrases.note: This question helps expose a constraint that may be perceived rather than fixed.

phrases.en: What is within your control this week?
phrases.pt: O que está sob seu controle nesta semana?
phrases.note: Shift attention from a broad frustration to an actionable time frame.

phrases.en: Would it help if I shared a similar experience?
phrases.pt: Ajudaria se eu compartilhasse uma experiência parecida?
phrases.note: Request permission so your experience supports rather than displaces the learner's thinking.

phrases.en: Which option are you most willing to test?
phrases.pt: Qual opção você está mais disposto a testar?
phrases.note: Commitment is stronger when the learner chooses the next experiment.

phrases.en: How will you know whether that step helped?
phrases.pt: Como você saberá se essa medida ajudou?
phrases.note: Agree on an observable signal that makes the next reflection more useful.

dialogue.speaker: Mentor
dialogue.en: What would a useful outcome from this conversation look like to you?
dialogue.pt: Como seria um resultado útil desta conversa para você?

dialogue.speaker: Learner
dialogue.en: I want to stop feeling that every request is urgent. I finish the week busy but unsure what I achieved.
dialogue.pt: Quero parar de sentir que toda solicitação é urgente. Termino a semana ocupado, mas sem saber ao certo o que realizei.

dialogue.speaker: Mentor
dialogue.en: What have you tried, and what happened?
dialogue.pt: O que você já tentou, e o que aconteceu?

dialogue.speaker: Learner
dialogue.en: I make a daily list, but I add each new request immediately because I assume a delayed reply will disappoint people.
dialogue.pt: Faço uma lista diária, mas acrescento cada nova solicitação imediatamente porque presumo que uma resposta tardia vai decepcionar as pessoas.

dialogue.speaker: Mentor
dialogue.en: It sounds as though the issue is prioritisation rather than capability. Which assumption is narrowing your options?
dialogue.pt: Parece que a questão é de priorização, não de capacidade. Que premissa está limitando suas opções?

dialogue.speaker: Learner
dialogue.en: I can test acknowledging new requests quickly while agreeing a realistic deadline. I will track whether anyone objects this week.
dialogue.pt: Posso testar reconhecer novas solicitações rapidamente e, ao mesmo tempo, combinar um prazo realista. Vou acompanhar se alguém se opõe nesta semana.

comprehension.prompt: What next step does the learner choose?
options: Acknowledge requests and agree realistic deadlines
Ignore every new request until the month ends
Work on all incoming requests immediately
comprehension.answer: Acknowledge requests and agree realistic deadlines

comprehension.prompt: How does the mentor reframe the problem?
options: As a prioritisation issue rather than a lack of capability
As proof that the learner needs a different profession
As a technical failure in the request system
comprehension.answer: As a prioritisation issue rather than a lack of capability

comprehension.prompt: Which assumption has shaped the learner's behaviour?
options: A delayed reply will disappoint people
Daily lists are forbidden at work
comprehension.answer: A delayed reply will disappoint people

productionPrompt: Coach someone through a challenge without solving it for them. Clarify the desired outcome, ask about prior attempts, offer a tentative reframe, and help them choose a testable next step.
retryHint: Ask two genuine questions before offering a reframe, use It sounds as though... tentatively, and leave the final choice and measure of progress with the learner.

title: Lesson 87 — Setting Strategic Priorities
topic: Distinguishing urgent work from strategically important work
objective: Distinguish urgent activity from strategic importance, make opportunity costs explicit, and define sequencing criteria for competing priorities.
pronunciationFocus: Contrast immediate pressure with long-term value: it is URGENT, but it is not our highest LEVERAGE work.

phrases.en: Urgency and strategic importance are not the same thing.
phrases.pt: Urgência e importância estratégica não são a mesma coisa.
phrases.note: Use this distinction when visible pressure is crowding out higher-value work.

phrases.en: This initiative has the greatest leverage on our long-term goal.
phrases.pt: Esta iniciativa tem o maior impacto sobre nosso objetivo de longo prazo.
phrases.note: Leverage describes an action with disproportionate influence on a larger objective.

phrases.en: Every additional priority dilutes execution.
phrases.pt: Cada prioridade adicional dilui a execução.
phrases.note: A priority list stops guiding choices when everything is included.

phrases.en: The opportunity cost is delaying the retention work.
phrases.pt: O custo de oportunidade é adiar o trabalho de retenção.
phrases.note: Name what cannot receive time or resources if this choice is made.

phrases.en: Which decision would make the other work easier or unnecessary?
phrases.pt: Que decisão tornaria o restante do trabalho mais fácil ou desnecessário?
phrases.note: Look for work that resolves uncertainty or unlocks several later tasks.

phrases.en: We need an explicit criterion for moving this forward.
phrases.pt: Precisamos de um critério explícito para avançar com isso.
phrases.note: A criterion prevents the loudest request from repeatedly resetting priorities.

phrases.en: For now, we are deliberately deprioritising expansion.
phrases.pt: Por enquanto, estamos deliberadamente reduzindo a prioridade da expansão.
phrases.note: State what will receive less attention so the priority decision has operational meaning.

phrases.en: We will revisit the sequence when the evidence changes.
phrases.pt: Reavaliaremos a sequência quando as evidências mudarem.
phrases.note: Tie reconsideration to new evidence rather than to a vague future date.

dialogue.speaker: Director
dialogue.en: We have requests for a new market launch, a visual redesign, and work to reduce customer cancellations. All three teams call their work urgent.
dialogue.pt: Temos solicitações para lançar em um novo mercado, fazer um redesign visual e trabalhar para reduzir cancelamentos de clientes. As três equipes chamam seu trabalho de urgente.

dialogue.speaker: Strategist
dialogue.en: Urgency and strategic importance are not the same thing. Which initiative has the greatest leverage on our annual goal?
dialogue.pt: Urgência e importância estratégica não são a mesma coisa. Que iniciativa tem o maior impacto sobre nossa meta anual?

dialogue.speaker: Director
dialogue.en: Retention. Expansion adds customers, but current cancellations would absorb much of that growth.
dialogue.pt: Retenção. A expansão acrescenta clientes, mas os cancelamentos atuais absorveriam grande parte desse crescimento.

dialogue.speaker: Strategist
dialogue.en: Then the opportunity cost of launching now is delaying the work most likely to improve net growth.
dialogue.pt: Então, o custo de oportunidade de lançar agora é adiar o trabalho com maior probabilidade de melhorar o crescimento líquido.

dialogue.speaker: Director
dialogue.en: We will prioritise retention, allow only essential redesign fixes, and deliberately deprioritise expansion.
dialogue.pt: Vamos priorizar a retenção, permitir apenas correções essenciais no design e reduzir deliberadamente a prioridade da expansão.

dialogue.speaker: Strategist
dialogue.en: Let's revisit the sequence when cancellations fall below four percent for two consecutive months.
dialogue.pt: Vamos reavaliar a sequência quando os cancelamentos ficarem abaixo de quatro por cento por dois meses consecutivos.

comprehension.prompt: Which work do the leaders prioritise?
options: Launching in a new market immediately
comprehension.answer: Reducing customer cancellations

comprehension.prompt: Why do they delay expansion?
options: Current cancellations would absorb much of the growth
No customers are interested in the new market
The redesign has already increased retention
comprehension.answer: Current cancellations would absorb much of the growth

comprehension.prompt: What will trigger reconsideration?
options: Cancellations staying below four percent for two months
Any team describing its work as urgent
The completion of one essential design fix
comprehension.answer: Cancellations staying below four percent for two months

productionPrompt: Prioritise three competing projects or responsibilities. Separate urgency from strategic value, name an opportunity cost, choose what to deprioritise, and define evidence that would change the sequence.
retryHint: Name the long-term goal first, compare leverage rather than enthusiasm, state the opportunity cost explicitly, and attach reprioritisation to a measurable trigger.

title: Lesson 88 — Telling a Nuanced Story
topic: Telling complex stories with shifts in stance and perspective
objective: Tell a complex narrative that distinguishes earlier beliefs from later understanding, integrates multiple perspectives, and avoids a falsely tidy conclusion.
pronunciationFocus: Mark shifts in perspective with a reset in pitch: at the TIME, I saw it differently; in RETROSPECT, the warning was clear.

phrases.en: At the time, I interpreted her silence as agreement.
phrases.pt: Na época, interpretei o silêncio dela como concordância.
phrases.note: At the time separates a past viewpoint from what the narrator understands now.

phrases.en: In retrospect, the warning signs were already there.
phrases.pt: Em retrospecto, os sinais de alerta já estavam presentes.
phrases.note: Use in retrospect for understanding made possible by later events.

phrases.en: From her perspective, the decision had already been made.
phrases.pt: Do ponto de vista dela, a decisão já havia sido tomada.
phrases.note: Attribute a viewpoint explicitly without claiming complete access to another person's motives.

phrases.en: What I took for resistance was partly a request for clarity.
phrases.pt: O que interpretei como resistência era, em parte, um pedido de clareza.
phrases.note: This pattern contrasts an initial label with a more nuanced later account.

phrases.en: Two things can be true at once.
phrases.pt: Duas coisas podem ser verdadeiras ao mesmo tempo.
phrases.note: Use this to resist forcing a complex experience into a single moral or cause.

phrases.en: The turning point came when we compared our assumptions.
phrases.pt: O ponto de virada ocorreu quando comparamos nossas premissas.
phrases.note: A turning point changes the direction or interpretation of the narrative.

phrases.en: That did not resolve the tension, but it changed its meaning.
phrases.pt: Isso não resolveu a tensão, mas mudou o significado dela.
phrases.note: A narrative can end with deeper understanding even when the practical conflict remains.

phrases.en: I am wary of reducing the experience to a simple lesson.
phrases.pt: Tenho receio de reduzir a experiência a uma lição simples.
phrases.note: Signal that a concise moral would erase important contradictions or uncertainty.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.en: When our community project slowed down, I blamed Leila for resisting every decision. At the time, I interpreted her silence in meetings as agreement followed by obstruction.
dialogue.pt: Quando nosso projeto comunitário desacelerou, culpei Leila por resistir a todas as decisões. Na época, interpretei o silêncio dela nas reuniões como concordância seguida de obstrução.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.en: From her perspective, however, the decisions had already been made before volunteers were invited to comment.
dialogue.pt: Do ponto de vista dela, porém, as decisões já haviam sido tomadas antes que os voluntários fossem convidados a opinar.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.en: The turning point came when we wrote down what each of us believed the consultation was for.
dialogue.pt: O ponto de virada ocorreu quando escrevemos o que cada um acreditava ser a finalidade da consulta.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.en: I thought we were improving a fixed plan. She thought the group was still deciding whether the plan should exist.
dialogue.pt: Eu achava que estávamos aprimorando um plano já definido. Ela achava que o grupo ainda estava decidindo se o plano deveria existir.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.en: What I took for resistance was partly a request for clarity. At the same time, some of her objections arrived too late to act on.
dialogue.pt: O que interpretei como resistência era, em parte, um pedido de clareza. Ao mesmo tempo, algumas objeções dela chegaram tarde demais para serem atendidas.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.en: The conversation did not remove the tension, but it changed its meaning. I am wary of reducing it to a simple lesson about communication.
dialogue.pt: A conversa não eliminou a tensão, mas mudou o significado dela. Tenho receio de reduzir tudo a uma lição simples sobre comunicação.

comprehension.prompt: How does the narrator's understanding change?
options: The conflict becomes a mismatch of assumptions as well as timing
Leila admits that she opposed every form of consultation
The narrator learns that no decisions had ever been discussed
comprehension.answer: The conflict becomes a mismatch of assumptions as well as timing

comprehension.prompt: What did Leila believe the group was deciding?
options: Who should write the final meeting notes
When the fixed plan would be announced
comprehension.answer: Whether the plan should exist

comprehension.prompt: Why does the narrator avoid a simple moral?
options: Both unclear consultation and late objections mattered
The project ended before anyone could speak
Every participant now remembers events identically
comprehension.answer: Both unclear consultation and late objections mattered

productionPrompt: Tell a story about a misunderstanding or changed judgement. Contrast what you believed then with what you understand now, include another perspective, and end without erasing unresolved tension.
retryHint: Signal the time shift with At the time and In retrospect, attribute another perspective carefully, and use partly or at the same time to preserve complexity.

title: Lesson 89 — Exposing Hidden Assumptions
topic: Exposing assumptions and expressing epistemic caution
objective: Expose the implicit assumptions carrying an argument, assess how conclusions depend on them, and express epistemic caution without becoming evasive.

phrases.en: The argument rests on an assumption that has not been made explicit.
phrases.pt: O argumento se apoia em uma premissa que não foi explicitada.
phrases.note: Identify the unstated premise needed to connect evidence to a conclusion.

phrases.en: That conclusion follows only if we treat demand as stable.
phrases.pt: Essa conclusão só decorre se tratarmos a demanda como estável.
phrases.note: Only if reveals a condition on which the inference depends.

phrases.en: We may be smuggling a value judgement into a factual claim.
phrases.pt: Talvez estejamos inserindo um juízo de valor em uma afirmação factual sem explicitá-lo.
phrases.note: Use this when descriptive language quietly depends on what counts as good.

phrases.en: The absence of evidence here is not evidence of absence.
phrases.pt: A ausência de evidência aqui não é evidência de ausência.
phrases.note: A failed search supports absence only when the method could detect what it sought.

phrases.en: I would attach more confidence to the direction than the magnitude.
phrases.pt: Eu atribuiria mais confiança à direção do que à magnitude.
phrases.note: Confidence can differ across parts of the same claim.

phrases.en: There is a risk of mistaking what is measurable for what matters.
phrases.pt: Há o risco de confundir o que é mensurável com o que importa.
phrases.note: Available metrics may represent only part of the value under discussion.

phrases.en: My confidence is provisional rather than negligible.
phrases.pt: Minha confiança é provisória, não insignificante.
phrases.note: Provisional preserves a reasoned view while remaining open to revision.

phrases.en: What observation would count against this interpretation?
phrases.pt: Que observação contaria contra esta interpretação?
phrases.note: A claim becomes testable when speakers specify what could weaken it.

dialogue.speaker: Iris
dialogue.en: The report says the training had no value because promotion rates were unchanged after six months.
dialogue.pt: O relatório diz que o treinamento não teve valor porque as taxas de promoção não mudaram após seis meses.

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.en: That rests on two assumptions: promotion is the relevant measure, and six months is enough time for an effect to appear.
dialogue.pt: Isso se apoia em duas premissas: promoção é a medida relevante e seis meses são suficientes para que um efeito apareça.

dialogue.speaker: Iris
dialogue.en: There is a risk of mistaking what is measurable for what matters. The training may affect retention first.
dialogue.pt: Há o risco de confundir o que é mensurável com o que importa. O treinamento pode afetar primeiro a retenção.

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.en: The absence of a promotion effect is not meaningless. It limits one version of the claim.
dialogue.pt: A ausência de efeito nas promoções não é irrelevante. Ela limita uma versão da afirmação.

dialogue.speaker: Iris
dialogue.en: My confidence is provisional. I would want comparison data on retention and role changes.
dialogue.pt: Minha confiança é provisória. Eu gostaria de dados comparativos sobre retenção e mudanças de função.

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.en: If those outcomes are also unchanged later, that would count against the broader interpretation.
dialogue.pt: Se esses resultados também permanecerem inalterados depois, isso contaria contra a interpretação mais ampla.

comprehension.prompt: How do Iris and Sam evaluate the report's conclusion?
options: They expose its assumptions while preserving relevant negative evidence
They accept promotion as the only possible measure of value
They dismiss every measured outcome as unimportant
comprehension.answer: They expose its assumptions while preserving relevant negative evidence

comprehension.prompt: Which assumptions underlie the report?
options: Promotion is the right measure and six months is sufficient
Retention always changes before training begins
Internal mobility can never be measured
comprehension.answer: Promotion is the right measure and six months is sufficient

comprehension.prompt: What evidence could weaken the broader interpretation?
options: No later change in retention or role mobility
A different title for the training programme
More promotion data from the same six months
comprehension.answer: No later change in retention or role mobility

productionPrompt: Examine an argument whose conclusion seems stronger than its evidence. Identify an implicit assumption, show how the conclusion depends on it, calibrate your confidence, and name disconfirming evidence.
retryHint: State the hidden premise as an assumption, use only if to expose the dependency, then say what evidence would change your view.

title: Lesson 90 — Reasoning with Analogy and Metaphor
topic: Using and critiquing analogy, metaphor, and framing
objective: Use analogy and metaphor to illuminate structure while testing where the comparison breaks down and how its framing guides judgement.

phrases.en: The analogy is useful because both systems distribute limited capacity.
phrases.pt: A analogia é útil porque os dois sistemas distribuem capacidade limitada.
phrases.note: Specify the shared structure that makes a comparison informative.

phrases.en: The comparison breaks down when incentives enter the picture.
phrases.pt: A comparação deixa de funcionar quando os incentivos entram em cena.
phrases.note: A strong analogy can mislead beyond the feature being compared.

phrases.en: The metaphor foregrounds fragility and obscures adaptability.
phrases.pt: A metáfora põe a fragilidade em primeiro plano e oculta a adaptabilidade.
phrases.note: Framing makes some aspects prominent while pushing others aside.

phrases.en: We should not let a vivid image substitute for a causal account.
phrases.pt: Não devemos deixar que uma imagem vívida substitua uma explicação causal.
phrases.note: Memorable language can feel explanatory without identifying a mechanism.

phrases.en: A closer parallel would be a portfolio rather than a pipeline.
phrases.pt: Um paralelo mais próximo seria um portfólio, não um fluxo linear.
phrases.note: Offer a comparison whose structure better fits the relevant features.

phrases.en: Taken literally, the framing implies a single point of control.
phrases.pt: Tomado ao pé da letra, o enquadramento implica um único ponto de controle.
phrases.note: Examine what the image suggests about agency or sequence.

phrases.en: The image clarifies the trade-off without resolving it.
phrases.pt: A imagem esclarece a escolha envolvida sem resolvê-la.
phrases.note: A comparison can organise a dilemma while leaving the decision open.

phrases.en: What does this framing invite us to notice or ignore?
phrases.pt: O que este enquadramento nos convida a perceber ou ignorar?
phrases.note: Ask how wording directs attention before accepting its judgement.

dialogue.speaker: Nadia
dialogue.en: The chief executive calls our innovation process a pipeline: weak ideas are filtered out and strong ones reach the market.
dialogue.pt: A diretora executiva chama nosso processo de inovação de fluxo linear: as ideias fracas são filtradas e as fortes chegam ao mercado.

dialogue.speaker: Owen
dialogue.en: The image clarifies selection, but taken literally it implies a fixed sequence and a single point of control.
dialogue.pt: A imagem esclarece a seleção, mas, tomada ao pé da letra, implica uma sequência fixa e um único ponto de controle.

dialogue.speaker: Nadia
dialogue.en: It foregrounds throughput and obscures the way ideas combine, pause, or return in a different form.
dialogue.pt: Ela põe o fluxo em primeiro plano e oculta como as ideias se combinam, fazem pausas ou retornam de outra forma.

dialogue.speaker: Owen
dialogue.en: A closer parallel might be a portfolio. We make uncertain investments and rebalance as evidence changes.
dialogue.pt: Um paralelo mais próximo talvez seja um portfólio. Fazemos investimentos incertos e os reequilibramos conforme as evidências mudam.

dialogue.speaker: Nadia
dialogue.en: That captures uncertainty, though it risks treating creative teams as financial assets.
dialogue.pt: Isso capta a incerteza, embora corra o risco de tratar equipes criativas como ativos financeiros.

dialogue.speaker: Owen
dialogue.en: Then use the analogy for resource allocation without letting it substitute for an account of creativity.
dialogue.pt: Então, use a analogia para a alocação de recursos sem deixá-la substituir uma explicação da criatividade.

comprehension.prompt: Why do Nadia and Owen prefer the portfolio analogy?
options: It better represents uncertainty and reallocation
It proves creative work behaves exactly like finance
It removes the need to explain innovation
comprehension.answer: It better represents uncertainty and reallocation

comprehension.prompt: What does the pipeline metaphor obscure?
options: Ideas can combine, pause, and return
Some ideas eventually reach a market
Selection requires any criteria at all
comprehension.answer: Ideas can combine, pause, and return

comprehension.prompt: What limitation does Nadia see in the portfolio analogy?
options: It may frame creative teams as financial assets
It contains no concept of uncertainty
It assumes resources can be allocated
comprehension.answer: It may frame creative teams as financial assets

productionPrompt: Use an analogy or metaphor to explain a complex process or choice. State the relevant similarity, identify where the comparison breaks down, and explain what the framing highlights and obscures.
retryHint: Name the shared structure with useful because, mark the boundary with breaks down when, and do not let a vivid comparison replace the mechanism.

title: Lesson 91 — Negotiating Under Pressure
topic: Strategic ambiguity and precise concessions
objective: Negotiate under high stakes by separating interests from positions, managing strategic ambiguity, and making conditional concessions without accidental commitment.

phrases.en: Our stated positions differ, but our underlying interests may overlap.
phrases.pt: Nossas posições declaradas diferem, mas nossos interesses subjacentes podem coincidir.
phrases.note: Positions state requests; interests explain what each request protects.

phrases.en: I do not want our silence to be construed as acceptance.
phrases.pt: Não quero que nosso silêncio seja interpretado como aceitação.
phrases.note: Clarify procedural meaning when ambiguity could create a commitment.

phrases.en: We could contemplate exclusivity, subject to a shorter term.
phrases.pt: Poderíamos considerar a exclusividade, desde que o prazo fosse menor.
phrases.note: A conditional opening is not the same as conceding outright.

phrases.en: That concession would need to be reciprocated on price.
phrases.pt: Essa concessão precisaria ser correspondida no preço.
phrases.note: Link movement on one term to movement on another.

phrases.en: For the avoidance of doubt, this is not yet an offer.
phrases.pt: Para evitar dúvidas, isto ainda não é uma oferta.
phrases.note: State whether language is exploratory or binding.

phrases.en: There may be scope to bridge the gap through sequencing.
phrases.pt: Talvez haja espaço para reduzir a divergência por meio do sequenciamento.
phrases.note: Different timing can reconcile apparently incompatible interests.

phrases.en: We cannot move on liability beyond that threshold.
phrases.pt: Não podemos ceder na responsabilidade além desse limite.
phrases.note: A specific limit is more usable than saying a term is difficult.

phrases.en: Any agreement remains contingent on board approval.
phrases.pt: Qualquer acordo continua condicionado à aprovação do conselho.
phrases.note: Disclose authority limits that affect whether agreement can bind.

dialogue.speaker: Buyer
dialogue.en: We need five years of exclusivity and a liability cap of twice the contract value.
dialogue.pt: Precisamos de cinco anos de exclusividade e de um limite de responsabilidade equivalente ao dobro do valor do contrato.

dialogue.speaker: Supplier
dialogue.en: We cannot move on liability beyond the contract value. We could contemplate exclusivity, subject to a shorter term.
dialogue.pt: Não podemos ceder na responsabilidade além do valor do contrato. Poderíamos considerar a exclusividade, desde que o prazo fosse menor.

dialogue.speaker: Buyer
dialogue.en: Our interest is supply security. Three years might work if the price falls after year one.
dialogue.pt: Nosso interesse é a segurança do fornecimento. Três anos talvez funcionem se o preço cair após o primeiro ano.

dialogue.speaker: Supplier
dialogue.en: We could bridge the gap through sequencing. A price reduction could follow a guaranteed volume threshold.
dialogue.pt: Poderíamos reduzir a divergência por meio do sequenciamento. Uma redução de preço poderia ocorrer após um volume mínimo garantido.

dialogue.speaker: Buyer
dialogue.en: I can take that structure back, but my silence on liability must not be construed as acceptance.
dialogue.pt: Posso levar essa estrutura para análise, mas meu silêncio sobre responsabilidade não deve ser interpretado como aceitação.

dialogue.speaker: Supplier
dialogue.en: Understood. This is not yet an offer, and any agreement remains contingent on both boards' approval.
dialogue.pt: Entendido. Isto ainda não é uma oferta, e qualquer acordo continua condicionado à aprovação dos dois conselhos.

comprehension.prompt: Where do the negotiators find possible movement?
options: A shorter exclusivity term linked to volume and later pricing
Unlimited liability in exchange for permanent exclusivity
comprehension.answer: A shorter exclusivity term linked to volume and later pricing

comprehension.prompt: Which boundary does the supplier maintain?
options: Liability cannot exceed the contract value
Exclusivity must last at least five years
Prices can never change after signing
comprehension.answer: Liability cannot exceed the contract value

comprehension.prompt: Why does the buyer clarify the meaning of silence?
options: To prevent it being treated as acceptance on liability
To accept every term without further discussion
To withdraw the request for supply security
comprehension.answer: To prevent it being treated as acceptance on liability

productionPrompt: Negotiate two linked high-stakes terms. Identify the interests beneath each position, state one firm boundary, exchange conditional concessions, and clarify what remains exploratory or subject to approval.
retryHint: Use subject to and contingent on for genuine conditions, pair every concession with reciprocity, and state when language is not yet an offer.

title: Lesson 92 — Crafting an Editorial Argument
topic: Building a concise, rhetorically controlled editorial argument
objective: Build a concise editorial argument with a defensible thesis, a fair concession, controlled rhetoric, and a proportionate call to action.

phrases.en: The case for action is narrower, and stronger, than advocates suggest.
phrases.pt: A justificativa para agir é mais restrita — e mais forte — do que sugerem seus defensores.
phrases.note: A limited claim can be more defensible than an expansive one.

phrases.en: The strongest objection deserves to be stated without caricature.
phrases.pt: A objeção mais forte merece ser apresentada sem caricatura.
phrases.note: Address the best opposing case rather than a distorted substitute.

phrases.en: That concern is legitimate, but it is not dispositive.
phrases.pt: Essa preocupação é legítima, mas não é decisiva por si só.
phrases.note: Recognise force while showing the concern does not settle the question.

phrases.en: The relevant comparison is not perfection, but the status quo.
phrases.pt: A comparação relevante não é com a perfeição, mas com a situação atual.
phrases.note: Compare proposals with realistic alternatives rather than an ideal state.

phrases.en: Rhetorical urgency should not outrun the evidence.
phrases.pt: A urgência retórica não deve ultrapassar as evidências.
phrases.note: Tone should remain proportionate to what the facts support.

phrases.en: The cost of delay is diffuse, which makes it easy to ignore.
phrases.pt: O custo do atraso é difuso, o que facilita ignorá-lo.
phrases.note: Distributed harm often receives less attention than immediate costs.

phrases.en: A reversible first step is preferable to indefinite caution.
phrases.pt: Um primeiro passo reversível é preferível à cautela por tempo indeterminado.
phrases.note: Reversibility can justify learning through action under uncertainty.

phrases.en: The burden now lies with those defending inaction.
phrases.pt: O ônus agora recai sobre quem defende a inação.
phrases.note: Use after showing that the status quo also imposes material costs.

dialogue.speaker: Editor
dialogue.en: Your draft says the city must pedestrianise every central street immediately.
dialogue.pt: Seu rascunho diz que a cidade deve tornar todas as ruas centrais exclusivas para pedestres imediatamente.

dialogue.speaker: Writer
dialogue.en: That is forceful, but the evidence only covers three streets and a six-month trial.
dialogue.pt: Isso tem força, mas as evidências abrangem apenas três ruas e um teste de seis meses.

dialogue.speaker: Editor
dialogue.en: Then make the case narrower: extend the trial because footfall rose and emergency access was maintained.
dialogue.pt: Então, restrinja a justificativa: amplie o teste porque o fluxo de pedestres aumentou e o acesso de emergência foi mantido.

dialogue.speaker: Writer
dialogue.en: The strongest objection is displaced traffic. That concern is legitimate, but not dispositive.
dialogue.pt: A objeção mais forte é o tráfego deslocado. Essa preocupação é legítima, mas não é decisiva por si só.

dialogue.speaker: Editor
dialogue.en: Compare a monitored extension with the status quo, not with a perfect transport system.
dialogue.pt: Compare uma ampliação monitorada com a situação atual, não com um sistema de transporte perfeito.

dialogue.speaker: Writer
dialogue.en: I will argue that a reversible extension is preferable to indefinite caution.
dialogue.pt: Vou argumentar que uma ampliação reversível é preferível à cautela por tempo indeterminado.

comprehension.prompt: How does the writer revise the editorial?
options: It argues for a monitored extension of a limited trial
It demands an immediate permanent ban across the city
It withdraws any claim that the trial produced evidence
comprehension.answer: It argues for a monitored extension of a limited trial

comprehension.prompt: What is the strongest objection?
options: Traffic may be displaced to neighbouring streets
Emergency vehicles were unable to enter the area
comprehension.answer: Traffic may be displaced to neighbouring streets

comprehension.prompt: Which baseline does the editor recommend?
options: A city with no central streets
comprehension.answer: The current situation

productionPrompt: Make a concise editorial case for a specific action. Narrow the thesis to the evidence, state the strongest objection fairly, compare action with the status quo, and end with a proportionate call to action.
retryHint: Replace sweeping language with the strongest defensible claim, concede one legitimate concern, and match rhetorical urgency to the evidence.

title: Lesson 93 — Synthesising a Complex Debate
topic: Synthesizing dense debate without flattening disagreement
objective: Synthesise a dense debate by mapping distinct claims, evidence, values, and uncertainties while preserving disagreements that cannot honestly be reconciled.

phrases.en: The debate contains three distinct questions that are often conflated.
phrases.pt: O debate contém três questões distintas que muitas vezes são confundidas.
phrases.note: Separate empirical, causal, and normative questions.

phrases.en: The participants agree on the diagnosis but diverge on the remedy.
phrases.pt: Os participantes concordam com o diagnóstico, mas divergem quanto à solução.
phrases.note: Locate precisely where consensus ends.

phrases.en: One claim is empirical; the other turns on a judgement about fairness.
phrases.pt: Uma afirmação é empírica; a outra depende de um julgamento sobre justiça.
phrases.note: Different questions require different evidence.

phrases.en: The apparent contradiction dissolves once the time horizons are separated.
phrases.pt: A aparente contradição desaparece quando se separam os horizontes temporais.
phrases.note: Claims can both hold when they concern different periods.

phrases.en: This point remains genuinely contested rather than merely unclear.
phrases.pt: Este ponto continua genuinamente controverso, não apenas pouco claro.
phrases.note: Explanation cannot eliminate a conflict rooted in different values.

phrases.en: The weight of evidence favours a limited claim on short-term effects.
phrases.pt: O conjunto das evidências favorece uma afirmação limitada sobre os efeitos de curto prazo.
phrases.note: Summarise where evidence leans while preserving boundaries.

phrases.en: No synthesis should manufacture consensus where none exists.
phrases.pt: Nenhuma síntese deve fabricar consenso onde ele não existe.
phrases.note: A faithful synthesis can clarify disagreement without resolving it.

phrases.en: The unresolved issue is who should bear the residual risk.
phrases.pt: A questão não resolvida é quem deve arcar com o risco residual.
phrases.note: Name the decision or value conflict that remains.

dialogue.speaker: Chair
dialogue.en: Can you synthesise the debate over using automated screening for public benefits?
dialogue.pt: Você pode sintetizar o debate sobre o uso de triagem automatizada para benefícios públicos?

dialogue.speaker: Analyst
dialogue.en: It contains three questions: whether screening finds errors, whether it changes access, and which mistakes are tolerable.
dialogue.pt: Ele contém três questões: se a triagem encontra erros, se muda o acesso e quais erros são toleráveis.

dialogue.speaker: Chair
dialogue.en: Where does the evidence point?
dialogue.pt: Para onde apontam as evidências?

dialogue.speaker: Analyst
dialogue.en: Evidence supports a limited claim that screening detects more inconsistent applications. Long-term deterrence is uncertain.
dialogue.pt: As evidências sustentam a afirmação limitada de que a triagem detecta mais solicitações inconsistentes. O desestímulo de longo prazo é incerto.

dialogue.speaker: Chair
dialogue.en: And where do the participants still disagree?
dialogue.pt: E em que os participantes ainda discordam?

dialogue.speaker: Analyst
dialogue.en: They agree that errors matter but diverge on whether applicants or the state should bear the residual risk.
dialogue.pt: Eles concordam que os erros importam, mas divergem sobre se os solicitantes ou o Estado devem arcar com o risco residual.

comprehension.prompt: What does the analyst's synthesis preserve?
options: Limited empirical agreement and a genuine ethical disagreement
Complete consensus that automated screening is harmless
A single question with one uncontested answer
comprehension.answer: Limited empirical agreement and a genuine ethical disagreement

comprehension.prompt: Which short-term claim has the strongest evidence?
options: Screening has no effect on administrative work
comprehension.answer: Screening detects more inconsistent applications

comprehension.prompt: What issue remains unresolved?
options: Who should bear the risk of a wrong decision
Whether errors can occur in public administration
comprehension.answer: Who should bear the risk of a wrong decision

productionPrompt: Synthesise a complex debate with several speakers or sources. Separate the questions and claim types, state where evidence and values align or diverge, and preserve one genuinely unresolved issue.
retryHint: Do not average positions into vague middle ground; classify each claim, distinguish apparent from genuine contradiction, and state the residual disagreement precisely.
